title: wm-state: rebuild failure rollback
description: P0. Mid-rebuild error strands windows in wm_state_park_<ws>. No cleanup. Wrap rebuild in try; on failure, capture per-wid original workspace BEFORE park and move them back. Verify: force error mid-rebuild, assert all windows return to origin.
close_reason: superseded by wm-state respawn refactor

title: Callers of clip-paste.sh must pin CLIP_PASTE_DISPLAY
design:
clip-paste.sh (dotfiles-92w.3) refuses to guess when more than one X display has a focused window, which is the permanent steady state on the native host (native i3 :0 + xrdp i3 :10, adr0004; sp014's plan runs copyq on :0 with clip-feed.sh bridging :10 -> :0).

So every caller must pass the session explicitly:

  CLIP_PASTE_DISPLAY="$DISPLAY" clip-paste.sh <row>

Task .4 (quickshell picker): the picker is itself an X client on the session the user is looking at, so its own DISPLAY is fresh and authoritative - forward it.
Task .5 (i3 wiring): the i3 binding likewise runs under the session's own DISPLAY.

Without this the script exits 1 with 'ambiguous session' and nothing pastes on the native host. Single-display environments (WSL/sway alone) work unpinned.

Discovered while fixing the reviewer's GAP 1 on dotfiles-92w.3.
notes: OBSOLETE as of dotfiles-92w.3 attempt 2: clip-set.sh no longer has a CLIP_PASTE_DISPLAY pin to propagate. The reduced script writes the entry to EVERY live display, so there is nothing for a caller to pin and no ambiguity to resolve. Recommend closing as won't-fix rather than implementing.
close_reason: Moot after the sp014 T3 scope reduction (user decision 2026-07-20). clip-paste.sh no longer exists; clip-set.sh writes to EVERY live display rather than refusing to guess between them, so there is no CLIP_PASTE_DISPLAY to pin and no ambiguous-session exit. Superseded by the clip-set.sh 0/1/2 exit contract landed in 102e45a.

title: clip-feed: TOCTOU window between TARGETS gate and payload read can launder a secret
description:
Follow-up from the dotfiles-92w.2 audit (parent epic dotfiles-92w, sp014).

i3/scripts/clip-feed.sh reads TARGETS (line 126) and only then reads the payload
(line 139). Measured gap between the two X SelectionRequests: 10.3 / 13.3 / 11.8 ms
(audit probe, timestamped selection owner). Poll cycle is ~520ms.

If a password-manager copy takes CLIPBOARD ownership INSIDE that gap, the gate has
already passed on the previous (non-secret) owner and read_src fetches the secret
from the NEW owner, feeding it into copyq history. The next poll correctly sees the
hint and skips -- but the secret is already in history. Exposure is roughly
10ms/520ms ~= 2% per password copy on :10.

This is inherent to X: TARGETS and the payload are separate protocol requests and
cannot be fetched atomically. It cannot be eliminated, only narrowed.

Proposed mitigation (cheap, ~3 lines): re-check TARGETS AFTER read_src and BEFORE
feed_dst, and drop the item if the hint is now present. Does not stop the payload
reaching a tmpfile, but does stop it reaching history -- which is the actual harm.

Not a defect in the shipped gate: the gate is correct as designed and was verified
load-bearing during audit (stripping it causes the payload to be fetched; with it
present, 0 payload requests over 16 polls). This is a residual race the task spec
did not cover.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED: post-read TARGETS re-check closes the gate/payload TOCTOU laundering window.

Mitigation (clip-feed.sh:164-173, inside the post-dedup branch):
    if ! targets || grep -qFx 'application/x-kde-passwordManagerHint' "$TGT"; then
      nap "$POLL"; continue
    fi
Reuses the existing timeout-wrapped targets() helper, so no new xclip call and
no new bare-DISPLAY call. Fails CLOSED (a re-check that times out or finds no
owner also drops). $LAST is deliberately NOT updated, so a legitimate copy
dropped because an owner hung is simply re-fed on the next poll -- self-healing.
Costs one extra TARGETS round-trip per DISTINCT new item, not per poll.

Evidence:
- Race exercised for real, not asserted structurally: new race-owner.py fixture
  owns CLIPBOARD with a benign hint-free window, then hands the selection to a
  hint-bearing window from INSIDE the handler for the feeder's own TARGETS
  request (after the SelectionNotify is flushed). The feeder's next request --
  the payload read -- is answered by the password-manager owner. Gate passed on
  win1, payload came from win2. Deterministic, fired in 3/3 runs.
- Each race scenario asserts the handoff actually fired ('handed' in owner.out)
  BEFORE asserting no leak, so a scenario that silently degraded into an
  ordinary benign copy cannot produce a vacuous green.
- RED (pre-fix, committed at fe116ef): 'toctou-race' FAILED -- history 4->5,
  cq read 0 == RACE-SECRET-marker, 'raced secret appears in no history row'
  reported 'row 0'. The reported vulnerability reproduced end-to-end.
- GREEN (post-fix): 35 passed / 0 failed, three consecutive runs.
- MUTATION CONTROL 'CONTROL recheck-is-load-bearing': strips ONLY the re-check
  (first gate left intact), reruns the same race -> 'raced secret IS fed when
  the re-check is absent', size before+1, cq read 0 == RACE-SECRET-CONTROL.
  Both halves present.
- awk strip ranges verified by diff: each removes exactly its own block and
  nothing else; all three variants pass sh -n.

Interaction found and handled (would have silently degraded an existing test):
the pre-existing 'CONTROL hint-check-is-load-bearing' stripped only the first
gate -- with the re-check added, the re-check would have caught that copy and
the control would have started asserting nothing while still printing PASS.
It now strips BOTH checks (they are defence in depth against the same hint) and
says so in a comment. To stop the first gate losing coverage, clip-owner.py now
logs each payload SelectionRequest and 'secret-not-laundered' asserts the first
gate's STRONGER unique property: the payload is never even REQUESTED, so it
never touches a tmpfile -- something the post-read re-check cannot provide.

Residual exposure (documented in the clip-feed.sh header, not hidden):
1. The secret payload DOES reach $NEW (mktemp, 0600, /tmp) before being
   dropped. On disk for one poll. Unavoidable without atomic TARGETS+payload
   fetch, which X does not provide. Accepted per the issue.
2. Narrowed, not eliminated: a THIRD owner taking CLIPBOARD between the payload
   read and the re-check would present hint-free targets and the secret would be
   fed. Requires two ownership flips inside ~10ms; the single-flip case the
   feeder is actually exposed to is covered.
3. Only hint-publishing password managers are recognised at all. One that
   advertises no hint is indistinguishable from a normal copy at BOTH checks.

Tests: i3/scripts/test-clip-feed.sh, 27 -> 35 assertions, all green.
Commits on bd-dotfiles-l6s.0: fe116ef (RED test), 8c82b7e (fix + controls).
Deviations: none from the proposed mitigation. Env: xorg-server-xvfb is not
installed (dotfiles-saa); extracted the pacman package to scratch and ran with
XVFB= override, which the suite already honours.
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED (reviewer reproduced RED, GREEN, and both mutation controls independently)

CONTRACT: re-check TARGETS after read_src, before feed_dst; drop if hint present.
Implemented at clip-feed.sh:171-173, inside the post-dedup branch, reusing the
existing timeout-wrapped targets() helper. No deviation from the proposed fix.

THE RACE IS GENUINELY EXERCISED (the crux -- verified, not accepted):
The strongest evidence is the mutation control's own output, which needs no
trust in the fixture's self-reporting. 'CONTROL recheck-is-load-bearing' strips
ONLY the re-check, leaving the FIRST gate intact, and the run feeds
RACE-SECRET-CONTROL (win2's hint-bearing payload) into history at row 0.
With the first gate intact, that outcome is only reachable if the gate passed
on win1 (hint-free) while the payload was answered by win2 (hint-bearing).
That IS the TOCTOU race, demonstrated by outcome rather than asserted.
Reviewer confirmed the strip guards: the awk range removes exactly the re-check
(FATAL if 'SECURITY GATE' is lost too), and all variants pass sh -n.

RED reproduced by reviewer: new test file + main's pre-fix clip-feed.sh ->
exit 1, 30 passed / 5 failed. 'toctou-race': history 4 -> 5, 'newest item'
actual RACE-SECRET-marker, 'raced secret appears in no history row' -> row 0,
plus 2 cascade failures in later scenarios seeing the leaked row. The reported
vulnerability reproduces end-to-end against the shipped feeder.

GREEN reproduced by reviewer: 35 passed / 0 failed, exit 0, three consecutive
runs (:88/:87). 'the handoff fired' PASSed in both race scenarios in all three
runs -- deterministic, no flake.

FIRST GATE HAS NOT LOST COVERAGE (the implementer's self-reported near-miss,
verified repaired). Adding the re-check would have hollowed out the existing
'CONTROL hint-check-is-load-bearing' -- it stripped only the first gate, so the
re-check would have caught the copy and the control would have printed PASS
while asserting nothing. Repair verified two ways:
 a) the control now strips BOTH blocks (test-clip-feed.sh:514-521) with a FATAL
    guard that the hint string is gone; it passes only by actually feeding.
 b) 'secret-not-laundered' now asserts the first gate's STRONGER unique
    property -- the payload is never even REQUESTED. Reviewer proved this
    assertion is NON-vacuous by mutation: a mutant with the first gate stripped
    and the re-check intact flips it to FAIL / actual 'requested', while
    'secret text appears in no history row' still PASSes. The two gates are
    therefore demonstrably distinct in strength, not redundant.

FAIL-CLOSED + NO SILENT DATA LOSS (verified by reading clip-feed.sh:171-174):
'! targets ||' means a re-check that times out (timeout -> nonzero) or finds no
owner (xclip -> nonzero) also drops. $LAST is updated only at line 174,
'feed_dst && cp "$NEW" "$LAST"', reachable only after the re-check passes --
so a legitimate copy dropped by a transient hang is re-fed next poll rather
than lost. The 35-test green run (which includes every ordinary capture,
dedup, src-down/return and cold-start scenario) confirms the re-check does not
eat legitimate clipboard content.

SP014 HARD REJECTS HOLD: '#!/bin/sh' (line 1) + 'set -u' (line 73); both xclip
call sites (109, 134) are timeout-wrapped with an explicit DISPLAY=; no bare
'xclip -o'; the re-check reuses targets() so it adds no new call site and no
bare-DISPLAY path. Suite still launches the feeder with DISPLAY=:77 (bogus).

RESIDUAL EXPOSURES -- accurately characterized in the header (49-61), neither
overstated nor hidden:
 1. payload reaches $NEW before the drop -- CONFIRMED empirically by the
    mutation above ('requested'); unavoidable without atomic TARGETS+payload
    fetch, which X does not provide. Explicitly accepted by the issue.
 2. two-flip race still leaks -- accurate reading of the code; needs two
    ownership changes inside ~10ms.
 3. non-hint-publishing managers unprotected -- accurate; both checks grep the
    same hint string.
None is cheaply fixable inside this contract. Filed dotfiles-d6k (P3) for the
one additive hardening found: truncate $NEW on the drop path to cut the
secret's on-disk lifetime from one poll to microseconds.

AUTOMATED CHECKS: no TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK/STUB in either file; sh -n and bash -n
clean; no dead code; no fallback/compat shims.

SCOPE: exactly i3/scripts/clip-feed.sh + i3/scripts/test-clip-feed.sh.
Zero quickshell paths touched (sibling agent on task .4 unaffected).
Branch merges cleanly onto main (102e45a).

Tests: 35 passed / 0 failed (27 -> 35, 8 new). Deviations: none.

title: i3 integration + keybind (ft003)
design:
#### type
task
#### effort
4h
#### files_touched
i3/config, i3/config.d/native.conf, i3/config.d/wsl.conf, copyq/dot.yaml
#### scope_change
SCOPE NOTE 2026-07-20: the keybind opens a clipboard PICKER (copy-to-clipboard), not a paste tool. Naming/comments in the i3 configs must not promise paste-under-cursor.
#### depends_note
dotfiles-l6s (TOCTOU hardening of clip-feed.sh) should land BEFORE or WITH this task - .5 autostarts clip-feed.sh, and autostarting the unhardened version ships the ~2pct-per-copy secret exposure window.
#### success_criteria
base i3/config binds key -> qs-clip.sh (all platforms per ft003); native.conf autostarts copyq :0 + clip-feed.sh; wsl.conf autostarts copyq :10 no feeder; after rotz link+reload pgrep copyq one server on primary display; keybind opens picker; autostart idempotent.
#### edge_cases
copyq already running (single-instance on exec_always reload); primary display absent; base var invisible across include boundary (keybind base, autostart overlay).
#### test_plan
i3/scripts/test-clip-integration.sh: grep base for bound key->qs-clip.sh; grep native.conf copyq+clip-feed; wsl.conf copyq-only; assert single-instance guards; simulated reload -> one copyq per display via pgrep.
notes:
ITERATION 2 — reviewer's blocking gap FIXED (commit 3371063 on bd-dotfiles-92w.5.0).

The finding was correct and the suite's own safety claim was false. Fix:
- count_feeders/stop_feeders now scope to $FEED_PATH = $TMP/xhome/.i3/scripts/clip-feed.sh (absolute, unique per run via $$). pgrep/pkill -f -- "$FEED_PATH". A feeder at any other path is never counted and never signalled.
- Basename pattern retained ONLY in foreign_feeders(), a preflight tripwire that aborts and names the offender, never kills. Documented consequence: on a deployed machine you must stop your own feeder to run the suite (refusal is recoverable, silent kill is not).
- Header rewritten: the false 'live session is never touched' claim replaced with the full THE FEEDER PATTERN rationale, including the credit to task .2's test-clip-feed.sh:466 precedent.

Decoy proof (requested item 4) — new [decoy-safety] section, 3 assertions:
- a decoy feeder at $TMP/prodsim/.i3/scripts/clip-feed.sh (basename identical, path unrelated) is started before the first scenario and asserted STILL ALIVE at the end. Real output: 'PASS decoy (pid 1485872, /tmp/clip-integration-test.1485857/prodsim/.i3/scripts/clip-feed.sh) still alive'.
- asserted never counted: count_feeders with only the decoy running == 0.
- tripwire asserted non-vacuous: re-run with the decoy's exemption removed, it must report the decoy pid (== 1).

Suite: 53 passed / 0 failed, exit 0 (was 50).

REGRESSION PROBE (proof the decoy assertion is not vacuous): reverted ONLY the two helpers back to the basename pattern, with anchor proof (both edited lines shown, cmp confirms non-no-op). Result: 51 passed / 2 FAILED — 'decoy still alive: expected running, actual killed by the suite', and the tripwire detection fails too.

IMPORTANT correction discovered by that probe: the 'feeders == 1' counts stayed GREEN under the regression, because stop_feeders kills the decoy BEFORE the first count runs, leaving exactly one to count. My first draft of the section comment claimed those upstream counts would also catch a regression — that was false and is now corrected in the file. The section comment states plainly that these three assertions are the ONLY guard, and that green counts + a dead feeder is the original bug's exact signature.

TRIPWIRE behaviour verified end-to-end: with a production-like feeder running at <scratch>/prodfeeder/.i3/scripts/clip-feed.sh, the suite printed 'FATAL: a clip-feed.sh is running outside this test run', named pid 1488337, exited 1 — and the feeder SURVIVED (verified with kill -0).

Live session untouched throughout: no feeder, no copyq, no stray Xvfb left behind; live quickshell still at 4 procs; i3 still only ever run as 'i3 -C'.
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED (re-audit, iteration 2 / commit 3371063)

BLOCKING GAP FROM ITERATION 1 — CLOSED. Verified by independent reproduction, not by reading the report.

Scoping fix verified at every call site (not just the helper defs). Full inventory of
process signalling in test-clip-integration.sh:
- :174 count_feeders / :178 stop_feeders -> pgrep/pkill -f -- "$FEED_PATH" (absolute,
  $TMP carries $$, unique per run). Basename pattern gone from both.
- :186 foreign_feeders -> basename pgrep, but READ-ONLY: prints pid+cmdline, never signals.
- :160 count_copyq_servers -> pgrep -x copyq is COUNT-only, filtered by DISPLAY=$DPY (:95).
  No pkill on copyq anywhere.
- :112 decoy / :114 Xvfb killed BY PID; :552 kill -0 is a liveness probe; :113 'cq exit'
  runs under DISPLAY=:95 + isolated XDG_CONFIG_HOME so it can only reach its own server.
No unscoped signalling remains.

Decoy assertion reproduced BOTH halves by the reviewer:
- Full suite, unmodified: 53 passed / 0 failed, EXIT=0. Decoy at
  /tmp/clip-integration-test.1490299/prodsim/.i3/scripts/clip-feed.sh survived.
- Regression probe: reverted ONLY the two helpers to the basename pattern (diff of :173-180
  shown as proof the edit landed) -> 51 passed / 2 FAILED, EXIT=1:
    FAIL decoy still alive — expected: running, actual: killed by the suite
    FAIL tripwire reports the decoy pid — expected: 1, actual: 0
  The guard fires. It is not vacuous.

IMPLEMENTER'S SELF-CORRECTION CONFIRMED ACCURATE. Under the regression, all four upstream
feeder-count assertions stayed GREEN ('feeders', 'feeders after reload 1', 'after reload 2',
'still running with :94 down') because stop_feeders kills the decoy before the first count.
The decoy-survival assertion at :551 is therefore the ONLY guard on the scoping, exactly as
the section comment at :540-549 now states. Load-bearing and confirmed working.

Tripwire scrutinized end-to-end with the reviewer's own decoy at an unrelated path
(scratchpad/auditdecoy/.i3/scripts/clip-feed.sh): printed FATAL, named pid 1491965 with full
cmdline, EXIT=1, aborted BEFORE starting Xvfb/copyq/feeder — and the foreign feeder SURVIVED
(kill -0 confirmed). It never kills. It is called once at preflight (:212) before any feeder
of its own exists, so it cannot false-abort mid-run on the suite's own feeder.

Criteria verified:
- base i3/config binds key -> qs-clip.sh: verified iteration 1; i3 -C duplicate-keybinding
  probe + drop-keybind mutation both green in this run.
- native.conf autostarts copyq :0 + clip-feed.sh; wsl.conf copyq-only: green (incl.
  drop-copyq / drop-feeder / wsl-feeder mutants all failing on the mutant as required).
- autostart idempotent: 'servers on :95' == 1 across two simulated reloads; 'feeders' == 1
  across two reloads; feeder survives absent source display :94.
- rotz link path: i3/dot.yaml link entry for clip-feed.sh asserted == 1.
Tests: 53 passed, 0 failed (was 50); 3 new assertions, all three independently verified
meaningful by mutation.
Deviations: none unlogged. Scope-change note (picker not paste) honoured in config wording.

Production config UNALTERED by the fix commit: git diff aac9ef6..HEAD reports UNCHANGED for
i3/config, i3/config.d/native.conf, i3/config.d/wsl.conf, copyq/dot.yaml, i3/dot.yaml.
3371063 touched only i3/scripts/test-clip-integration.sh (+123/-2).

Live session untouched by this audit: quickshell still 4 procs, no copyq, no stray Xvfb, no
feeder, real clipboard never written, i3 only ever run as 'i3 -C'.

ACCEPTED OPERATIONAL COSTS (documented, not defects):
1. On a deployed machine you must stop your own feeder to run this suite (tripwire refuses).
   Correct trade: a refusal is recoverable and self-announcing, a silent kill is not.
2. A decoy orphaned by SIGKILL of a previous run trips the tripwire on the next run. Fails
   SAFE — aborts and names a path containing 'clip-integration-test.<pid>', self-identifying
   as test debris.
3. Cosmetic: copyq's monitor rewrites a 2-line 'running=false' ini after cleanup's rm -rf,
   leaving a 4K empty dir per run under /tmp. Pre-existing, no clipboard data persisted
   (grepped for the probe item: none). Reviewer swept the accumulated dirs.

HUMAN VERIFICATION STILL REQUIRED (unproven by machine — the end-to-end path was never
exercised against a real compositor):
  After 'rotz link', press $mod+Shift+v ONCE on the native session and confirm the
  clipboard-history picker appears and selecting an entry copies it. Every machine check
  here is structural (config parse, process counts, mutation) plus a stubbed keybind command;
  no real i3 reload was performed by design, to protect the live session.

title: quickshell history-browser UI
design:
#### type
feature
#### effort
8h
#### files_touched
quickshell/config/ClipHistory.qml, quickshell/qs-clip.sh, quickshell/config entry wiring
#### scope_change
SCOPE REDUCED by user 2026-07-20: Enter copies to clipboard, it does NOT paste. Enter invokes clip-set.sh <row> (renamed from clip-paste.sh) and closes; the user then pastes manually wherever they want.
#### success_criteria
qs-clip.sh opens window on active display (explicit DISPLAY); list == copyq size newest-first, truncated previews; type filters; Enter -> clip-set.sh row + close; Esc close; renders :0 + :10.
#### edge_cases
empty history (empty-state, Enter no-op); huge history (capped, note cap); multiline/long (preview truncated, full entry still set on clipboard); unicode previews; rapid reopen (single instance).
#### test_plan
quickshell/test-clip-history.sh: seed N items; Xvfb launch; assert model count==copyq size + previews match first line; select+Enter -> assert clip-set stub got right row argv.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED: quickshell clipboard-history picker — ClipHistory.qml (UI) + qs-clip.sh (list/set/toggle back end), wired into config/shell.qml. Enter COPIES via clip-set.sh and closes; it does not paste.

Branch bd-dotfiles-92w.4.0 @ .worktrees/bd-dotfiles-92w.4.0
Commits: 58832c4 (feature), a42f293 (test determinism fix)

DESIGN NOTE — all non-UI logic lives in qs-clip.sh, not the QML. The QML never
talks to copyq and never talks to clip-set.sh; it runs 'qs-clip.sh list' and
'qs-clip.sh set <row>'. History parsing, capping, preview truncation and
display resolution are therefore unit-testable in sh with no X server, and
what is left in QML is presentation, which is the only part a headless suite
can just observe indirectly.

Test: XVFB=<xvfb> bash quickshell/test-clip-history.sh  ->  59 passed, 0 failed
(two Xvfb displays :95/:96, isolated XDG, own copyq server; xdotool XTEST keys)

Success criteria:
- opens on the active display, DISPLAY derived not inherited: qs-clip.sh
  enumerates live quickshell instances, reads each one's session out of
  /proc/<pid>/environ and asks it (ipc show) whether it answers 'cliphistory'.
  An inherited DISPLAY is accepted only if it matches one of them; with two
  sessions live and no match it REFUSES and names them rather than guessing.
  Scenarios 'derivation: a stale DISPLAY is refused' + '...that DOES match is
  honoured'.
- list == copyq size, newest first, truncated previews: 'row count == copyq
  size', plus the model size proved through observed effect — 7 Downs over a
  4-row history selects row 3 (clamp), which only holds at exactly 4 rows.
- type filters: 'typing filters, and Enter still copies the COPYQ row'.
  Filtering to a single match that is copyq row 2 publishes 2, not 0.
- Enter -> clip-set.sh row + close; Esc closes without copying: both asserted
  on the argv a clip-set stub on disk actually received, and on whether the X
  window is still mapped.
- renders on :0 + :10: full open/select cycle runs on both :95 and :96.

Edge cases: empty history (empty state, Enter invokes nothing, window stays);
cap (QS_CLIP_CAP, 5 items cap 3 -> 3 rows); multiline/long (preview is line
one truncated at QS_CLIP_PREVIEW, and the REAL clip-set.sh then puts the FULL
multiline entry on both displays' clipboards); unicode byte-exact end to end;
rapid reopen (6 toggles -> exactly one window, ever).

clip-set.sh EXIT CODES ARE NOT SWALLOWED. Only 0 closes the picker. 1
(nothing written anywhere, safe to retry) and 2 (partial write, clipboard
indeterminate) both leave the window up with different messages: after 1 the
user is told the clipboard is unchanged and Enter retries; after 2 they are
told to check before pasting. Asserted by 3 scenarios incl. a retry.

MUTATION EVIDENCE (each applied to the shipped file, full suite re-run, then
reverted):
- M1 publish filtered position instead of copyq row -> 2 failures
- M2 filter matches everything                      -> 4 failures
- M3 list drops the last row                        -> 9 failures
- M4 swallow clip-set exit codes (always close)     -> 3 failures
- M5 guess the display instead of refusing          -> 3 failures
- M6 previews never truncated                       -> 1 failure
- M7 Escape wired to a no-op                        -> 1 failure
M5 initially SURVIVED because the sed anchor silently did not match; re-applied
via python and it died. M7 initially died only by cascade (a scenario failing
to close inverted the next scenario's toggle), so a42f293 forces the picker
shut between scenarios and M7 now dies on its own assertion.

NOT VERIFIED HEADLESSLY (stated rather than faked):
1. The production entry, config/shell.qml, is NOT launched by the suite — it
   pulls in Bar, NotificationServer and FocusBorder, which would contend with
   the live session's quickshell for the same D-Bus names. The suite launches
   the REAL ClipHistory.qml (symlinked) under a two-line ShellRoot entry; that
   config/shell.qml instantiates ClipHistory {} is asserted by inspection only.
2. Visual correctness is not asserted. The suite asserts geometry-free facts
   (window mapped / not mapped, which row Enter published). Rendering was
   confirmed once by hand: a screenshot on Xvfb :97 showed the placeholder,
   the selected row with its accent bar and both previews in order, and the
   QML log carried zero warnings — but that is a manual observation, not a
   regression test.
3. Real :0 / :10 were never used. ':10 renders' is covered by a second Xvfb
   display, which is a faithful stand-in for the socket/DISPLAY plumbing but
   not for xrdp itself.
4. Unicode FILTERING is untested (xdotool type of multibyte text into Qt is
   unreliable headlessly). Unicode PREVIEWS and unicode entries surviving the
   full copy path are both asserted.

Deviations:
- Preview is the first NON-BLANK line, not literally the first line. An entry
  starting with blank lines would otherwise render as a blank row. Asserted.
- New env knobs, all optional, all documented in the file headers: QS_CLIP_SH,
  QS_CLIP_SET, QS_CLIP_CAP (default 200), QS_CLIP_PREVIEW (default 120),
  QS_CLIP_DISPLAY. The suite uses the first two to point at the worktree and
  at a stub; production leaves them unset (asserted: with QS_CLIP_SET unset,
  the real i3/scripts/clip-set.sh is what runs).
- Filtering matches the preview text, not the entry body, so what you see is
  what you filter.
- No i3 config file was touched (task .5's job). quickshell/dot.yaml needed no
  change: config/ is already rotz-linked, and qs-clip.sh sits beside the other
  qs-*.sh launchers which are referenced by $HOME/.dotfiles path.

Discoveries filed: dotfiles-9ey (quickshell/overlay/shell.qml is a verbatim
945-line copy of config/Overlay.qml; ClipHistory deliberately avoided that
pattern).
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED (reviewer re-ran everything independently)

CRITERIA VERIFIED
- Opens on active display, DISPLAY DERIVED not inherited: qs-clip.sh:129-170
  enumerates pgrep quickshell, reads session from /proc/<pid>/environ
  (session_key_of, :116), and confirms each answers the cliphistory IPC target
  before it is eligible. Inherited DISPLAY is only ever used to SELECT among
  independently-found instances (:156), never as the source. Two live sessions
  + no match => die (:163). Reviewer-authored M5 (different anchor than the
  implementer's: 'if [ $_n -eq 1 ]' -> 'if true') => 3 failures incl.
  'exits non-zero' and 'and not on :95'. Refusal path genuinely covered.
- list == copyq size, newest-first, truncated previews: 'row count == copyq
  size' PASS; row 0 = newest add, row 3 = oldest add. M6 (drop truncation) and
  M3 (drop last row) both die.
- Type filters; Enter -> clip-set.sh <copyq row> + close: THE ANTI-BUG of this
  task. ClipHistory.qml:151 passes filtered[index].row, not index. Reviewer
  reproduced M1 (publish filtered position) => exactly 2 failures, both the
  filter scenarios ('filtered-to-one still copies row 2', 'the only match is
  row 3'). M2 (filter matches everything) => 4 failures. Row mapping is real.
- Esc closes without copying: M7 => 1 failure, and it is the Esc scenario's
  OWN assertion ('the picker is gone'), not a downstream cascade. Commit
  a42f293's inter-scenario close_picker() is confirmed to have fixed the bleed
  rather than papered over it -- no later scenario broke under M7.
- Renders :0 + :10: two Xvfb displays, full open/select cycle on both.
  Disclosed by implementer as a stand-in for xrdp; accepted.

MODEL-SIZE INFERENCE IS SOUND (audited specifically). moveDown clamps at
filtered.length-1, so 7 Downs lands on min(7, N-1); landing on row 3 is
possible only at N=4. Verified EMPIRICALLY, not just analytically: under M3
(list drops the last row, N=3) the assertion '7 Downs over 4 rows selects row
3' FAILS. The inference discriminates N=4 from N=3 in practice.

DESIGN CLAIM HOLDS. The QML has exactly two Process blocks (ClipHistory.qml:74
and :108) and both invoke ['sh', clipSh, ...]. Zero copyq calls, zero direct
clip-set.sh calls -- verified by grep; the only textual hits are comments. The
'logic in sh, testable without X' story is real, so the suite's coverage story
stands.

EXIT CODES. ClipHistory.qml:111-122 handles 0/1/2 plus a fallback; only 0
calls hide(). M4 (always close) => 3 failures. Messages do not overstate: exit
1 says 'the clipboard is unchanged' (matches clip-set.sh's 'nothing written'),
exit 2 says 'some displays may hold it, some may not; check before pasting'
(indeterminate, correctly NOT reported as failure).

MUTATION EVIDENCE -- REVIEWER-RUN, every patch asserted to have applied
(byte-delta + git diff --stat 1-line change; the M5 false-negative class was
specifically guarded with an anchor-count check that exits 3 on no-match):
  M1 filtered position   -> 2 fails    M5 guess the display -> 3 fails
  M2 filter matches all  -> 4 fails    M6 no truncation     -> 1 fail
  M3 drop last row       -> 8 fails    M7 Esc no-op         -> 1 fail
  M4 swallow exit codes  -> 3 fails
All 7 killed. (M3 was 8 not the reported 9 -- reviewer's mutant formulation
differs slightly; immaterial, dies decisively either way.)

TESTS: 59 passed, 0 failed, re-run by the reviewer from a clean worktree.
Xvfb was extracted from the pacman package into scratch (xorg-server-xvfb is
not installed -- dotfiles-saa) and passed via the suite's XVFB= override.

AUTOMATED CHECKS: no TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK/stub/placeholder in any new file. No
dead code. Hard rejects clear: qs-clip.sh is #!/bin/sh (:1) with set -u (:44);
no bashisms; every xclip call in the suite is timeout-wrapped (:531,:533,:546);
inherited DISPLAY is never trusted.

REVIEWER ADDED EVIDENCE THE IMPLEMENTER DID NOT HAVE. Disclosure #1 was that
config/shell.qml wiring is asserted 'by grep only'. Reviewer ran qmllint -I
config/ on the PRODUCTION shell.qml: exit 0, no unresolved-type or ClipHistory
complaints. So the production entry point parses AND ClipHistory {} resolves
as a local component -- the real risk behind that disclosure (shell.qml fails
to load) is now covered statically, without launching a contending instance.
Follow-up issue NOT warranted.

ENV KNOBS SAFE. Repo-wide grep: QS_CLIP_* is referenced by exactly three files
-- qs-clip.sh, ClipHistory.qml (definitions/defaults) and test-clip-history.sh
(the only setter). Nothing in i3, rotz, dot.yaml or any launcher sets one, so
no test-only path can activate in production. Confirmed positively by the
scenario asserting that with QS_CLIP_SET unset the real i3/scripts/clip-set.sh
is what runs.

DEVIATIONS -- all logged by the implementer, all acceptable:
- Preview is the first NON-BLANK line (blank-leading entries would render as a
  blank row); asserted.
- Five optional QS_CLIP_* knobs; production-unset, verified above.
- Filtering matches preview text, not entry body (what you see is what you
  filter) -- consistent with the visible list.
- No i3 config touched (task .5's scope); quickshell/dot.yaml needed no change
  (config/ already rotz-linked).

KNOWN COVERAGE BOUNDARIES (accepted, honestly disclosed, none contract-bearing)
- The suite has NO assertion on status-message TEXT (verified: no such grep
  hit anywhere in the file), so swapping the exit-1 and exit-2 wording would
  survive. Messages verified correct by reading instead. Distinguishing the
  wording is above the task's success_criteria.
- Visual correctness not regression-tested (manual screenshot only).
- ':10' is a second Xvfb, not real xrdp.
- Unicode FILTERING untested (xdotool multibyte into Qt); unicode previews and
  the full unicode copy path ARE asserted.
These were stated rather than faked; honest disclosure credited.

title: clipboard-set script (clip-set.sh)
design:
#### type
task
#### effort
3h
#### files_touched
i3/scripts/clip-set.sh (replaces clip-paste.sh), i3/scripts/test-clip-set.sh, docs/notes/spec/sp014.md
#### scope_change
SCOPE REDUCED by user 2026-07-20: no synthesized paste. The script no longer detects window class, no longer synthesizes ctrl+v / ctrl+shift+v, and does not use xdotool for keystrokes. It only places the entry on the clipboard; the user pastes manually.
#### success_criteria
Given a copyq row, sets CLIPBOARD+PRIMARY on BOTH live displays (:0 and :10) to that entry text; content byte-exact incl. multiline + unicode + large entries; a display that is absent is skipped without error; exit nonzero + no selection writes on bad/missing/out-of-range row or empty entry.
#### edge_cases
one display absent (skip, still succeed on the other); no display at all (nonzero exit); multiline; unicode/emoji; empty entry; huge entry (1MB+, INCR); bad/out-of-range row.
#### test_plan
i3/scripts/test-clip-set.sh under Xvfb with TWO displays up: assert the entry is readable from BOTH displays CLIPBOARD and PRIMARY, byte-exact; tear one display down -> assert still succeeds on the survivor; assert nonzero + zero selection writes on the guard cases. Mutation-test: breaking the second-display write must fail an assertion.
#### notes
CLI contract consumed by task .4 (quickshell picker invokes it per selected row). Exit-code semantics must be stated precisely and match actual behavior.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED (attempt 2, scope-reduced): clip-paste.sh -> clip-set.sh; no synthesized paste, writes every live display.

Branch bd-dotfiles-92w.3.0 — commits 19faf3b (pure git mv, keeps --follow history) + a369d6c (reduction + spec).

STRIPPED (all paste-path only, deleted not commented): focused_window(), derive_display() + its socket-glob probe + CLIP_PASTE_DISPLAY pin, the root-window guard, keystroke_for_class() + the 20-entry terminal class table, xdotool getwindowclassname, xdotool key --clearmodifiers, and xdotool from the required-tools list. Test side: the paste-target.py X client fixture, start/stop_target*, await_paste, and the python-xlib dependency.

KEPT: plain 'copyq read' client contract, CLIPBOARD+PRIMARY writes, timeout-wrapped xclip everywhere, ownership-settle poll, all guard cases, settle()/arm()/arm_file() copyq seeding, byte-exactness rigor (multiline / unicode / 1MB INCR).

NEW display behavior: enumerate $CLIP_SET_SOCKET_DIR/X* (default /tmp/.X11-unix), write CLIPBOARD then PRIMARY on each, poll each until it serves the entry back. A display that refuses the CLIPBOARD write is a dead session -> skipped silently. Zero displays accepted -> exit 1. Enumeration order is irrelevant by construction (the :0-beats-:10 bug class is gone, not patched).

EXIT-CODE CONTRACT (gap 3 resolved, header and behavior now agree):
  0 = entry on CLIPBOARD+PRIMARY of every live display, each observed serving it back
  1 = precondition failed, NO selection written anywhere (bad/missing/out-of-range row, empty entry, missing tool, no live display). All checked before the first write.
  2 = partial write, state indeterminate (PRIMARY write failed after CLIPBOARD succeeded, or ownership did not settle).

TESTS: i3/scripts/test-clip-set.sh, two Xvfb displays for every scenario, 75 assertions, 75 passed 0 failed. DISPLAY is exported as :987 (nonexistent) on every invocation, so nothing can reach a selection by inheritance.

MUTATION EVIDENCE (Xvfb extracted from the pacman pkg into scratch; suite honours XVFB=):
  M1 break-second-display-write (break after first success) -> 14 FAIL, 61 pass  [the gap-2 killer]
  M2 reversed enumeration order (ls -r)                     -> 0 FAIL  [EXPECTED: order-independence is the fix]
  M3 PRIMARY write redirected to SECONDARY                  -> 14 FAIL, 61 pass
  M4 succeed when zero displays were written                -> 4 FAIL
  M5 dead display fatal instead of skipped                  -> 2 FAIL
  M6 empty-entry guard removed                              -> 11 FAIL

SPEC (docs/notes/spec/sp014.md): solution part 3 rewritten (no paste, publishes to every live display); plan file tree clip-paste.sh -> clip-set.sh with new description; the 'no xdotool type' anti-pattern reworded to 'no text is ever retyped into a window'; the never-trust-DISPLAY bullet now describes enumeration; Task 3 block retitled + scope_change/success_criteria/edge_cases/test_plan rewritten. Also fixed four stale paste-under-cursor claims outside the four listed targets (problem capability-boundary line, picker paragraph, trade-offs line, and Task 4's two clip-paste.sh references) — a spec asserting removed behavior seemed worse than the extra surface.

Deviations: none against the reduced contract.
Discoveries: dotfiles-0xr (pin CLIP_PASTE_DISPLAY) is now moot — noted on that issue, recommend won't-fix.
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED (re-audit vs the reduced contract)

Criteria verified (independent re-run, not the implementer's numbers):
- Sets CLIPBOARD+PRIMARY on BOTH live displays: suite re-run 75 passed / 0 failed with Xvfb 21.1.24 extracted to scratch. assert_on_both() reads each of the 4 selections from outside the script (test-clip-set.sh:110-115).
- Byte-exact multiline/unicode/1MB: newline-count, byte-length and 1000000-byte assertions all green (test-clip-set.sh:232-257).
- Absent display skipped without error: 'dead-socket-among-live' exits 0 with both real displays set; 'surviving-display' kills :94 mid-suite and still exits 0 (test-clip-set.sh:261-271, 331-345).
- Nonzero + zero selection writes on guard cases: bad-row / missing-row / out-of-range / empty-entry / no-display / only-dead all exit 1 AND pass assert_untouched (4 sentinel selections each).

GAP 1 (:0-beats-:10 lexicographic glob) — ELIMINATED BY CONSTRUCTION, verified by reading clip-set.sh:123-144: the loop has no break and no first-match; every socket is written. derive_display() and the socket-glob probe are gone from the tree (grep: zero hits for xdotool/derive_display/CLIP_PASTE/clip-paste repo-wide).

GAP 2 (display branch had zero coverage) — CLOSED with a discriminator the implementer did not run. Reproduced M1 (break after first display) = 14 FAIL, every failure on :94. Then built M8 (skip the FIRST display, asymmetric to M1) = 17 FAIL, every failure on :93. Symmetric kill on both sides proves the suite observes each display independently, so M2's 0-failures is genuine order-independence and not an uncovered branch. Also reproduced M4 (succeed on zero displays) = 4 FAIL.

GAP 3 (exit 1 claimed 'nothing written' while 3 paths wrote first) — CLOSED by code order, not by rewording the header: all die()/exit-1 sites are clip-set.sh:74,77,81,89,92,93, strictly before the first write at :131. The only other exit 1 (:147) is reachable only when WROTE==0. Both post-write failures use die_partial()/exit 2 (:139,:141).

Scope reduction is clean: zero vestigial code. The 2 grep hits for 'paste' are prose stating what the script does NOT do (:29) and why CLIPBOARD matters (:99). sh -n clean; both files mode 100755; git log --follow reaches b984a78 so the rename preserved history.

Exit code 2 (newly invented, not in the contract): ACCEPTED. Contract said 'exit nonzero'; 2 satisfies it and carries strictly more information for task .4 — 1 = safe to retry/fall back, 2 = clipboard indeterminate. Header states it precisely and behavior matches, which is what the task's notes field demanded.

Spec edits beyond the four assigned targets: REVIEWED AS CONTENT, all four accurate stale-claim removals following directly from the user's scope reduction. Task 4's clip-paste.sh -> clip-set.sh rename was not optional — leaving it would have pointed .4's implementer at a nonexistent script.

Residual risks recorded for .4/.5, neither a gap:
- Liveness heuristic conflates 'absent' with 'transiently unresponsive': a live display that fails the 5s-timeout CLIPBOARD write is skipped silently, so a real failure could read as exit 0 if another display succeeded. Window is narrow (the op is connect + XSetSelectionOwner + fork). Documented at clip-set.sh:48-50; contract gave no prescription for transient.
- settled_on() compares only a 64-byte prefix (clip-set.sh:108-119), so a prior clipboard sharing a 64-byte prefix could settle early. Consequence is exit 0 marginally early, not wrong content.

Tests: 75 passed, 0 failed (all 75 new). Mutants reproduced: M1 14 FAIL, M4 4 FAIL, plus reviewer-authored M8 17 FAIL.
Deviations: none against the reduced contract. Implementer self-flagged the spec over-reach and the discarded git reset --hard; both verified harmless.

title: cross-display feeder (native :10->:0)
design:
#### type
task
#### effort
5h
#### files_touched
i3/scripts/clip-feed.sh
#### success_criteria
:10 CLIPBOARD change -> appears in :0 copyq once within 1s; survives :10 absent/down (no crash, no busy-loop, CPU<1%); repeat copy deduped.
#### edge_cases
:10 not running; hung selection owner (timeout not block); repeat copy; binary/image (skip); double-start (single-instance guard).
#### test_plan
i3/scripts/test-clip-feed.sh: Xvfb :10; feed marker -> assert :0 read 0==marker + size+1; repeat -> size unchanged; kill :10 -> feeder alive + CPU quiescent 3s.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED: i3/scripts/clip-feed.sh (feeder) + i3/scripts/test-clip-feed.sh (27-assertion headless suite).

Evidence:
- ':10 CLIPBOARD change -> appears in :0 copyq once within 1s': scenarios cross-display-capture + capture-latency; measured 58ms (assert <1000ms) with the shipped POLL=0.5.
- 'survives :10 absent/down, no crash, no busy-loop, CPU<1%': scenario src-teardown — feeder alive after SIGKILL of the source X server, 0 jiffies over 3s (threshold 5); plus src-returns and start-with-src-absent (cold start before the display exists).
- 'repeat copy deduped': scenario repeat-copy-deduped (size unchanged over 3s) + distinct-copy-after-repeat (a genuinely new copy still lands).
- Edge cases: hung/absent owner (every xclip in timeout, clip-feed.sh:85,110); image (image-skipped); empty selection (empty-selection-skipped); double-start (double-start-guarded, flock single-instance).
- SECRET LAUNDERING (contract point 3 of copyq/dot.yaml): 'copyq add' bypasses copyq automatic commands, so the :0 hint-drop rule cannot fire on fed items. The feeder inspects TARGETS BEFORE reading the payload and skips any selection advertising application/x-kde-passwordManagerHint (clip-feed.sh:131-135). Asserted by scenario secret-not-laundered (with the shipped commands.ini active on the receiving server) and by CONTROL hint-check-is-load-bearing, which runs a patched copy of the feeder with only that gate stripped and shows the secret IS fed without it.
- Contract compliance: plain 'copyq add' invocation, no XDG_CONFIG_HOME juggling, no second tab, no clipboard hijack on :0 (add, never copy).
- Tests: i3/scripts/test-clip-feed.sh — 27 passed, 0 failed. Run: PATH=<xvfb>:$PATH i3/scripts/test-clip-feed.sh
- Mutation-tested the suite (4 mutations, all caught): dedup removed -> 1 fail; inherited DISPLAY trusted -> 8 fails; -s empty guard removed -> 2 fails; security gate removed -> 6 fails.
- Commits: f4397bd, b1bc9d0 on bd-dotfiles-92w.2.0
- Deviations: (1) TARGETS text-target filter written then REMOVED as dead code — mutation showed removing it changed no outcome (xclip already refuses an image-only owner). (2) Real bug found and fixed: children inherit the flock fd, so a feeder restarted right after the old one is killed saw the lock still held and exited as a duplicate, leaving nothing running — every child now closes fd 9. (3) Xvfb absent on host, extracted from the pacman package into scratch; filed dotfiles-s1v.
- Untouched by design: i3/config.d/native.conf (task .5 wires the autostart), i3/scripts/clip-paste.sh (task .3).
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED

Criteria verified (reviewer re-ran everything; 27/0 reproduced independently):
- ':10 CLIPBOARD change -> :0 copyq within 1s': suite on my own displays (:88/:87), capture latency 241ms measured, history grew by exactly 1, 'cq read 0' == marker.
- 'survives :10 absent/down, no crash, no busy-loop, CPU<1%': src-teardown scenario re-run — feeder alive after SIGKILL of source X server, 110->110 jiffies over 3s (0 used, threshold 5). src-returns + start-with-src-absent also green.
- 'repeat copy deduped': repeat-copy-deduped (size unchanged over 3s) + distinct-copy-after-repeat.
- Edge cases: hung/absent owner (every xclip timeout-wrapped, clip-feed.sh:85,110,116); image-only owner verified independently — xclip exits 1 'target STRING not available', nothing fed; empty selection (-s guard, mutation-confirmed); double-start (flock, exactly 1 process remains).

SECURITY GATE independently verified at the X protocol level (stronger than the
shipped test, which only asserts history contents). Instrumented selection owner
logging every SelectionRequest:
- Shipped script vs hint-bearing owner: 16 TARGETS, 0 payload requests. Then 8 TARGETS, 0 payload on a second run. The secret payload provably never leaves the owner process.
- Reviewer's own strip of the gate (sed '133,135d' on the shipped file, NOT the test's awk): 3 TARGETS + 3 UTF8_STRING — the payload IS fetched. Gate is load-bearing.
- Non-hint owner control: payload IS fetched (UTF8_STRING requested), so the skip is specific to the hint, not a broken feeder.

Deviations — all three verified acceptable:
1. Removed TARGETS text filter. Confirmed genuinely dead: image-only owner is already refused by xclip itself (exit 1, verified), and a mixed image+text owner (browser-style) correctly fetches and feeds the text. Spec edge case 'binary/image (skip)' remains covered.
2. flock fd-9 fix. Verified real and load-bearing by mutation: shipped script survives the i3-reload kill-then-restart path (replacement ALIVE); with 9>&- removed from nap(), the replacement EXITS and NO feeder is left running. Exactly the bug described.
3. Xvfb extracted to scratch, no sudo, no system change. Reviewer did the same.

Contract compliance (copyq/dot.yaml): plain 'copyq add' with only DISPLAY prefixed, no XDG juggling (point 1); 'add' never 'copy' so :0 clipboard is not hijacked; point 3 correctly identified as unable to protect fed items, hence the feeder's own gate. Follows the clip-sync.sh mould (shebang, set -u, mktemp+trap, cmp -s dedup, timeout-wrapped xclip).

Automated checks: no TODO/FIXME/stub/dead code; no bare xclip -o; every X call carries explicit DISPLAY=; #!/bin/sh + set -u; exec bits set; only the 2 intended files touched (clip-paste.sh and i3 config untouched, sibling agent unaffected). 'Cant open display' backoff checked for fragility: hardcoded format string, xclip has no libintl linkage, message identical under LC_ALL=de_DE.UTF-8 — not locale-fragile.

Tests: 27 passed, 0 failed (both new files). Suite has genuine negative controls and selections are owned by python-xlib, not copyq, so capture assertions cannot false-pass.

Follow-up filed: dotfiles-l6s (P1) — TOCTOU window of 10.3-13.3ms measured between the TARGETS gate and the payload read; a password copy landing inside that gap (~2% per copy) would be laundered before the next poll catches it. Inherent to X (no atomic targets+payload fetch), not a defect in the shipped gate, and outside this task's stated criteria — hence a follow-up, not a rejection.

title: copyq backend — install + memory-only + hint-drop
design:
#### type
task
#### effort
6h
#### files_touched
copyq/dot.yaml, copyq/copyq.conf, copyq/commands.ini
#### success_criteria
rotz installs copyq + starts server on DISPLAY; plain copy -> copyq read 0 matches; x-kde-passwordManagerHint=secret copy NOT captured (size unchanged); after restart size 0, no on-disk history files.
#### edge_cases
image/non-text (skip); empty; multi-MB text; two rapid copies both in order.
#### test_plan
copyq/test-copyq.sh via Xvfb: plain-text capture assert; hint-atom copy size-unchanged assert; restart -> size 0 + find datadir empty. Named scenarios.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED: copyq memory-only clipboard backend with secret-drop rule.

Evidence (test: copyq/test-copyq.sh — 20 passed, 0 failed):
- rotz links + install render: 'rotz --dry-run -d <wt> link copyq' resolves
  copyq.conf -> ~/.config/copyq/copyq.conf and commands.ini ->
  ~/.config/copyq/copyq-commands.ini; 'rotz --dry-run install copyq' renders
  'sudo pacman -S --needed --noconfirm copyq xclip' on this Manjaro host.
- plain copy captured: scenario plain-text-capture — size 0->1,
  'copyq read 0' == hello-plain-marker.
- hint item NOT captured: scenario secret-dropped — size unchanged, newest
  item still the prior plain copy.
- restart -> size 0: scenario restart-history-empty.
- nothing on disk: scenario no-disk-persistence — no file under the config
  dir contains item content (incl. a 3 MB item, well over
  item_data_threshold=1024); tab .dat absent or <=8 bytes.
- edge cases all asserted: 3 MB text stored at exactly 3000000 bytes
  (not truncated); two rapid copies keep order (read 0 == rapid-TWO,
  read 1 == rapid-ONE); empty copy leaves the server responsive; image/png
  target adds no text item.
- config not clobbered: repo copyq.conf + commands.ini byte-identical after a
  full server lifecycle through the rotz symlinks; symlink not replaced.

Test has teeth — mutation-tested against the shipped artifacts:
- strip the hint rule from commands.ini -> secret-dropped FAILS (2 failures,
  size 1 vs expected 2).
- flip store_items to true in copyq.conf -> no-disk-persistence and
  restart-history-empty FAIL (3 failures).
Plus two in-suite negative controls proving the assertions are not vacuous.

Commits on bd-dotfiles-92w.1.0: 5e66bb6, 44cf782
Deviations: see the preceding DEVIATIONS note (6 items; the significant ones
are the store_items mechanism replacing the draft's tmpfs relocation, and the
draft's false 'copyq drops hint items natively' claim).
Blocker: copyq could not be installed system-wide (sudo password); verified
against copyq 16.0.0 extracted from the pacman package instead.
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED (independent re-verification, not taken on faith)

Reviewer re-ran everything against the REAL pacman-installed copyq 16.0.0-1
(/usr/bin/copyq, 'pacman -Qo' confirmed), on a self-provisioned Xvfb 21.1.24.
Result: 20 passed, 0 failed — matches the implementer's report exactly.

Criteria verified:
- rotz installs copyq + starts server on DISPLAY: 'rotz --dry-run -d <wt> link
  copyq' resolves copyq.conf -> ~/.config/copyq/copyq.conf and commands.ini ->
  ~/.config/copyq/copyq-commands.ini; 'rotz --dry-run install copyq' renders
  'sudo pacman -S --needed --noconfirm copyq xclip' + 'rotz link copyq --force'
  (the established repo idiom — same shape as tmux/st/quickshell/xterm
  dot.yaml). Server started on Xvfb :97/:94/:93 in every run.
- plain copy -> copyq read 0 matches: scenario plain-text-capture, size 0->1,
  read 0 == hello-plain-marker.
- hint copy NOT captured: scenario secret-dropped, size unchanged AND read 0
  still the prior plain item (guards against silent replacement).
- after restart size 0, no on-disk history: scenario restart-history-empty +
  no-disk-persistence. Reviewer went BEYOND the suite here — the suite only
  greps $XDG_CONFIG_HOME, so I ran an independent canary copy and grepped the
  ENTIRE isolated tree (config + XDG_DATA_HOME + XDG_CACHE_HOME) plus the real
  ~/.config/copyq, ~/.local/share/copyq, ~/.cache/copyq: zero hits. Only files
  left on disk are copyq_geometry.ini / copyq_tabs.ini / copyq-filter.ini /
  copyq.pub / copyq-monitor.ini — no item payload. Satisfies sp014's explicit
  'verify nothing lands under ~/.config/copyq / ~/.local/share/copyq'.

Edge cases all asserted and re-observed green: 3 MB item stored at exactly
3000000 bytes (not truncated); two rapid copies keep order (read 0 == rapid-TWO,
read 1 == rapid-ONE); empty copy leaves server responsive ('copyq eval 1+1'==2);
image/png target adds no text item. Plus config-not-clobbered (md5 of both repo
inis identical after a full server lifecycle; symlink not replaced by a file).

MUTATION TESTING RE-RUN BY REVIEWER (the implementer's central claim, verified
rather than trusted — this is a secret-handling boundary):
- Stripped the hint rule from the SHIPPED commands.ini -> 18 passed, 2 FAILED
  (secret-dropped both assertions). The rule is genuinely load-bearing.
- Flipped store_items=true in the SHIPPED copyq.conf -> 17 passed, 3 FAILED
  (no-disk-persistence x2 + restart-history-empty). Confirms the .dat
  persistence path is inside the config dir, i.e. the suite's grep scope is
  the correct one.
Both mutation results match the implementer's numbers exactly. The suite's two
in-suite negative controls further prove the phase-1 assertions are not vacuous.
The implementer's self-reported earlier false pass is corrected: capture is now
driven by a real multi-target X selection owner (python-xlib), not 'copyq copy'
(which copyq ignores as self-owned).

sp014 hard rejects — all clear:
- no bare 'xclip -o' without timeout: every xclip call is 'timeout 10/30'
  wrapped (test-copyq.sh:208,222,230); no '-o' read anywhere.
- no persistent-disk history: verified above, beyond spec scope.
- never trust inherited DISPLAY: DISPLAY set explicitly on every invocation.
- config via rotz dot.yaml not dtlf.sh: dtlf.sh untouched in the diff.
No TODO/FIXME/stub/placeholder in copyq/. No dead production code.

Downstream contract (tasks .2/.3/.4 depend on it) CHECKED AGAINST REALITY:
dot.yaml's claim that copyq puts its socket inside the config dir is true —
started a server with an isolated XDG_CONFIG_HOME and found the socket at
<cfg>/copyq/.copyq_s. That validates the design decision to keep config at
~/.config/copyq so clients run a bare 'copyq' with no env juggling.

Deviations: logged (6) and acceptable.
- store_items=false replacing the draft's XDG_CONFIG_HOME->tmpfs relocation is
  NOT a spec deviation at all: sp014's plan literally reads 'copyq item store
  on tmpfs / item-saving disabled' — both were sanctioned. The chosen path is
  the better one, for the socket reason verified above.
- The draft's 'copyq drops hint items natively' claim was false, and the
  implementer was right to say so: my own mutation run shows a hint-bearing

title: sp013 T2: wrapper window verb + stop integration
design:
#### type
task

#### effort
6h

#### files_touched
nushell/actions/preview, nushell/actions/preview-test

#### success_criteria
- preview window [N] (default 1) spawns preview-wv detached, writes ~/.cache/preview/wv-<N>.pid; alive pid with /proc/<pid>/cmdline matching preview-wv → idempotent no-op; stale/mismatched pid → cleaned + respawned
- Missing binary → error naming 'rotz install preview-webview'; daemon not answering /status → error naming 'preview start'
- preview window [N] --close SIGTERMs cmdline-verified pid + removes pidfile; preview stop closes all wv-*.pid windows before daemon

#### edge_cases
Recycled pid (alive, cmdline mismatch) → treated stale, never killed. Double --close → idempotent clean message. Pidfile dir missing → created. Concurrent window <N> → at most one survivor, no orphan pidfile. --close on never-opened N → clean message exit 0.

#### test_plan
Extend nushell/actions/preview-test with fake preview-wv (sleep stub on temp PATH, existing fake-daemon pattern): spawn writes pidfile; second spawn no-ops; stale cleaned + respawned; cmdline-mismatch pid NOT killed (assert stub untouched); --close terminates + removes pidfile; stop closes all; missing-binary error names rotz recipe; daemon-down error names 'preview start'.
notes:
REVIEW FIX: orphaned sleep-3600 leak in fake preview-wv stub (reviewer-caught gap).

Root cause: the stub's TERM trap did `exit 0` without killing its
backgrounded `sleep 3600` child (nushell/actions/preview-test:139
formerly `trap 'exit 0' TERM`). Every wrapper-initiated SIGTERM (window
--close, stop) killed the outer script but orphaned the sleep to PID 1 for
up to an hour. 5 leaks per suite run (tests 10, 12, 13, 17x2) — reviewer's
ps showed 20 PPID-1 orphans across 4 batches. Confirmed independently: my
own ps -eo pid,ppid,etimes,cmd | grep 'sleep 3600' showed the same 20
orphans (etimes 198-407s, matching my 2 preceding runs + reviewer's 2)
before the fix — my earlier 'no leftover processes' claim was wrong; I'd
only checked stub *scripts* (pgrep -f preview-wv) and the daemon, not the
stub's own detached sleep grandchild.

Fix: trap changed to `trap 'kill $! 2>/dev/null; exit 0' TERM` — $! is
the most recently backgrounded job (the sleep), killed before the script
exits. No-exec design preserved (tracked pid's /proc/<pid>/cmdline still
reads the script's own args, so cmdline-verification tests are unaffected).

Verification (all on branch bd-dotfiles-hoe.2.0, worktree
/home/jan/.dotfiles/.worktrees/bd-dotfiles-hoe.2.0):
- Reaped the 20 pre-existing orphans (pkill -9 -f '^sleep 3600$'), confirmed
  zero via ps before re-testing.
- nu nushell/actions/preview-test: 19/19 passed, run 3x total (2 before
  commit + 1 after against the committed tree).
- ps -eo pid,ppid,etimes,cmd | grep 'sleep 3600' after each of the 3 runs:
  empty every time — zero orphans.

Commit: ebb785a on bd-dotfiles-hoe.2.0 (stacked on 735aee2). git log
main..HEAD now shows 735aee2 (feat) + ebb785a (fix).
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED (re-audit after rejection fix)

Criteria verified (first audit + delta re-audit):
- window spawn/pidfile/idempotency/stale-handling: nushell/actions/preview:220-274 (window-spawn), :78-84 (dir creation); tests 10-13 pass on reviewer rerun.
- Loud errors: missing binary names 'rotz install preview-webview' (preview:238-242, test 18); daemon down names 'preview start' (preview:247-251, test 19).
- --close + stop sweep: window-close preview:199-215 SIGTERMs only after pid-alive AND pid-cmdline-matches (:206); close-all-windows preview:288-301 runs before daemon stop in main-stop (:310); tests 14-17.
- Anti-patterns: single new SIGTERM in diff, cmdline-gated; /status probe before every spawn (:247-251); no consumer calls preview-wv directly (repo grep clean).
- Rejection gap CLOSED: commit ebb785a (1-line delta, preview-test:139) fixes the stub TERM trap to 'kill $! 2>/dev/null; exit 0' — reviewer reran suite, 19/19 pass, and 'ps -eo pid,ppid,etimes,cmd | grep sleep 3600' returned ZERO orphans post-run (previously 5 PPID-1 orphans per run).

Tests: 19/19 passed (9 pre-existing + 10 new), independently rerun by reviewer twice across audits.
Deviations: logged and acceptable — (1) test-fixture stdout redirect for bystander helper; (2) fresh .0 worktree after phantom-dispatch note; (3) no file-locking for concurrent window <N>, matches existing daemon-start robustness level, test_plan requires no concurrency test.

title: sp013 T1: preview-wv Rust crate + rotz build
design:
#### type
task

#### effort
5h

#### files_touched
preview-webview/Cargo.toml, preview-webview/src/main.rs, preview-webview/dot.yaml

#### success_criteria
- preview-wv <N> [--port P] opens window loading http://127.0.0.1:<port>/preview<N>; port resolution: flag > $PREVIEW_PORT > 4200 (ft005 api_surface GET /preview<N> consumed verbatim)
- WM_CLASS preview-wv + title 'preview <N>' (xprop-verified) — contract Task 3 float rules match on
- Exits 0 on window close; bad args → usage message + nonzero exit
- rotz install preview-webview builds ~/.local/bin/preview-wv via cargo-guarded recipe (mirrors preview/dot.yaml toolchain guard)

#### edge_cases
N non-numeric/negative → usage error nonzero. Daemon down → window shows connection error, process stays up. No DISPLAY → clean GTK init error + nonzero exit, no panic. --port non-numeric → usage error.

#### test_plan
cargo test: arg-parse table (N/port/defaults/invalid). Manual: launch against live daemon → renders /preview<N>; xprop confirms WM_CLASS; close → exit 0; DISPLAY unset → nonzero, no panic backtrace.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED: preview-wv Rust crate (wry 0.45 + tao 0.30) + rotz-cargo-guarded build recipe.

Evidence:
- preview-wv <N> [--port P] loads http://127.0.0.1:<port>/preview<N>; port resolution flag > $PREVIEW_PORT > 4200: preview-webview/src/main.rs parse_args() (unit-tested, 11 cases pass).
- WM_CLASS preview-wv + title 'preview <N>' (xprop-verified live): xprop -id <wid> WM_CLASS WM_NAME -> WM_CLASS(STRING) = "preview-wv", "preview-wv"; WM_NAME(STRING) = "preview 7". Required a raw XSetClassHint call (force_wm_class() in main.rs) since GTK3 auto-capitalizes the class field derived from prgname (would otherwise read "Preview-wv", breaking Task 3's case-sensitive i3/sway for_window class= match) — see DEVIATION below.
- Exits 0 on window close: verified live against i3/X11 (:10.0) — closed via 'i3-msg [title="preview 9"] kill' (sends WM_DELETE_WINDOW), captured process exit code = 0. (Note: xdotool windowclose on this display fell back to a raw XDestroyWindow instead of the WM_DELETE_WINDOW protocol despite WM_PROTOCOLS advertising it — a tooling quirk, not app behavior; i3's own graceful kill confirmed the real WM_DELETE_WINDOW path works correctly.)
- Bad args -> usage + nonzero exit: verified live — no N, non-numeric N, negative N, non-numeric --port all print usage to stderr and exit 2.
- No DISPLAY -> clean nonzero exit, no panic: verified live — 'env -u DISPLAY -u WAYLAND_DISPLAY preview-wv 1' prints 'preview-wv: failed to initialize GTK/GDK: Failed to initialize GTK' and exits 1, no backtrace. Required a gtk::init() pre-check before tao's EventLoop::new(), which panics internally via .expect() on init failure — see DEVIATION below.
- rotz install preview-webview builds ~/.local/bin/preview-wv via cargo-guarded recipe (preview-webview/dot.yaml, mirrors preview/dot.yaml's toolchain-guard + akm-root pattern): recipe's build+cp commands verified manually against the worktree source (rotz itself can't fully resolve akm-root/$HOME/.dotfiles/preview-webview until this branch merges — expected, matches how any new dot.yaml is tested pre-merge).
- Tests: cargo test --release -> 11 passed, 0 failed (arg-parse table: defaults, port flag override, env fallback, missing/non-numeric/negative N, bad --port value/missing, invalid env port falls back, unknown trailing arg, url/title formatting).
- cargo build --release: clean. cargo fmt --check: clean. cargo clippy --release -- -D warnings: clean.
- Commits: 20d489d on bd-dotfiles-hoe.1.0.

Deviations from design (both necessary, not scope creep — same file set: preview-webview/src/main.rs, preview-webview/Cargo.toml):
1. Added gtk/glib/gdkx11/x11 as explicit direct deps (matching tao 0.30's internal gtk-rs 0.18 versions) to call gtk::init() ourselves before tao::EventLoop::new(). tao's own EventLoop::new() calls gtk_init internally via .expect(), which PANICS with a backtrace when DISPLAY is unset/unreachable — violates the 'no DISPLAY -> clean error, no panic' edge case. Pre-checking via gtk::init() (which uses gtk_init_check(), returning Result instead of aborting) fixes this cleanly.
2. Added a raw XSetClassHint call (x11 crate, xlib feature) to force both WM_CLASS fields to literal 'preview-wv'. Without it, GTK3/X11 derives WM_CLASS from glib::set_prgname with the class field auto-capitalized ('Preview-wv'), which would silently fail i3/sway's case-sensitive for_window [class="preview-wv"] match in Task 3 — the exact contract this task's success criterion calls out ('Task 3 float rules match on'). Verified via xprop both fields now read 'preview-wv' verbatim.

Concerns for reviewer:
- The XSetClassHint fix opens a second short-lived raw Xlib connection (XOpenDisplay/XSetClassHint/XFlush/XCloseDisplay) purely to set the property; best-effort (logs + continues on failure, never fails the window). Worth a second look but matches standard practice for GTK3 apps needing full WM_CLASS control.
- gtk::init() pre-check: after it succeeds, tao's EventLoop::new() internally calls gtk::Application::new() + app.register() again — this is safe (GTK's init is idempotent once IS_INITIALIZED is set) and was verified working end-to-end (live window rendered correctly), but is worth a sanity nod in review.
- Termux/proot ARM untested per spec's known limitations (webkit2gtk + cargo availability unverified there) — out of scope for this task.
- No cargo test coverage for force_wm_class/run_window (GTK/X11-dependent, not unit-testable without a display) — covered instead by the manual xprop/i3-msg verification above per the task's own test_plan.
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED

Criteria verified (all reproduced independently by reviewer, not just implementer notes):
- URL + port resolution: main.rs parse_args/window_url; cargo test --release 11/11 pass incl. flag>env>4200 precedence and ft005-verbatim http://127.0.0.1:<port>/preview<N>.
- WM_CLASS/title: live launch on i3/:10.0 — xprop WM_CLASS(STRING) = "preview-wv", "preview-wv"; WM_NAME = "preview 42".
- Exit codes: window close via i3-msg kill -> exit 0; no/bad/negative N, bad --port, unknown arg -> usage + exit 2; DISPLAY unset -> clean GTK error + exit 1, no panic.
- Rotz recipe: executed dot.yaml installs.cmd verbatim (stub akm-root -> worktree, fake HOME): clean-cache cargo build + cp succeeded, installed binary functional. Mirrors preview/dot.yaml guard.
- Edge case daemon-down: port 4999 closed, process alive after 4s, close -> exit 0.
- Anti-patterns: no unwrap/panic on user input (unwraps only in #[cfg(test)]); engine-only binary; target/ gitignored, no artifacts tracked.

Tests: 11 passed, 11 new (all meaningful, exact-equality asserts on production parse path). fmt --check clean, clippy -D warnings clean.

Deviations: logged and acceptable — (1) gtk/glib/gdkx11/x11 direct deps + gtk::init() pre-check, required for the no-DISPLAY no-panic edge case (tao panics internally); (2) raw XSetClassHint forcing both WM_CLASS fields, required for Task 3's case-sensitive class match (GTK3 capitalizes res_class). Both verified working live.

sp012 Task 6 — /home/jan/.dotfiles/docs/notes/spec/sp012.md
notes:
CORRECTION — imagemagick verdict REVERSED: DROP -> KEEP. My earlier deviation log and evidence note were WRONG on this point; this note supersedes them.

WHAT I GOT WRONG
I claimed bench/webview/bench.sh was 'unrelated... no tie to quickshell/dot.yaml'. It is tied, and the drop would have silently broken it on a fresh machine. Verified independently before reverting:
- 'git grep -l -i imagemagick main -- *dot.yaml' -> quickshell/dot.yaml ONLY. It was the sole installer of imagemagick anywhere in this repo before b7aaa67.
- bench/webview/bench.sh:120 runs 'magick -size 1920x1080 gradient:navy-orange "$WORK/web/wall.jpg" 2>/dev/null' as a wallpaper fallback in the '--content image' mode documented at bench/webview/README.md:14.
- That call has NO availability guard. bench.sh uses a 'command -v X || log WARN' convention for its other soft deps (bench.sh:97-100, qml6/qt6-webengine) but never applied it to magick, and the '2>/dev/null' swallows even 'command not found' -> silent breakage, not a warning.

ROOT CAUSE OF MY ERROR (worth recording so it is not repeated)
My narrow claim was actually correct and still stands: test-blink-measure.sh genuinely never used imagemagick (verified at HEAD and at its initial commit 7409b21), so the task's edge_case text about it IS wrong. But I scoped the CONSUMER search to quickshell/ and never ran the INSTALLER search repo-wide. A dep question needs the inverted check ('who declares it?' repo-wide), not just 'who calls it?' in one directory. Compounding it: imagemagick 7.1.2.27-1 is already installed on this box, so the breakage is invisible locally — a host-local 'which magick' can never catch this class. I treated a quickshell-scoped check as sufficient for a repo-scoped question.

ACTION TAKEN
- Reverted the imagemagick removal in commit 2da6d8f (stacked on b7aaa67, NOT amended into it — b7aaa67 verified intact with its original 3 files, so the reviewer's audited base is preserved). quickshell/dot.yaml is now byte-identical to main ('git diff main -- quickshell/dot.yaml' -> empty).
- Did NOT touch bench.sh — out of scope for T6; keeping the dep is the correct, simpler path.
- KEPT (unaffected, re-verified at HEAD after the revert): quickshell/region/ deletion and the i3/config.common sweep.

RE-VERIFICATION OUTPUT
- 'rg -n -i imagemagick quickshell/dot.yaml' -> lines 21, 27, 33 (Manjaro / Arch / Arch ARM), original formatting restored.
- 'i3 -C -c <wt>/i3/config' -> exit 0; 'i3 -C -c <wt>/i3/config-xrdp' -> exit 0.
- 'rg -l "quickshell/region"' excl docs/archive/.beads -> no hits (kept work holds).
- 'git show --name-status 2da6d8f' -> quickshell/dot.yaml (M) ONLY.
- Net branch diff vs main: i3/config.common + quickshell/region/shell.qml (deleted) only; dot.yaml correctly drops out.

DISCOVERY FILED: dotfiles-d59 (discovered-from dotfiles-dqo.6) — bench/webview/bench.sh depends on imagemagick with no guard and no install declaration of its own, free-riding on quickshell/dot.yaml. quickshell itself no longer needs magick (qs-region.py crops via 'scrot -a'; zero magick/convert/identify hits in quickshell/), so the dep is now held ONLY for bench.sh. DO NOT re-attempt the drop until dotfiles-d59 is resolved.
