title: T2: /file/<path> route + dispatch + path-jail + code/md renderers
design: See sp008 ### Task 2 for full H4 props.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED: /file/<path> route + dispatch + path-jail + code/md renderers.

Evidence:
- GET /file/<path> renders code via chroma and markdown via goldmark: preview/render_code.go, preview/render_md.go, wired in preview/render.go's renderFile() dispatch (markdown ext checked first, then chroma lexers.Match, else safe fallback). Test: TestRenderFileByType table test {go, markdown, txt, unknown} in preview/render_test.go — all 4 subtests pass, asserting content-type text/html + signature substring (chroma wrapper class, goldmark <h1>, or 'no preview' marker).
- path.go (preview/path.go) rejects .., absolute escape, and symlink escape with 400 BEFORE any content read. resolveInRoot() does containment check TWICE: once on the syntactic Join+Clean result (zero I/O — catches .. and absolute-escape) and once after filepath.EvalSymlinks (catches symlink escape). Containment uses a proper trailing-separator prefix check (underRoot), not naive strings.HasPrefix — TestResolveInRootRejectsSiblingPrefixCollision proves /root vs /root-evil is not confused. Unit tests: preview/path_test.go, 8 tests, all passing.
- Unknown/binary type falls back to a safe 'no preview' HTML response (renderFallback in render.go), status 200, never 500. Render failures (goldmark/chroma error) fall back to an HTML-escaped <pre> (renderPlainFallback), also never 500. Tested via TestRenderFileByType/unknown + TestRenderFileNoExtension.

Security test (task's explicit ask): handleFile in preview/server.go wires resolveInRoot -> renderFile, mapping ErrPathEscape->400, os.ErrNotExist->404, other->500.
- TestHandleFileRejectsDotDotEscapeAndReadsNothing: /file/../secret.txt -> 400, secret content (chmod 0000 tripwire) never appears in response.
- TestHandleFileRejectsSymlinkEscapeAndReadsNothing + TestHandleFileSymlinkEscapeViaFullMux: symlink inside root pointing to a chmod-0000 secret outside root -> 400 via both direct handler call AND the real srv.Handler() mux, secret content never leaked.
- NOTE: Go's stdlib http.ServeMux 307-redirects any literally-unclean URL path (containing '..') before dispatching to ANY handler — confirmed empirically, logged as a bd note. This means a live /file/../../etc/passwd request never reaches our code at all (redirects to /etc/passwd, which then 404s — safe, but not our boundary). The actual security boundary (path.go's resolveInRoot) is unit-tested directly and via handleFile called directly (bypassing the mux quirk) to prove OUR code returns 400. The symlink-escape case IS reachable through the real mux (clean URL, no dots) and is tested end-to-end.

Edge cases covered (preview/render_test.go): empty file (TestRenderFileEmpty), no-extension file (TestRenderFileNoExtension), huge file capped at maxRenderSize=5MiB with truncation marker (TestRenderFileHugeCapped), Unicode + CRLF (TestRenderFileUnicodeCRLF), nonexistent path -> 404 not 500 (TestRenderFileNonexistent, TestHandleFileNonexistentReturns404). Path with spaces/URL-encoding: net/http decodes percent-escapes before r.URL.Path reaches our code, so no separate decode needed in path.go.

Tests: 21 tests in package preview, all passing. go test ./... -race: PASS (33.9s). gofmt -l: clean. go vet ./...: clean.

Deps added: github.com/alecthomas/chroma/v2 v2.14.0, github.com/yuin/goldmark v1.7.8 (both already in local module cache, no new external dependency risk beyond what T4/T7/T8/T9 will also need per the spec's plan).

Deviations: none beyond the ServeMux-redirect note above (informational, does not change behavior or test coverage needed).

Files touched: preview/server.go, preview/render.go, preview/path.go, preview/render_code.go, preview/render_md.go, preview/go.mod, preview/go.sum (new deps) — matches task's files_touched list plus go.mod/go.sum (implied by adding chroma/goldmark).

title: akm-graph viewer: park idle label RAF loop (GPU hot at idle)
description: positionLabels() in akm-graph/static/app.js runs requestAnimationFrame unconditionally forever, calling graph.getNodePositionsMap() every frame — a GPU->CPU readback pipeline stall 60x/sec that keeps the GPU hot even when the graph is settled and idle. Fix: make the loop self-terminating (stop when simulation settled AND no pan/zoom gesture) and restart it on simulation.onStart, zoom gestures (onZoomStart/onZoomEnd), and window resize. Cosmos's own GL draw loop (cheap static points, no readback) left as-is.
notes: Implemented in akm-graph/static/app.js: split positionLabels into syncLabels() (one readback pass) + self-terminating positionLabels() that reschedules only while camMoving || graph.isSimulationRunning. Added pumpLabels() to wake the parked loop. Restart triggers wired: simulation.onStart/onEnd, events.onZoomStart(camMoving=true)/onZoomEnd(camMoving=false), window resize, and renderData(). Cosmos GL draw loop left as-is (cheap static points, no readback). Static-verified: node --check OK, go build OK, no dangling startLabelLoop refs. RUNTIME GPU verify pending — no Chrome/GPU in dev env; user must confirm with a GPU monitor while graph idle.

title: akm-graph viewer: nodes render black + links invisible (0-1 vs 0-255 color)
design: app.js supplies node/link colors as 0-1 float RGBA arrays (e.g. [0.18,0.60,1.00,1]). cosmos v1.6.1 normalizes array colors as [r/255,g/255,b/255,a] — it expects RGB in 0-255. So every color collapses to ~black and the dark linkColor [0.25,0.30,0.35,0.6] is invisible on the #0d1117 background. Symptoms: all dots black, no edges visible. Data is correct (67 nodes, 233 links delivered to setData). Slipped through because ak8.2 smoke + ak8.6 e2e only asserted node/link DATA + title, never verified rendered color/edges visually. Fix: scale RGB *255 in the color accessors (alpha stays 0-1). Reported by user opening the graph.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED: fix on branch bd-dotfiles-mry.0 (commit 43f89fa).

Root cause: cosmos v1.6.1 normalizes array colors as [r/255,g/255,b/255,a] (RGB 0-255). app.js supplied 0-1 floats -> all nodes ~black, dark links invisible on #0d1117.

Fix: app.js rgba255() scales palette RGB *255 in nodeColor + linkColor accessors (alpha stays 0-1).

Evidence:
- Headless screenshot pixel analysis (pure-python PNG decode): non-bg color buckets 10 (all-black, BEFORE) -> 60-94 (AFTER) = full type palette rendered (blue us / green sp / amber im / orange ft / purple adr / teal cat / yellow hub).
- Regression guard: smoke.sh Stage 3 screenshots fixture page, asserts >=20 distinct color buckets. Retries up to 5x (headless WebGL paint timing is racy: pre-draw frames are dark) and passes on first frame over threshold; genuine 0-1 bug is dark on ALL frames.
- Non-tautology: reverted fix -> 10 buckets every try -> FAIL; restored -> 58-94 -> PASS. Stability: fixed viewer PASS 3/3 runs.
- go test -count=1 green (app.js still embedded, TestEmbedViewerAssetsOnly etc. pass).
Left in_progress for review.
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED

Root-cause fix verified: cosmos-bundle.js normalizes array colors via [K[0]/255,K[1]/255,K[2]/255,K[3]] (confirmed by grep) — 0-1 float inputs collapse to ~black, 0-255 inputs render. app.js rgba255() (line 28) scales RGB *255, leaves alpha (c[3]) untouched. Applied to ghost nodes (line 43), typed nodes (line 44), and LINK_COLOR (line 30 -> used line 118).

Criteria verified:
- Bundle /255 normalize: grep found [K[0]/255,K[1]/255,K[2]/255,K[3]] in cosmos-bundle.js.
- rgba255 on node+link colors, alpha NOT scaled: app.js:28,30,43,44,118 confirmed by Read.
- Full smoke suite (3 stages): PASS — title 'OK nodes=10', tooltip TOOLTIP_OK, Stage 3 color 96 buckets (>=20). Try 1 dark (9) then try 2 healthy (96) demonstrating one-sided flakiness the retry handles.
- Non-tautology: reverted *255 -> [c0,c1,c2,c3], re-ran smoke -> Stages 1-2 PASS, Stage 3 FAIL max 10 buckets across all 5 tries, exit 1. Restored via git checkout, byte-identical to HEAD (empty diff).
- go test -count=1 ./...: ok akmgraph (embed test green, app.js still embedded).
- No TODOs/stubs/dead code in changed files.

Guard soundness: max-over-5-screenshots is sound for one-sided flakiness — the real bug is dark on EVERY frame (deterministic ~10 buckets, shown 5/5), timing miss is dark on SOME. Threshold 20 sits in a wide gap between dark floor (~10) and healthy (58-96), so no false-pass risk; max cannot rescue a genuine all-dark regression.

Tests: go 1 pkg ok; smoke 3/3 stages; 1 new guard (Stage 3).
Deviations: none.

title: [bug] clip-sync wedges on blocking xclip -o (no timeout) — image selection owner hangs the loop
description: clip-sync.sh calls 'xclip -selection <sel> -o' with no timeout. Under xrdp/WSLg, if the selection owner is unresponsive (e.g. an image/bmp selection, or a dead xrdp client), xclip -o blocks indefinitely. One hung read freezes the whole poll loop -> PRIMARY<->CLIPBOARD sync stops -> paste serves stale content. Observed: daemon child 'xclip -selection clipboard -o' stuck 1m34s, a separate 'xclip -selection clipboard -t image/bmp -o' stuck 2h. Fix: wrap all xclip invocations in 'timeout' so a hung read/write is abandoned and the loop continues next tick. Follow-up to dotfiles-m1y / dotfiles-f3c.
close_reason: timeout-guarded all xclip calls; live-tested both directions, no wedge

title: [bug] clip-sync autostart pkill self-matches launcher — daemon never survives i3 reload
description: wsl.conf line 24 'pkill -f i3/scripts/clip-sync.sh; ~/.i3/scripts/clip-sync.sh &' runs via sh -c. The sh -c cmdline contains the substring 'i3/scripts/clip-sync.sh', so pkill -f matches its own parent shell + the just-launched child, killing clip-sync before it runs. Result: no PRIMARY<->CLIPBOARD sync in WSL/xrdp, only a stale orphan xclip -i. Regression in dotfiles-f3c (commit 88d862d). Fix: anchor pkill pattern with $ so it matches the running script cmdline (ends in clip-sync.sh) but not the launcher (ends in &).
close_reason: Anchored pkill pattern with $; live-tested bidirectional sync + reload leaves 1 daemon

title: bd git auto-hooks installed despite akm bd init — stale jsonl import clobbers closes during work-merge
description: Found during work-merge of dotfiles-ak8.1 (branch bd-dotfiles-ak8.1.1, merge 5f88717). /home/jan/.dotfiles/.git/hooks contains post-checkout and post-merge bd hooks. During land-bd-task.sh (git checkout main + git merge), the hooks re-imported the stale .beads jsonl snapshot and reverted dotfiles-ak8.1 from CLOSED back to OPEN/P2, wiping the audit-evidence close and the implementer notes. The close had to be re-applied manually. CLAUDE.md states 'bd auto-hooks are uninstalled by akm bd init' and config.yaml has no-auto-flush/no-auto-import — but the hooks are present, so something re-installed them (bd hooks install?). Fix: re-run akm bd init (idempotent per CLAUDE.md) and find what re-installs the hooks, or the clobber loop will eat every reviewer close that coincides with a git checkout/merge.
close_reason:
Remediated — verified 2026-07-20, no code change needed. The acute clobber cannot occur in the current state.

The bug (2026-06-12): active post-checkout/post-merge bd hooks ran bd import on every git checkout/merge, reloading the stale jsonl and reverting closed issues to open.

Current state, verified:
- Every bd hook in .git/hooks is an INERT worktree-guard stub — no bd import/export/hooks-run logic in any of them (grep-confirmed). post-checkout/post-merge do not exist as active hooks; bd hooks list reports both 'not installed'.
- akm bd doctor --diagnose: all green, incl. '✓ no auto-export/import hook logic', export.auto=false, dolt.auto-commit=on, snapshot in sync with Dolt.
- Ran akm bd init (the issue's own prescribed idempotent remedy) to re-assert: 'bd auto-hooks stripped, bd config pinned, Dolt schema healed'. Removed two dead post-*.disabled leftover files from .git/hooks.

Reinstaller identified (the issue's open question 'find what re-installs the hooks'): NOT anything in the dotfiles automation (grep of *.sh/*.nu/akm found no 'bd hooks install'). It is a MANUAL 'bd hooks install' — bd's own 'bd doctor' nags 'Missing 2 recommended hooks'. Verified in an isolated throwaway repo that bd 1.0.4's 'bd hooks install' writes hooks running 'bd hooks run post-merge/post-checkout' (clobber-capable; an explicit 'bd hooks run' is NOT stopped by the no-auto-import config guard).

Recurrence is self-defended: akm's bd_race_hooks regex 'bd +(export|import|hooks +run)' MATCHES a reinstalled 'bd hooks run' hook, so akm bd doctor detects + strips it, and CLAUDE.md documents 'run akm bd init after bd hooks install re-installs auto-hooks'. The akm hardening (bd_race_hooks treating inert worktree-gate stubs as harmless; bd_strip_hooks) clearly landed after this June-12 issue and is what produced the current safe state.

Durable hardening (core.hooksPath redirect to permanently defeat bd hooks install) was considered and declined as disproportionate: acute bug remediated, recurrence needs a manual mis-step against documented guidance, and akm bd doctor already auto-heals it. Filed nothing further.

title: Proxy — prefix routes, body rewrites, ws pass-through
design:
#### type
feature
#### effort

#### files_touched

#### success_criteria
- GET /{project}/{file} ensures child, strips prefix, proxies; HTML "/static/ rewritten to "/{project}/{file}/static/ (probed v0.7.1 fixture)
- watch.js ws://\${window.location.host}/watch rewritten to /{project}/{file}/watch; other assets stream unmodified
- ws /{project}/{file}/watch upgrade-proxies to child /watch end-to-end; active conns increment clients, close decrements, lastActive updates
- rewrite-miss → unmodified body + one warning per child
- unknown project/file → 404; .. traversal → 400/404 never filesystem hit
#### edge_cases
Accept-Encoding: identity forced toward child; URL-decode before route match; trailing slash variants; child dies mid-request → 502 JSON; large SVGs stream (only HTML+watch.js buffered)
#### test_plan
httptest fake d2 backend with probed fixtures: byte-exact golden rewrites; mutated watch.js → passthrough + warning; raw-TCP minimal ws handshake through router to fake upgrade backend → echo + client count 1→0; traversal + 404 tables
notes: Fix report: hasTraversal() segment check + Clean-canonical check on decoded path (commit 0d65479), RED proof both gaps, ws assert hardened, 36 tests -race + 20x stress clean. Re-audit reviewer dispatched (background), session: a94f25d0e64381632
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED (round 2 — both round-1 gaps fixed, independently RED-proven)

Criteria verified (all via go test -race -count=1, 36 tests PASS):
- prefix route + HTML /static/ rewrite: TestProxyHTMLRewrite PASS (old "/static/ absent, "/myproject/test.d2/static/ present)
- watch.js ws rewrite + others stream unmodified: TestProxyWatchJSRewrite + TestProxyOtherStaticStreamed PASS
- ws upgrade e2e + client count 1->0 + lastActive: TestProxyWebSocketEcho (now GUARDS) + TestProxyWebSocketClientCount PASS
- rewrite-miss -> unmodified + one warning/child: TestProxyHTMLRewriteMiss + TestProxyWatchJSRewriteMiss PASS
- unknown -> 404; .. traversal -> 400/404 never FS hit: TestProxy404UnknownRoute + TestProxyTraversalRawForms + TestHasTraversal PASS
- edge cases: Accept-Encoding identity, URL-decode route, dead-child 502 all PASS

GAP 1 (CRITICAL inverted traversal guard) FIXED:
- Independently RED-proven old guard broken: strings.Contains(path.Clean(/myproject/test.d2/../../etc/passwd),'..') => path.Clean=/etc/passwd => returns FALSE (allowed). New hasTraversal() (proxy.go:172-189) rejects any /-delimited '..' segment OR non-canonical path.Clean(p)!=p (trailing-slash tolerant). Rejects all reviewer e2e forms 400.
- My own 6-extra-vector adversarial probe (....// , fully-encoded %2e%2e%2f, mixed decoded+encoded, //leading, trailing /.., ./segment): 0 real bypasses. Two apparent flags (%252e%252e double-encode, ..\..\ backslash) proven SAFE on-the-wire: child receives /%252e%252e/etc and /..%5C..%5Cetc — single HTTP decode never yields a '..' segment, '\' is not a Linux separator. No filesystem escape.

GAP 2 (ws echo tautology) FIXED:
- proxy_test.go:709/713 now t.Fatalf on read err + t.Errorf on payload mismatch. RED-proven by corrupting echo payload ping->pingX: test FAILS 'ws echo payload mismatch: want ping got pingX'; reverted -> PASS. Genuine round-trip assertion.

Scope: diff vs baseline 8dfe56f = exactly proxy.go + proxy_test.go. Round-1-approved areas (rewriteHTML/rewriteWatchJS/proxyWebSocket/warnOnce/AddClient) UNTOUCHED (git diff confirms). go vet clean; build clean; stress traversal+ws -count=20 green.

title: Child manager — spawn, touch-reload, restart, reaper
design:
#### type
feature
#### effort
8h
#### files_touched
d2/router/{children.go,children_test.go}
#### success_criteria
- ensure(key) spawns $D2_ROUTER_D2_BIN --watch --browser 0 --host 127.0.0.1 --port <assigned> once per key; concurrent callers share child (per-key lock); readiness = TCP probe with deadline
- port assignment probes free from $D2_ROUTER_CHILD_PORT_BASE (4801); occupied skipped
- reload = os.Chtimes touch; restart = SIGTERM→2s→SIGKILL→respawn new pid; stop removes from map
- reaper kills clients==0 idle past $D2_ROUTER_IDLE (30m); active untouched
- crash: Wait goroutine records lastError, removes child; daemon shutdown kills all children, no zombies
#### edge_cases
d2 binary missing → spawn error → 502 downstream; watched file deleted; restart on gone file errors; port range exhausted; child never opens port → readiness timeout 502; double-stop race
#### test_plan
fake child via $D2_ROUTER_D2_BIN test helper listening on --port: concurrent ensure → one process; restart → pid change; crash helper → lastError; reaper tiny-idle kill; port probe skips pre-occupied (net.Listen)
notes: Fix report: test-only change (commit 1f7ab3e), children.go byte-identical. New TestCrashAfterReadyRecordsLastError with RED proof (stub :193 → fail). 20x -race stress clean. Re-audit reviewer dispatched (background), session: a7912bff3c1e81e02

- crash records lastError + removes child: new TestCrashAfterReadyRecordsLastError. --crash-after-ready helper opens port, becomes ready (Ensure succeeds), then exit(3) -> wait goroutine reaches crash block (children.go:190-203), sets LastError at :193. Asserts Ensure ok + ch.LastError!=nil + map removal (race-free post-removal read).

title: focus-dim: quickshell dialogs stay bright with rest dimmed
description: Follow-up to dotfiles-0xd. When a quickshell dialog (run launcher, notifications, popups, etc.) is summoned (e.g. $mod+p), it should be treated as the focused cut-out (bright) with the rest of the screen dimmed. Currently quickshell + Rofi are in IGNORE_CLASSES which makes focus_rect=None and the entire screen dim. Switch behavior: detect quickshell-spawned dialogs and use their geometry as the focus_rect instead of ignoring them. Applies to both X11 (qs-focus-dim.py) and Wayland (FocusDimWayland.qml). Verify bar itself still stays bright (z-order distinguishes dialog from bar).
design:
# Focus-dim: Dialogs Stay Bright — Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** Use infinifu:plan-scrum-master (automated) or infinifu:plan-supervised (user reviews each batch) to implement this plan.

**Goal:** When a focused window is a quickshell `qs-` popup, Rofi, or any floating window, the dim cut-out follows it (window bright, rest dimmed). When a non-floating ignored class (the quickshell bar) is "focused", or there is no focus, the screen fully dims. Behavior identical on X11/i3 and Wayland/sway.

**Bd epic:** `dotfiles-56r`

**Predecessor:** `dotfiles-0xd` (focus-dim base, merged).

**Architecture:** Extend the existing focus-decision logic in two files (`quickshell/qs-focus-dim.py`, `quickshell/config/FocusDimWayland.qml`). Add a "is floating" signal to the focus walker so floating windows bypass the class-based ignore. Remove `rofi` from the ignore list. Invert the `qs-` title branch from ignore to focus-target.

**Tech Stack:** Python 3 + GTK3 + cairo (X11), Quickshell QML + `swaymsg` (Wayland). No new dependencies.

---

## Conventions

- Quickshell config in `quickshell/config/` (symlinked via rotz). Helper scripts in `quickshell/`.
- Verification is **manual visual confirmation** — no automated overlay test framework. Synthetic tree-walk tests for the Python `walk` / `should_ignore` are allowed and encouraged (the dotfiles-0xd T3 implementer used them).
- Commits use Conventional Commits: `feat(quickshell):`, `fix(quickshell):`, `refactor(quickshell):`.
- Anti-patterns inherited from dotfiles-0xd remain in force (see `board/done/focus-dim.md` once archived, otherwise `board/ready/focus-dim.md`):
  - No silent exception swallow — `print(..., file=sys.stderr, flush=True)` in `except` paths.
  - No Xlib at module top-level.
  - No `--no-verify` on commits. Pre-commit hooks must pass.
  - No TODO without follow-up bd task.
  - Do not introduce a new dispatch path or new files — modify in place.

## File tree (changes only)

```
quickshell/
├── qs-focus-dim.py            (modified — walker + should_ignore)
└── config/
    └── FocusDimWayland.qml    (modified — applyContainer + walker)
```

---

## Task 1: X11 — floating + qs- titled treated as focusable, rofi removed

Update the Python overlay so floating windows and `qs-` titled windows become cut-out targets. Remove `Rofi`/`rofi` from `IGNORE_CLASSES`.

**Files:**
- Modify: `quickshell/qs-focus-dim.py`
- Create: `quickshell/test_qs_focus_dim_dialogs.py` (one-shot synthetic walk test)

**Step 1: Write the failing synthetic walk test**

The codebase has no test harness, but `qs-focus-dim.py` is plain Python — we can drive `walk` + `should_ignore` directly. Create `quickshell/test_qs_focus_dim_dialogs.py`:

```python
"""One-shot synthetic walk tests for qs-focus-dim dialog handling.
Run: python3 quickshell/test_qs_focus_dim_dialogs.py
"""
import importlib.util, pathlib, sys, types

# Stub gi/cairo before import so the GTK overlay code doesn't try to draw.
sys.modules.setdefault("gi", type(sys)("gi"))
sys.modules["gi"].require_version = lambda *a, **k: None
gi_repo = types.ModuleType("gi.repository")
for n in ("Gtk", "Gdk", "GLib"):
    setattr(gi_repo, n, types.SimpleNamespace())
sys.modules["gi.repository"] = gi_repo
sys.modules["cairo"] = types.SimpleNamespace()

spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(
    "qsd", pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "qs-focus-dim.py"
)
qsd = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(qsd)


def make_leaf(cls="", title="", fullscreen=0):
    return {
        "focused": True,
        "window": 1,
        "window_properties": {"class": cls, "instance": cls},
        "name": title,
        "rect": {"x": 100, "y": 100, "width": 800, "height": 600},
        "deco_rect": {"height": 0},
        "fullscreen_mode": fullscreen,
        "nodes": [],
        "floating_nodes": [],
    }


def wrap_floating(leaf):
    return {"nodes": [], "floating_nodes": [
        {"nodes": [leaf], "floating_nodes": []}
    ]}


def wrap_tiled(leaf):
    return {"nodes": [leaf], "floating_nodes": []}


def run():
    # Case 1: regular tiled window — focusable
    qsd.focus_rect = None
    qsd._compute_focus_rect(wrap_tiled(make_leaf(cls="Firefox", title="example.com - Mozilla Firefox")))
    assert qsd.focus_rect is not None, "regular tiled window should produce focus_rect"

    # Case 2: floating qs- dialog — focusable
    qsd.focus_rect = None
    qsd._compute_focus_rect(wrap_floating(make_leaf(cls="quickshell", title="qs-overlay-projects")))
    assert qsd.focus_rect is not None, "floating qs- dialog must be focusable"

    # Case 3: floating Rofi — focusable (rofi removed from IGNORE_CLASSES)
    qsd.focus_rect = None
    qsd._compute_focus_rect(wrap_floating(make_leaf(cls="Rofi", title="rofi")))
    assert qsd.focus_rect is not None, "floating rofi must be focusable now"

    # Case 4: bar (class quickshell, tiled, no qs- title) — IGNORED
    qsd.focus_rect = "PLACEHOLDER"
    qsd._compute_focus_rect(wrap_tiled(make_leaf(cls="quickshell", title="")))
    assert qsd.focus_rect is None, "tiled quickshell bar must remain ignored"

    # Case 5: qs- titled tiled window — focusable via title prefix
    qsd.focus_rect = None
    qsd._compute_focus_rect(wrap_tiled(make_leaf(cls="anything", title="qs-anything")))
    assert qsd.focus_rect is not None, "qs- titled tiled window should be focusable"

    # Case 6: fullscreen wins over focusable
    qsd.focus_rect = "PLACEHOLDER"
    qsd._compute_focus_rect(wrap_floating(make_leaf(cls="quickshell", title="qs-foo", fullscreen=1)))
    assert qsd.focus_rect is None, "fullscreen wins over focusable"

    # Case 7: no focused window — full dim
    qsd.focus_rect = "PLACEHOLDER"
    qsd._compute_focus_rect({"nodes": [], "floating_nodes": []})
    assert qsd.focus_rect is None, "no focus → full dim"

    print("All 7 synthetic walk cases passed.")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    run()
```

**Step 2: Run the test — must FAIL**

Run: `python3 quickshell/test_qs_focus_dim_dialogs.py`
Expected: `AttributeError: module 'qsd' has no attribute '_compute_focus_rect'` — fails because the helper does not exist yet. This proves the test is wired before any implementation.

**Step 3: Refactor `qs-focus-dim.py` to apply the new rule + expose a pure decision helper**

In `quickshell/qs-focus-dim.py`:

1. Change `IGNORE_CLASSES`:
   ```python
   IGNORE_CLASSES = {'quickshell'}  # rofi removed — caught by floating rule
   ```

2. Update `should_ignore` to take `in_floating`:
   ```python
   def should_ignore(c, in_floating):
       """Return True if c should be treated as 'no focused window' (full dim)."""
       if in_floating:
           return False
       props = c.get('window_properties', {})
       cls = props.get('class', '')
       instance = props.get('instance', '')
       title = c.get('name', '')
       if title.startswith('qs-'):
           return False
       return cls in IGNORE_CLASSES or instance in IGNORE_CLASSES
   ```

3. Update `walk` to track floating-subtree descent:
   ```python
   def walk(node, parents, in_floating):
       if node.get('focused') and node.get('window'):
           return node, parents, in_floating
       for child in node.get('nodes', []):
           r = walk(child, parents + [node], in_floating)
           if r:
               return r
       for child in node.get('floating_nodes', []):
           r = walk(child, parents + [node], True)
           if r:
               return r
       return None
   ```

4. Extract the decision logic into a pure `_compute_focus_rect(tree)` at module scope so the test can call it without GTK:
   ```python
   def _compute_focus_rect(tree):
       """Update module-level focus_rect from an i3 tree dict. Pure function (no GTK)."""
       global focus_rect
       result = walk(tree, [], False)
       if not result:
           focus_rect = None
           return
       leaf, parents, in_floating = result
       if should_ignore(leaf, in_floating) or leaf.get('fullscreen_mode', 0) > 0:
           focus_rect = None
           return
       r = leaf.get('rect', {})
       deco_h = leaf.get('deco_rect', {}).get('height', 0)
       in_tabbed = any(p.get('layout') in ('tabbed', 'stacked') for p in parents)
       direct_in_tabbed = parents and parents[-1].get('layout') in ('tabbed', 'stacked')
       if in_tabbed and not direct_in_tabbed:
           deco_h = 0
       focus_rect = (
           r.get('x', 0),
           r.get('y', 0) - deco_h,
           r.get('width', 0),
           r.get('height', 0) + deco_h,
       )
   ```

5. Replace `refresh_focused._do` body so it calls `_compute_focus_rect`:
   ```python
   def refresh_focused():
       def _do():
           try:
               tree = json.loads(
                   subprocess.check_output(
                       ['i3-msg', '-t', 'get_tree'], timeout=2
                   ).decode()
               )
               _compute_focus_rect(tree)
               GLib.idle_add(_redraw_all)
           except Exception as exc:
               print(f"qs-focus-dim: refresh_focused: {exc}",
                     file=sys.stderr, flush=True)
       threading.Thread(target=_do, daemon=True).start()
   ```

The previous inline `walk` and inline decision logic (the entire `if not result: ... else: ...` block currently in `refresh_focused._do`) are now removed — replaced by the single call to `_compute_focus_rect(tree)`. Don't leave the old code around as a fallback.

**Step 4: Run the test — must PASS**

Run: `python3 quickshell/test_qs_focus_dim_dialogs.py`
Expected output: `All 7 synthetic walk cases passed.`

Also run `python3 -m py_compile quickshell/qs-focus-dim.py` to catch syntax issues. Expected: silent success.

## Task 2: Wayland — floating + qs- titled treated as focusable, rofi removed

Mirror Task 1 in `FocusDimWayland.qml`. Sway represents floating windows in their workspace's `floating_nodes` array; the walker needs to track that descent.

**Files:**
- Modify: `quickshell/config/FocusDimWayland.qml`

**Step 1: Verify sway floating tree shape (~5 minutes, before editing)**

Canonical sway tree shape (verified against sway IPC docs):

- Workspace nodes contain `nodes` (tiled children) and `floating_nodes` (floating children).
- A node inside `floating_nodes` typically has `type: "floating_con"` and either represents the window directly or contains it as a `con` child.
- Leaf con: `type: "con"`, with `app_id` (Wayland-native), `window_properties.class` (XWayland), and a `pid`.

The walker marks `in_floating = true` when descending into `floating_nodes`. This is the same propagation strategy as the i3 Python path (Task 1) — keeps logic symmetric.

If a live sway session is available, sanity-check with:
```bash
swaymsg -t get_tree | jq '.. | select(.type? == "floating_con") | {name, app_id, type}' | head
```
Expected: at least one entry per visible floating window. If unavailable, proceed using the documented shape — the synthetic guarantee is "anything reachable only via `floating_nodes` is in_floating".

**Step 2: Modify `applyContainer` to accept `in_floating`**

Current signature (line 202): `function applyContainer(c) { ... }`. Update to:

```qml
readonly property var ignoreAppIds: ["quickshell"]  // rofi removed

function applyContainer(c, in_floating) {
    var appId = c.app_id || ""
    var cls = (c.window_properties || {}).class || ""
    var title = c.name || ""

    if (c.fullscreen_mode > 0) {
        hasFocus = false
        return
    }
    // Floating windows and qs- titled windows bypass class-based ignore
    if (!in_floating && !title.startsWith("qs-")) {
        if (ignoreAppIds.indexOf(appId) >= 0 || ignoreAppIds.indexOf(cls) >= 0) {
            hasFocus = false
            return
        }
    }
    var r = c.rect || {}
    var decoH = (c.deco_rect || {}).height || 0
    fx = r.x || 0
    fy = (r.y || 0) - decoH
    fw = r.width || 0
    fh = (r.height || 0) + decoH
    hasFocus = (fw > 0 && fh > 0)
}
```

Control-flow order matters: fullscreen first (always wins), then the floating/qs- bypass, then the class-ignore check.

**Step 3: Update `focusScan.onExited` walker to track `in_floating`**

Current walker body (lines 183–194):
```qml
function walk(node) {
    if (found) return
    if (node.focused && node.pid) {
        dimOverlay.applyContainer(node)
        found = true
        return
    }
    var children = (node.nodes || []).concat(node.floating_nodes || [])
    for (var i = 0; i < children.length; i++) walk(children[i])
}
walk(tree)
```

Replace with:
```qml
function walk(node, in_floating) {
    if (found) return
    if (node.focused && node.pid) {
        dimOverlay.applyContainer(node, in_floating)
        found = true
        return
    }
    var tiled = node.nodes || []
    for (var i = 0; i < tiled.length; i++) walk(tiled[i], in_floating)
    var floating = node.floating_nodes || []
    for (var j = 0; j < floating.length; j++) walk(floating[j], true)
}
walk(tree, false)
```

**Step 4: Update the event-driven call sites (`swaySubscribe`)**

The event-driven path (`swaySubscribe.onRead` handlers around lines 94 and 97) currently calls `applyContainer(e.container)` directly. The single-container event payload does not include parent-walk info, so the safest fix is to trigger a fresh `focusScan` instead of calling `applyContainer` directly.

For each `dimOverlay.applyContainer(e.container)` call inside `swaySubscribe.onRead`, replace with:
```qml
focusScan.running = true
```

Rationale: `focusScan` walks the whole tree with `in_floating` propagation, so the floating state is always correct. The cost is one extra `swaymsg -t get_tree` per event, which is the same cost the i3 path already pays via the always-`get_tree` `refresh_focused`. Performance is acceptable for human-scale focus events.

Also add a subscription to the `floating` window event so toggling tiled↔floating updates the overlay. Inside `swaySubscribe`'s parsed-event branches, add `else if (e.change === "floating")` that also triggers `focusScan.running = true`.

**Step 5: Mechanical syntax check + grep regression checks**

Without a live sway session, agents cannot start quickshell. Run these greps in the worktree:

```bash
grep -n 'ignoreAppIds' quickshell/config/FocusDimWayland.qml
# Expected exactly one hit, content: ["quickshell"]  (no "rofi")

grep -n 'applyContainer' quickshell/config/FocusDimWayland.qml
# Expected: declaration with (c, in_floating); plus 1 call site in focusScan walk.
# applyContainer should NOT appear inside swaySubscribe blocks anymore.

grep -nE '\b(bw|br|bc|inset)\b' quickshell/config/FocusDimWayland.qml
# Expected empty (regression check from dotfiles-0xd T7 anti-pattern).

grep -n 'Component.onCompleted' quickshell/config/FocusDimWayland.qml
# Expected empty (regression check from dotfiles-0xd T6 anti-pattern).

grep -n 'walk(tree' quickshell/config/FocusDimWayland.qml
# Expected: walk(tree, false)  — starts with in_floating=false at root
```

If all greps pass, behavior verification is deferred to the user at runtime.

**Step 6: Commit**

```bash
git add quickshell/config/FocusDimWayland.qml
git commit -m "feat(quickshell): focus-dim Wayland — floating + qs- titled focusable"
```

**Success criteria:**
- [ ] All five greps in Step 5 return the expected results.
- [ ] `applyContainer` signature is `(c, in_floating)`.
- [ ] Both `walk` recursion sites pass `in_floating` correctly (`true` into `floating_nodes`, propagated otherwise).
- [ ] `swaySubscribe` event branches trigger `focusScan.running = true` instead of calling `applyContainer` directly.
- [ ] `floating` window event also triggers `focusScan`.

title: Epic: focus-dim overlay
design:
## Goal
30% black dim overlay outside focused window. Mirrors focus border lifecycle on X11/i3 + Wayland/sway.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] 30% black dim outside focused window rect, click-through, on X11 and Wayland
- [ ] Bar + quickshell overlays stay bright (z-order verified)
- [ ] Hides on fullscreen, ignored classes (quickshell/Rofi), no focused window
- [ ] Updates on focus/move/resize/workspace/binding events with <100ms lag
- [ ] Multi-monitor: cut-out only on focused monitor; others fully dimmed (X11). Wayland multi-mon inherits known border limitation.

## Approach
Sibling to FocusBorder. FocusDim.qml dispatcher → qs-focus-dim.py (X11 GTK cairo) or FocusDimWayland.qml (layer-shell). Mount in shell.qml.

## Spec
board/spec/focus-dim.md (9 tasks)
close_reason: Shipped: focus-dim overlay base (X11 GTK cairo + Wayland layer-shell). 9 tasks closed. Stories: n/a (no product/stories.yaml). Retro: parallel scrum-master flow works; main friction is worktree-base lag + reviewer interactive pause — addressable via dispatch prompt wording.

title: wm-state: restore must defer old-terminal kill until new attach
design:
## Bug
pre-clean-target kills target ws's terminals BEFORE the new respawn-flow attaches new kittys. Killing the kitty detaches its tmux client. tmux-start sets destroy-unattached on, plus tmux-cleanup hook fires on client-detached and kills any unattached non-pinned sessions in the group. Result: by the time main restore issues exec kitty -e tmux attach -t <session>, the session is gone and the new kitty's tmux attach fails. Saved tmux_window_id worthless.

## Reproducer
1. wm-state save with 3 kittys, each in its own tmux session (local_3..5).
2. wm-state restore <same ws>.
3. pre-clean-target kills the 3 kittys -> their tmux clients detach -> destroy-unattached + cleanup hook destroy local_3..5.
4. exec kitty -e tmux attach -t local_3 fails (no such session).

## Fix
Split pre-clean into two phases:
- isolate-target <name>: rename target -> <name>-old, no kill. Old terminals stay alive on <name>-old, tmux clients stay attached, sessions stay alive.
- kill-old-terminals <name>-old: walk -old ws, kill terminal windows. Run AFTER new respawn finished and new kittys attached to those sessions (so each session has the new client, destroy-unattached doesn't fire).

main restore loop:
  1. for ws: isolate-target (rename only)
  2. for ws: workspace + append_layout + exec new terminals
  3. wait for new windows mapped+swallowed (poll i3 tree, or sleep ~500ms)
  4. for ws: kill-old-terminals on <name>-old
  5. return to start_ws

Collision case (<name>-old already exists from prior restore): handle in isolate-target the same way pre-clean-target does today (kill its terminals first since those are leftover from a previous run; then rename current target on top).

## Why bug not feature
Current code kills sessions before reattach -> happy path is broken end-to-end. Smoke scenarios 1-3 in ciw.6 only passed because the implementer attached new kittys to fresh wmsmoke_a/b/c sessions they created manually, not to the saved ones. Real-world restore against grouped local_N sessions fails.

## Acceptance
- [ ] After wm-state restore, every saved tmux session is still alive (verify tmux list-sessions before+after).
- [ ] Each new kitty's tmux client is attached to the originally-saved session (verify via tmux display-message -p '#{window_id}').
- [ ] Old terminals on <name>-old are killed by end of restore.
- [ ] -old workspace contains only non-terminal leftovers (firefox etc.) after restore completes.

## Out of scope
Polling for swallow completion (could be added later); MVP can use a fixed delay after spawn loop.
close_reason: Split pre-clean into isolate-target (rename only) + kill-old-terminals (run after spawn). Restore now: rename target -> -old (phase 1), focus + append_layout + exec new attaches (per ws), sleep 600ms, kill old terminals on -old (phase 2), return to start_ws. Old tmux sessions stay alive across the cutover because their old client survives until the new client has attached.

title: preview show: dock window beside launching frame by image shape
description: Claude/ad-hoc 'preview show' windows tab into whatever i3 container has focus. Dock the preview beside the original (launching) frame instead, tiled (never floating), with split direction chosen by image aspect: landscape->split above (top), portrait->split left, square (~0.8-1.25 w/h)->split below, unless the frame is too small to usefully split, then tab. Implemented in the nushell preview wrapper: capture active window at launch, measure image dims (identify/file), i3-msg split+move-to-mark.
close_reason: preview show docks the window beside the launching frame by image shape: landscape->top, portrait->left, square->below (tab if frame<900px). Key insight: reparenting a mapped wry/WebKitGTK window on xrdp's GL-limited Xorg self-closes it (CloseRequested), so the split is set on the frame BEFORE the window maps; top/left reorder by moving the reparent-safe terminal, never the webview. All 3 modes verified live, windows survive.

title: quickshell launcher: PATH scan blind to symlinked executables
description:
quickshell/overlay/shell.qml:103 scans PATH with:

  find {} -maxdepth 1 -executable -type f

find without -L does not follow symlinks, so a symlink is -type l and never matches -type f. Every rotz-linked binary in ~/.local/bin is therefore invisible to the mod+d launcher.

Confirmed missing: preview, project-picker, akm, akm-root, beads, specify, solaar, ksnip, cleanup-thins, tmux-message, tmux-session-cleaner, tmux-to-workstation, tmux-ssh, tmux-cleanup, tmux-start, tmux-project, ws-switch.nu, repos-health.nu, wm-launch-terminal, ff.

Real binaries (bd, st, preview-d, preview-wv, qs-stats-daemon) show fine, which is why this went unnoticed -- the launcher looked like it worked.

Fix: add -L so symlinks resolve to their targets. Broken symlinks still excluded (stat fails, -type f cannot match). Surfaced while wiring firefox ff (dotfiles-dc1).
notes: Fixed in 144f804: added -L to the find in quickshell/overlay/shell.qml:103. Verified empirically before/after -- 'find ~/.local/bin -maxdepth 1 -executable -type f' returned only real binaries; with -L it additionally returns preview, project-picker, solaar, tmux-*, ff and the rest of the symlinked set. qs-overlay.sh runs quickshell straight from $HOME/.dotfiles/quickshell/overlay, so the repo edit is what a restarted instance loads (the ~/.config/quickshell-overlay rotz link is not the path in use).
close_reason: Fixed in 144f804 (pushed): added -L to the PATH scan in quickshell/overlay/shell.qml:103. ff and the other ~20 symlinked bins in ~/.local/bin now appear in mod+d. Verified before/after with the exact find command. User opted to keep the PATH-scan design rather than redesign the launcher around .desktop entries; CLI-noise tradeoff tracked separately.
