title: d2-router serves 404 for a .d2 file created after the daemon started (stale route index)
description:
d2-router builds its route caches once behind 'routeOnce sync.Once' (d2/router/proxy.go: buildRoutes initialises both route caches on first call) and never re-walks. A .d2 file created after the daemon started therefore RESOLVES but does not ROUTE:

  GET /api/resolve?path=/home/jan/.dotfiles/realtest.d2
    -> 200 {"project":"dotfiles","file":"realtest.d2","url":"http://127.0.0.1:4800/dotfiles/realtest.d2"}
  GET /dotfiles/realtest.d2
    -> 404

Verified 2026-07-17. Killing d2-router-d and restarting it makes the same URL return 200 — confirming a stale index, not a bad path.

Consequence, and it is worse after adr0009: preview-d now hands the browser d2-router's resolved URL directly, so /api/resolve promising a URL that 404s is a user-visible broken preview for any newly-created .d2. Creating a new diagram and previewing it is a completely ordinary workflow.

Also note 'd2-router stop' does not stop a preview-d-lazy-spawned daemon: it reports 'no pidfile — daemon may not have been started with this wrapper' and leaves the process running, so the obvious recovery does not work either. That may deserve its own issue.

Options: re-walk on resolve miss; watch the tree (fsnotify); invalidate the cache on a resolve for an unindexed path; or have /api/resolve refuse to return a URL it cannot route (fail honestly rather than hand out a 404 URL).

Refs: d2/router/proxy.go (routeOnce/buildRoutes), d2/router/registry.go, nushell/actions/d2-router (main-stop), ft002, adr0009.
notes:
Additional evidence 2026-07-17 — the SAME root cause bites from the opposite direction: the index tracks neither file CREATION nor file DELETION.

Deleted /home/jan/.dotfiles/realtest.d2, and d2-router's child for it kept running and stayed REGISTERED. From GET /api/status:
  {"project":"dotfiles","file":"realtest.d2","port":4801,"pid":2595553,"clients":0,"lastCompile":"2026-07-17T13:20:40Z","lastError":null}
The file does not exist; the child is alive, holds port 4801, has 0 clients, and reports lastError: null. d2-router itself stays healthy (/api/status 200), so nothing is wedged — but children are never reaped when their source disappears OR when their last client goes away (both children show clients:0 after the webview died).

So this issue is not just 'new files 404'. The registry is built once behind routeOnce and never reconciled with the filesystem in either direction: additions are invisible (resolve hands out a URL that 404s) and deletions leak a child process + a port. Fixing only the resolve-miss path would leave the leak.

Suggests the fix should be a reconciliation pass rather than a one-shot cache invalidation — e.g. re-walk on resolve miss AND reap children whose source is gone / whose client count has been 0 for some interval. Worth checking d2/router/children.go's ChildManager for any existing reap path before designing one.
close_reason:
Fixed both directions of the stale-index bug in d2/router, verified live on the daemon.

CREATE-SIDE (the titular 404): proxy.go now lazily re-walks a project on a routing miss. The route set moved from a frozen sync.Once cache to a copy-on-write atomic.Pointer[routeCache] — readers Load() lock-free, rebuilds serialize on walkMu and Store() a fresh snapshot. On a miss, lookupRoute re-walks the project the path names (walkProjectFiles), swaps in a cache that keeps every OTHER project's routes verbatim and replaces only this project's, then retries once. Debounced (rewalkDebounce, 500ms default, a test-tunable var) so a flood of 404s for a genuinely-absent file can't spin the walker. NewProxyHandler gained a reg param (nil-safe: a nil registry just means a miss can't be reconciled — used by the fixed-index unit tests).

Verified live: reproduced the exact issue scenario (create t1o-live.d2 after daemon start), resolve returned 200 with a URL, and routing that URL — which returned 404 before — now returns 200.

DELETE-SIDE (the child+port leak): children.go reaper now stats each child's AbsPath and reaps a child whose source .d2 was deleted regardless of clients or idleness. The old rule (Clients==0 && idle>timeout) left a deleted file's child alive for as long as a browser held the /watch socket — the live-preview socket after adr0009. os.Stat under e.mu; only os.ErrNotExist counts (a transient stat error is not a deletion). Log line names the reason ('source file deleted' vs 'idle').

Verified live: a throwaway daemon (D2_ROUTER_IDLE=2s) spawned a child for a file, the file was deleted, and the reaper logged 'children: reaper: "dotfiles/t1o-del.d2" source file deleted — stopping'; child list went to []. Unit test isolates the delete path rigorously (active client + 30m idle, so ONLY deletion can reap).

TDD: 5 tests, each watched RED first. Create-side: reindex-after-create (mutation caught by my own baseline request poisoning the debounce — real bug in the TEST, fixed by disabling debounce for that case), genuinely-missing-stays-404, debounce-throttles. Delete-side: reaps-deleted-file, keeps-present-file (guards against over-eager reaping killing live previews). Full d2router suite green under -race, vet clean.

KNOWN RESIDUALS (not fixed — scope was create-404 + child-leak, both done; noted for follow-up if they bite):
1. The index HTML landing page (newIndexHandler over the shared idx) is NOT updated by the re-walk — only the ProxyHandler's route cache is. A newly-created file routes correctly but won't appear in the '/' listing until restart. Cosmetic.
2. Route cache retains a DELETED file's route until the next miss-triggered re-walk of that project (a hit never re-walks). Re-requesting a just-deleted route can re-spawn a child that the reaper then kills — a slow flap, not a leak. Wiring route-drop into the reap would couple ChildManager to ProxyHandler; deferred.
3. /api/resolve still returns a URL for a path under a project root without checking existence (unchanged). Now self-correcting: routing re-walks and 404s honestly for a truly-absent file.

Refs: d2/router/proxy.go (routeCache/rewalkProject/lookupRoute), d2/router/children.go (reapWithTimeout), d2/router/main.go, adr0009.

title: preview window opens on the active workspace, not next to the nvim it previews for
description:
'preview window N' spawns preview-wv wherever the WM focus happens to be, so the preview lands on whatever workspace the spawning shell was on rather than beside the editor it is previewing for.

Observed repeatedly 2026-07-17: spawns from an agent shell landed the window on ws 'promotool' while the user was working on ws 'dotfiles', and later vice-versa — the window is invisible until the user hunts for it or moves it manually ('i3-msg [class=preview-wv] move workspace current').

Expected: a preview window should open next to / on the same workspace as the nvim instance it is bound to, since that is the editor whose cursor drives it (sp009 per-slot nvim registration already binds slot -> nvim addr).

Design notes / open questions:
- preview-d already knows the slot -> nvim address binding (SlotManager.NvimAddr, sp009 Task 1) — the association exists, it just is not used for placement.
- Placement is a WM concern: i3/sway assign rules match on WM_CLASS (preview-wv), which is identical for every slot, so a static config rule cannot distinguish slot 1 from slot 2.
- Options: have the wrapper resolve the target workspace from the bound nvim (e.g. via i3-msg query for the window holding that nvim) and spawn with an explicit 'i3-msg [con_id=...] mark' / 'move to workspace <ws>'; or set a per-slot X property / WM_NAME the WM rules can match; or have the spawning nvim pass its own workspace to 'preview window'.
- Cross-WM: i3 and sway both apply here (see i3/config + sway/config.d/default sp013 rules); Termux/proot has no WM.

Refs: nushell/actions/preview (window-spawn), nvim/plugins/preview.lua, i3/config sp013 rules, sway/config.d/default, sp013, sp009 Task 1.
close_reason:
Fixed: preview windows now open on the workspace their nvim registered from, not wherever the spawning shell was focused.

Design (user-chosen from three options): capture the workspace at REGISTER time, since that is the one moment the answer is knowable — :PreviewStart runs from inside nvim, so the focused workspace IS nvim's. A later 'preview window N' runs in a different process and cannot work it out: nvim sits under tmux, which severs the pid chain from the terminal window the WM knows. The tempting shortcut (tmux session 'dotfiles_4' -> workspace 'dotfiles') was rejected — 'local_0' exists with no matching workspace, so the convention is not total.

Implementation:
- slot gains a workspace field + SetWorkspace/Workspace (preview/session.go); SlotNumbers() sorted so GET /slots is stable.
- POST /register accepts an optional "workspace" (preview/server.go). Optional by design: a caller with no WM (Termux) records nothing.
- NEW GET /slots serves slot metadata — the read-back path, since 'preview window N' is a different process from the nvim that registered.
- Wrapper (nushell/actions/preview): current-workspace queries the WM; main-register sends it; place-window moves the window after spawn, matching on class AND per-slot title (every slot shares WM_CLASS preview-wv, so class alone would drag every preview window along).
- I3SOCK is unset for every WM query (per the quickshell-session-model memory): a stale inherited I3SOCK points at another session's WM and would place the window on the wrong screen. A test asserts no leak.
- Empty workspace = leave placement to the WM. Never invent one.

TWO BUGS CAUGHT DURING VERIFICATION, both from testing reality rather than trusting green tests:
1. First live test was CONFOUNDED — focused ws happened to equal the recorded ws, so the window would have landed correctly by default. Proved nothing. Re-tested with recorded (dotfiles) != focused (promotool).
2. That discriminating test FAILED: the window is spawned detached and is not mapped yet when place-window fires, so i3 matched nothing — and i3-msg EXITS 0 while reporting {"success": false, "error": "Nothing to move"} in the body. Trusting the exit code silently no-ops. Fixed by polling on the body's success flag (20 x 150ms ~ 3s, bounded). The unit test could not catch this because the fake i3-msg only exited 0; it now returns i3's real success body.

VERIFIED live on i3: focused=promotool, slot 2 recorded=dotfiles, window landed on dotfiles. preview suite green, vet clean, wrapper suite 21/21 (new tests 20/21; mutation-tested by removing place-window -> test 21 fails).

KNOWN CAVEAT (accepted in the chosen design): the workspace goes stale if nvim is moved to another workspace after registering. Re-running :PreviewStart re-registers and corrects it — SetWorkspace overwrites.

title: rotz install preview-webview exits 0 and claims success when cp fails ETXTBSY on a running preview-wv
description:
preview-webview/dot.yaml copies the freshly built binary with a plain 'cp "$SRC/target/release/preview-wv" ~/.local/bin/preview-wv'. If a preview-wv window is currently RUNNING, the kernel refuses to overwrite the executing image:

  cp: cannot create regular file '/home/jan/.local/bin/preview-wv': Text file busy

Verified 2026-07-17 with window 1 open (pid 2376353): rotz TRUE exit code is 0 (checked without a pipeline masking it), the dot still prints 'preview-webview: built ~/.local/bin/preview-wv from ...', and cargo reports 'Finished release'. The ONLY signal is a cp line on stderr among build noise.

Consequence: 'rotz install preview-webview' after a code change, with a preview window open, SILENTLY leaves the OLD binary in place while reporting success. The user believes they are running new code. Same silent-success class as dotfiles-joz.

NOT the same as dotfiles-nqx: a fresh host has no preview-wv running, so ETXTBSY never fires there. This bites re-installs / iterative development on a host with a window open.

FIX (standard ETXTBSY workaround — rename is allowed on a busy inode, overwrite is not):
  cp "$SRC/target/release/preview-wv" ~/.local/bin/preview-wv.new
  mv -f ~/.local/bin/preview-wv.new ~/.local/bin/preview-wv
mv() is an atomic rename: it unlinks the old inode (the running process keeps its open image and keeps working until it exits) and installs the new one. Also consider 'set -e' or an explicit '|| exit 1' in the cmd so a failed copy actually fails the dot rather than exiting 0 — worth checking whether rotz propagates a nonzero installs.cmd at all, since that would be a broader rotz-wide reporting gap affecting every dot.

Sibling preview/dot.yaml:25 is NOT affected — 'go build -C "$SRC" -o ~/.local/bin/preview-d' has the same hazard in principle, but go writes via a temp+rename internally; worth a confirm run with preview-d live before assuming it is safe.

Refs: preview-webview/dot.yaml (cp line), preview/dot.yaml:25, sp013 T1.
close_reason: Fixed in preview-webview/dot.yaml: plain cp replaced with cp to preview-wv.new + mv -f atomic rename, plus '|| exit 1' on cargo build / cp / mv so a failure can no longer fall through to the 'built' success echo. Verified against the exact failing case — rebuild forced with window 1 still running (pid 2376353): inode changed 1428532 -> 1428545 (real new binary installed), zero 'Text file busy', no leftover .new, and the running process survived on its old inode as rename(2) guarantees. CAVEAT left open: whether rotz itself propagates a nonzero installs.cmd exit is still untested — the '|| exit 1' provably stops the false success message, which was the silent-success core, but a repo-wide rotz reporting gap may still exist and would affect every dot. Not filed separately; re-open or file if it bites.

title: d2 preview renders blank: /d2embed proxies the document but not its root-relative assets
description:
Sending a .d2 file to a preview window renders a BLANK iframe. User-confirmed visually 2026-07-17 (xrdp, preview-wv window 1, alpha.d2 fixture); no error shown in the window.

REPRO:
  preview start
  preview window 1
  preview send ~/.dotfiles/d2/router/fixtures/walk/alpha.d2   # -> blank iframe

ROOT CAUSE — asset origin, not frame headers.
/file/<path> emits an iframe whose src is preview-d's own /d2embed/<path> (proxy.go:421). handleD2Embed reverse-proxies exactly ONE top-level d2-router document via proxyAndStripFrameHeaders (proxy.go:227) and correctly returns the genuine 'd2 --watch' page (200, frame headers stripped — verified, NOT the renderBackendUnavailable fallback).

But that document requests its assets at ROOT-RELATIVE paths:
  <script src="/dotfiles/alpha.d2/static/watch.js">
  <link href="/dotfiles/alpha.d2/static/watch.css">
Inside the iframe these resolve against preview-d (:4200), which does not serve them:

  http://localhost:4200/dotfiles/alpha.d2/static/watch.js   -> 404 (19 bytes)
  http://localhost:4200/dotfiles/alpha.d2/static/watch.css  -> 404 (19 bytes)
  http://127.0.0.1:4800/dotfiles/alpha.d2/static/watch.js   -> 200 (2651 bytes)
  http://127.0.0.1:4800/dotfiles/alpha.d2/static/watch.css  -> 200 (206 bytes)

watch.js never loads -> nothing populates <div id="d2-svg-container"> -> blank. Silent because #d2-err is driven by the same script that never loaded. Lazy-spawn of d2-router works fine (d2-router-d came up on :4800 unprompted) — the daemon plumbing is not at fault.

DESIGN TENSION — the fix is a decision, not a patch.
proxy.go:125-127 states the single-document scope is DELIBERATE: 'This is deliberately NOT a general path-prefix reverse proxy of d2-router's whole asset/websocket surface (poc006's explicit recommendation) — just this one top-level document.' That recommendation is precisely what makes the embed inert: the document alone cannot render. poc006 defended against d2-router's proxied watch page emitting frame-blocking headers (which in practice it does NOT — verified 200 with no X-Frame-Options/CSP) and the real failure came from the asset origin instead.

Options (need a call, ideally revisiting poc006's reasoning):
 1. Scoped passthrough for /dotfiles/<name>/static/* -> d2-router, narrower than a full reverse proxy but wider than today.
 2. Rewrite asset URLs in the proxied document to absolute http://127.0.0.1:4800/... (cross-origin from the iframe; check d2-router CORS + whether watch.js's websocket also needs it).
 3. Accept poc006's scope and drop the d2 embed until d2-router can emit relative/configurable asset roots.
Note watch.js implies a live-reload WEBSOCKET — any fix must decide whether --watch reload is in scope or whether a static SVG render is the better embed.

PRE-EXISTING: preview/proxy.go is sp008 Task 7 code, untouched by the sp013 sync — this was broken before sp013 landed and is not a regression from it.

Refs: preview/proxy.go:112-127 (scope comment), :227 proxyAndStripFrameHeaders, :421 renderD2Embed; nushell/actions/d2-router; docs/notes/lab/poc006.md; ft002 (d2-router), ft005 (preview-d).
close_reason:
Fixed by adr0009 (supersedes adr0006): preview-d now embeds d2-router as a DIRECT cross-origin iframe at its resolved URL, exactly as renderAkmEmbed already did for akm-graph. /d2embed/, proxyAndStripFrameHeaders and the frame-header machinery are deleted — net 195 lines removed.

Root cause was deeper than the asset 404 first filed here. 'd2 --watch' serves an empty #d2-svg-container and pushes the SVG over a websocket watch.js opens at root-relative /{project}/{file}/watch, beside root-relative /{project}/{file}/static/* assets. A document-only proxy stranded BOTH on preview-d's origin. A scoped static passthrough (built first, then deleted) fixed watch.js 404->200 but could never work: the socket was still 404. Only d2-router's own origin can serve all three.

adr0006's premise was disproved, not worked around: d2-router at v0.7.1 — the SAME version poc006 probed — emits no X-Frame-Options and no CSP frame-ancestors (verified directly against :4800, not through the stripping proxy). The proxy defended against a header that does not exist while breaking the feature it enabled.

VERIFIED end-to-end by the user: a real 219-byte diagram renders in the preview window, and appending to the file live-reloads the diagram with no resend. Websocket confirmed ESTAB from WebKitNetworkPr to d2-router:4800. preview suite + d2router suite green, vet clean.

TESTING CAVEAT worth remembering: every earlier 'blank' observation was against d2/router/fixtures/walk/*.d2, which are 0-BYTE files (they exist to test d2-router's walk/discovery, not rendering). An empty .d2 renders blank no matter what — d2 emits no SVG file at all for one. The bug was real and independently proven at the HTTP layer, but the verification was worthless until a non-empty file was used. User caught this, not me.

title: preview wrapper spawns preview-wv without LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE on xrdp
description:
nushell/actions/preview:264 spawns preview-wv via 'bash -c PREVIEW_PORT=... nohup preview-wv ...' inheriting the caller's env verbatim (comment at :253-255 says DISPLAY/XAUTHORITY carry through automatically). Nothing forces software GL.

Risk: on an xrdp session (virtual Xorg :10, no real GPU) a client forcing hardware GL through mesa wedges xorgxrdp's x264 encoder — documented for servo/verso in the xrdp-servo-wedges-h264-encoder memory (symptom chain: encoder corrupts -> phone RDP client crashes -> stale session on :10 -> next connect fails 0x104). preview-wv is wry/WebKitGTK, same class of GL-forcing client, so the same wedge is PLAUSIBLE but NOT yet confirmed — it has never been run on this host (build blocked by dotfiles-nqx, webkit2gtk-4.1 missing).

Verify before fixing: build preview-wv, launch it in an xrdp session WITHOUT the guard, and see whether the encoder actually wedges. If it does, force software GL at the wrapper spawn (single chokepoint — every caller routes through it per adr0003), gated on an xrdp/virtual-Xorg detection rather than unconditionally, so native :0 keeps hardware GL. Candidate env: LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1 and/or WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1.

Blocked on dotfiles-nqx (cannot build to test).
close_reason:
Fixed: preview-wv now self-detects a GPU-less xorgxrdp display and forces software GL before gtk::init(), so no spawn path can drive hardware GL on the virtual Xorg. Native :0 (real GPU) is left on hardware GL.

Approach: fixed in the BINARY (preview-webview/src/main.rs), not the wrapper — the binary already sets GDK_BACKEND=x11 before gtk::init() (direct precedent), and doing GL there covers every spawn path (wrapper, nvim, direct) in one place. It also avoided a live conflict: a concurrent session has ~156 lines of uncommitted bfk docking work in nushell/actions/preview, the file the original fix would have touched.

Detection (is_xrdp_display): parse the display number from :10.0, scan /proc for a running Xorg whose args carry this display's EXACT token (":N") AND mention xrdp. Deliberately PER-DISPLAY: a desktop runs native :0 (GPU) alongside xrdp :10 on the same host (quickshell-session-model memory), so a global /dev/dri check would wrongly disable GPU on :0. On match, set LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1 + WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1 via set_env_if_unset (an explicit caller override still wins). Logged ('xorgxrdp display :10.0 — forcing software GL') since a runtime setenv is invisible in /proc/<pid>/environ.

Did NOT confirm the wedge with an unguarded run (the issue's open question) — deliberately, it risks the live xrdp session, and it is unnecessary: on a GPU-less virtual Xorg there is no hardware GL to lose, so forcing software GL is correct whether or not the x264-encoder wedge is real. The guard is gated on detection so it costs native GPU sessions nothing. The wedge remains formally unconfirmed; the fix is sound either way. If a detection MISS ever occurs, it degrades to the pre-fix status quo (no guard), never worse.

TDD: 5 detection tests RED-first (18 total, was 13) — exact-token match, the :0-native-vs-:10-xrdp coexist case, empty/remote DISPLAY, and :10-must-not-match-:100. VERIFIED live on xrdp :10: spawned with the GL vars explicitly unset, the binary logged the guard line, reached the event loop, and the session stayed healthy (daemon 200) — the guard auto-applies with no manual env and no wedge. cargo test 18/18, clippy + fmt clean.

Refs: preview-webview/src/main.rs (is_xrdp_display/display_number/running_xorg_cmdlines/set_env_if_unset + run_window guard), xrdp-servo-wedges-h264-encoder memory.

title: preview-webview/dot.yaml missing webkit2gtk-4.1 system dep
description: rotz install preview-webview fails on a host without webkit2gtk-4.1: cargo build dies at javascriptcore-rs-sys with 'Package javascriptcoregtk-4.1 was not found in the pkg-config search path'. The dot guards only on the cargo toolchain and never installs the wry/WebKitGTK system libs, so a fresh Arch host cannot build preview-wv from the dot alone. Fix: add an installs.depends or distro-branched pacman line (extra/webkit2gtk-4.1, plus whatever else wry/tao pull in — libsoup3, gtk3) to preview-webview/dot.yaml, mirroring how other dots branch on whoami.distro. Repro on this machine (Arch, cargo present, webkit2gtk-4.1 absent) 2026-07-17 after syncing sp013.
close_reason: Fixed in preview-webview/dot.yaml: pkg-config --exists javascriptcoregtk-4.1 guard installs webkit2gtk-4.1 via pacman when absent, with an actionable stderr message on non-pacman distros. Dep set verified by tracing every .pc the crates need to its owning package — all are webkit2gtk-4.1 or transitive deps of it (gtk3/libsoup3/glib2/cairo/pango), so one package is sufficient and correct on a minimal host, not just on this desktop. Runtime pacman guard chosen over three identical whoami.distro branches (same package name on Arch/Manjaro/Arch ARM; no (or ...) helper precedent in the repo; matches this dot's existing runtime 'command -v cargo' idiom). Verified: rotz install preview-webview re-run, guard short-circuited on this host, build finished, binary intact.

title: preview-wv dies silently on xrdp without software-GL env (WebKitGTK hardware GL on virtual Xorg)
description:
Demo on xrdp :10.0 (sp013): first preview-wv window rendered, then died silently on redraw — wv-1.log shows only 'libEGL warning: DRI3 error' (no DRI3 on xorgxrdp). Respawn with LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1 WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1 survived repeated hot-swaps (code + markdown + akm-graph). Same failure class as the servo/xorgxrdp H.264 encoder memory: hardware GL through mesa on the virtual Xorg is unstable.

Fix options: wrapper window-spawn exports the two env vars when session is xrdp-like (no DRI3 / DISPLAY heuristic), or always (costs native :0 webkit compositing perf), or preview-wv sets them itself pre-GTK-init when DRI3 probe fails. Decide + test on native :0 too. Files: nushell/actions/preview (window-spawn env), possibly preview-webview/src/main.rs.
close_reason: Invalid — controlled repro (user hands-off): plain preview-wv on xrdp :10 survived zettel→code→markdown hot-swaps with no env vars, exit path never triggered. All observed 'silent deaths' were the user manually closing windows (clean EXIT=0 via WM_DELETE_WINDOW). libEGL DRI3 warnings are cosmetic on xorgxrdp. LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE not needed for webkit2gtk here.

title: [bug] region screenshot collides across concurrent X sessions (:0 + :10) — shared state, global pkill, unscoped IPC
description:
If a native :0 session and an xrdp :10 session are up at once (the normal case on the desktop machine), the region screenshot tool cross-talks between them. quickshell/qs-session.sh already establishes the correct per-DISPLAY pattern (QS_SID derived from DISPLAY, per-session FIFOs/logs, qs_same_session() environ matching, qs_kill_session() so 'other sessions' matches are left alone'). The region tool ignores all of it.

Three defects:

1. SHARED GRAB POINTER. qs-screenshot.sh:45 writes the grab path to "${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR:-/tmp}/qs-shot-src" and qs-shot-action.sh:15 reads it. XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is per-USER, not per-display — /run/user/1000 is the same file for :0 and :10. Two overlays open => the second grab clobbers the first's path, so w/Esc in one session saves or rm's the OTHER session's grab. Fix: suffix per session, e.g. qs-shot-src.$QS_SID using qs-session.sh's filename-safe id (':0' -> '_0').

2. GLOBAL PKILL. qs-shot-action.sh:17 runs pkill -f 'quickshell -p .*region' with no session scoping — the fallback path kills the other display's overlay. Fix: reuse qs-session.sh's qs_kill_session / qs_same_session environ matching.

3. UNSCOPED IPC TARGET (less certain — verify before fixing). qs-shot-action.sh:8 does Could not open config file at ".../region", selecting the instance by config path only. With one instance per display started from the SAME path, the IPC may reach the wrong session. quickshell has instance selection (-i) — check whether it disambiguates by display on its own before adding scoping.

NOT reproducible on this machine (WSL has no :0) — needs the desktop, where native + xrdp coexist. Defects 1 and 2 are unambiguous from the code regardless.

Context: raised by the user as the ksnip pain point — ksnip's single global instance meant you had to kill a session to capture on the other display. Worth fixing whichever design wins; note the GDK live overlay ([[poc008]]) avoids this structurally (spawn-per-invocation, $DISPLAY inherited, no shared state, no daemon — and [[poc009]] already ruled out the daemon that would have reintroduced it).
acceptance_criteria: With overlays open on :0 and :10 simultaneously: w/Esc in one session acts only on that session's grab; cancelling one leaves the other's overlay running; each session's IPC reaches its own instance; no shared qs-shot-src path between displays
notes:
ABSORBED BY sp012 (epic dotfiles-dqo, /home/jan/.dotfiles/docs/notes/spec/sp012.md): cross-session collisions — DESIGNED OUT (spawn-per-invocation, $DISPLAY inherited, no shared state, no daemon; per-DISPLAY scoping via qs-session.sh QS_SID). Its regression test is Task 2 (dotfiles-dqo.2), which can only fully RUN at Task 8 on the desktop machine — WSL has no :0.

Left OPEN deliberately — the defect still exists until the epic ships. Do not start separate work here; it would duplicate the epic.
close_reason:
FIXED by sp012 and CONFIRMED BY THE USER on the desktop machine (2026-07-16) — the only environment where this regression can be tested at all, since it needs a native :0 and an xrdp :10 live simultaneously.

USER-OBSERVED: overlays open on BOTH :0 and :10 at once; cancelling one left the other alive and untouched. No interference.

This is user evidence, not self-measured — materially stronger than anything else in this epic, where the author of the code also took every measurement. It could not be reproduced by any agent: WSL has no native Xorg :0 (a WSLg X0 socket exists, but that is not the same thing), and the overlay takes a pointer grab that would seize the user's input.

WHY IT IS FIXED — the shared state is gone at the root, not patched:
- The old ${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/qs-shot-src pointer was per-USER, not per-display, so :0 and :10 clobbered each other's grab path. The live design captures AFTER selection, so that handoff file does not exist at all. Nothing shared = nothing to collide.
- The old fallback ran an unscoped 'pkill -f quickshell -p .*region', reaching into the other display's session. The new launcher uses qs_kill_session from quickshell/qs-session.sh, which matches /proc/<pid>/environ against this session's DISPLAY and leaves other sessions alone.
- The old IPC selected the instance by config path only. There is no IPC now — one spawn per keypress, $DISPLAY inherited from the launching session.
Both defects were unambiguous from the code; the fix is structural (spawn-per-invocation, no daemon, no shared state), which is why poc009 killing the warm-daemon idea mattered beyond the blink.

Acceptance criteria: 'cancelling one leaves the other's overlay running' — CONFIRMED by the user. 'no shared qs-shot-src path between displays' — verified in code (the path no longer exists). 'each session's IPC reaches its own instance' — MOOT, there is no IPC.

title: [bug] screenshot overlay maps black for 67-150ms on open — Qt first-frame render after map
description:
MEASURED 2026-07-15 on :10 (WSL xrdp, no compositor). Opening the region overlay blanks the whole screen to black for ~83ms (5 frames @60fps) before the frozen grab appears.

Evidence (ffmpeg x11grab 60fps lossless + signalstats YAVG, harness scratchpad/blink-measure.sh):
  t=1.400  luma 42.51  (baseline desktop)
  t=1.417  luma 16.54  <- black; limited-range Y=16 IS black. Extracted frame f_03 RGB mean = 1.37/255
  t=1.483  luma 16.54  (holds ~83ms)
  t=1.500  luma 42.51  (frozen image finally paints)
  t=1.550..1.650       (dim animation fades 42.5 -> 33.5 over 140ms — working as designed, NOT the blink)
Frame f_03 visually: entire screen black, only the bar's screenshot hint strip + cursor visible.

CAUSE: region/shell.qml:131-134 PanelWindow has color: "#000000" and is visible by default. X maps the window and clears it to the black background pixel immediately; Qt then needs ~83ms to decode the 2560x1440 PNG (Image asynchronous: false, cache: false) and render the first frame. The window is on screen, black, for that whole gap. The QML comment at :59-61 claims 'the unmap itself is invisible — no blink' — that reasoning covers CLOSE only; open was never addressed.

Distinct from dotfiles-ed3 (exit expose storm, Firefox white repaint) — different mechanism, different layer, fixable without sudo or an Xorg restart.

FIX DIRECTION: do not map the window until the scene is ready. PanelWindow { visible: false } + flip visible=true once the Image reports Image.Ready (and ideally after one frame is rendered). Verify the decode really completes before the map — if asynchronous:false already decodes at component-completion, the win is only ~1 frame and the real cost is elsewhere (quickshell/QML startup), which the same trace can tell us. Consider also a cheaper-to-decode intermediate (raw/BMP/JPEG) if PNG decode dominates the 83ms.
acceptance_criteria: Re-running scratchpad/blink-measure.sh shows no frame with RGB mean below ~50% of desktop baseline between mode-enter and the frozen image appearing; any residual black is <=1 frame (~16ms); the dim fade-in still animates from the undimmed frozen shot; region select and w/Esc paths unchanged
notes:
ABSORBED BY sp012 (epic dotfiles-dqo, /home/jan/.dotfiles/docs/notes/spec/sp012.md): open blink — DISSOLVES under the live design (no opaque fullscreen map ever occurs). Verified closed at Task 7 (dotfiles-dqo.7), not before.

Left OPEN deliberately — the defect still exists until the epic ships. Do not start separate work here; it would duplicate the epic.
close_reason:
FIXED by sp012 (epic dotfiles-dqo) — the blink does not get fixed, it stops existing: no opaque fullscreen window ever maps.

VERIFIED by T7 (dotfiles-dqo.7) against the MERGED system on live :10, via the committed harness quickshell/test-blink-measure.sh driving the real bind path (not a scratch harness):
  run1 black_frames=0  baseline=47.92  min=47.15  frames=479
  run2 black_frames=0  baseline=47.90  min=47.25  frames=479
  run3 black_frames=0  baseline=47.90  min=39.84  frames=479
  run4 black_frames=0  baseline=47.89  min=47.26  frames=480
4/4 runs, not a median — the acceptance criterion demanded every run, because the old design's count varied 3-7 run-to-run. ~60 frames/s sustained (479-480 over 8s), so no dropped frames faking a gap.

POSITIVE CONTROL: all 4 runs produced a real 1920x1200 capture, so the flow genuinely ran rather than silently no-opping. That control matters — two runs falsely passed during poc008/poc009 for exactly that reason.

Was: the whole screen went black 67-150ms (RGB mean 1.37/255) per open. Cause was the opaque fullscreen PanelWindow map (X clears to the black background pixel) plus Qt first-paint of a 2560x1440 image on the software backend — both paid per-open regardless of process age, which is why [[poc009]]'s warm-instance approach could not have helped.

Latency for reference: 251-277ms from launcher exec to the overlay window being discoverable. The old design spent ~200ms in scrot alone BEFORE quickshell even started, then blinked.

title: [bug] region screenshot bind unreachable on WSL/xrdp — mode+bind stranded in native.conf overlay
description: i3/config (base) carries the for_window [title=qs-region] float rule (line 220) and a stale comment reserving $mod+Shift+s, but the mode "screenshot" block and the $mod+Shift+s bind live in i3/config.d/native.conf. WSL/xrdp machines link config.d/wsl.conf instead, so the key is unbound there — i3-msg -t get_config shows no screenshot mode. All runtime deps are present on WSL (quickshell running, scrot/xclip/magick installed, XRDP_SESSION set so qs-screenshot.sh forces QT_QUICK_BACKEND=software). Layering contract says base owns keybinds/modes (it already has bindsym Print exec i3-scrot); overlays own autostart execs only. Fix: move mode + bind into i3/config base, drop from native.conf. config.common (proot variant) stays out — meta-proot ships no quickshell.
acceptance_criteria: i3-msg -t get_config on WSL/xrdp shows mode "screenshot" and the $mod+Shift+s bind; $mod+Shift+s opens the region overlay on :10; native.conf no longer defines the mode; no duplicate-keybinding nagbar on either platform
close_reason:
Moved mode "screenshot" + $mod+Shift+s bind from i3/config.d/native.conf into i3/config base; corrected the stale line-150 comment (said the key was reserved for ksnip). Commit 08cd2cf.

Verified on this machine (WSL, i3 over xrdp :10, wsl.conf overlay):
- i3 -C -c ~/.i3/config → exit 0, no duplicate-keybinding warnings
- i3-msg reload; i3-msg -t get_config → mode "screenshot" + $mod+Shift+s bind now present (were absent before)
- Drove the flow via i3-msg (inherits session env): overlay spawned (quickshell -p .../region), binding_state = screenshot, frozen grab written to /run/user/1000/qs-shot-src
- qs-shot-action.sh whole → exit 0, file saved to ~/Pictures/screenshots/shot_20260715-124614.png, clipboard = that path, overlay quit, binding_state back to default, tmp grab + pointer file cleaned up
- PNG inspected: 2560x1440, mean 0.134 / stddev 0.122, visually confirmed real desktop content (not the black-frame no-compositor failure)

Note: dotfiles-ux1 (IPC exitMode not landing) did NOT reproduce in this run — mode reset cleanly on the IPC happy path.
Discovered separately: dotfiles-d7v — bar's mode hint strip is baked into the frozen grab (mode fires before scrot).

title: viewer polish: highlight on label only, dot keeps type color (sp011 follow-up)
design: User revision after Task 4: no gold dot color/size boost; label pill emphasis only; dot escapes isolate dim while current. Shipped directly, smoke green.
close_reason: Shipped in commit on main: nodeRGBA keeps type color (isHighlighted only exempts dot from isolate dim); nodeSize boost removed; dead consts + stale comments cleaned; smoke HIGHLIGHT_OK + STANDALONE_OK green; daemon rebuilt + respawned

title: Enable X BackingStore for xrdp Xorg (exit-blink fix, needs sudo)
description: Measured on :10 (no compositor): screenshot-overlay exit has a 2-frame dark dip at unmap = expose storm (no backing store, every underlying window repaints). Fix: sudo sed -i '/Driver "xrdpdev"/a\    Option "BackingStore" "True"' /etc/X11/xrdp/xorg.conf, then restart Xorg :10 and verify via xdpyinfo backing-store state + re-run frame-luma measurement. If it works, consider same for :0 native (picom would then only provide inactive-dim). Also consider managing /etc/X11/xrdp/xorg.conf via xrdp/dot.yaml installs.cmd.
notes:
ABSORBED BY sp012 (epic dotfiles-dqo, /home/jan/.dotfiles/docs/notes/spec/sp012.md): exit expose storm — NOT assumed fixed. Task 7 (dotfiles-dqo.7) re-measures it. It should largely dissolve (no fullscreen unmap to trigger the storm) but it is CONTENT-DEPENDENT: it only flashes when an underlying app repaints slowly, so a clean run proves nothing. Must be measured under two underliers (Firefox = the known-bad one that produced the 42->76-80 spike, plus a plain terminal). The BackingStore fix proposed in this issue may still be a no-op — clients must request backing store; verify before implementing.

Left OPEN deliberately — the defect still exists until the epic ships. Do not start separate work here; it would duplicate the epic.
close_reason:
MOOT — the exit blink is gone, but NOT via BackingStore. sp012's live overlay removed the mechanism: there is no fullscreen window to unmap, so no expose storm to trigger. The BackingStore work this issue proposed was never needed and should not be attempted.

VERIFIED by T7 (dotfiles-dqo.7) on live :10, measured NOT assumed — and specifically under the KNOWN-BAD underlier this issue's own notes named, because the storm is content-dependent and a clean run proves nothing on its own:

  Underlier = 8x st terminals:
    run1-4  post-exit max == baseline (-0.1% .. +0.0%)  no spike
  Underlier = FIREFOX, drag directly over its window (962,30 952x1137) — the app
  that produced the ORIGINAL 42 -> 76-80 luma spike:
    ff-run5  baseline=49.59  post-exit max=49.64  (+0.1%)  no storm
    ff-run6  baseline=49.07  post-exit max=49.74  (+1.4%)  no storm

Old design: +80% luma spike (Firefox repainting white on expose). New: +0.1%/+1.4%.

CAVEAT recorded honestly: the BackingStore hypothesis itself was never tested and is now untestable-by-irrelevance. My earlier note on this issue flagged that Option "BackingStore" "True" may be a no-op anyway (clients must request backing store via XSetWindowAttributes; GTK/Qt/Firefox generally leave it NotUseful). That remains unproven — but nothing needs it now. If a future design reintroduces a fullscreen opaque overlay on a compositor-free X server, re-open this rather than assuming BackingStore would save it.

Also corrected on this issue earlier: the original description called the exit a '2-frame dark dip'. It was measured to be a BRIGHT flash (Firefox clearing white). The dark-dip reading was likely contaminated by a stale picom instance running pre-935e568 config.

title: [bug] region overlay exitMode not landing — i3 mode stuck after IPC close
description: After IPC-driven cancel/whole on :10, execDetached('i3-msg mode default') at quit never takes effect even with a 60ms finalQuit delay — i3 stays in the screenshot mode until something else resets it. Esc/w fallback path resets mode fine; the IPC happy-path does not. Investigate: env of detached child (I3SOCK/SWAYSOCK/wmMsg pick), whether execDetached spawn survives teardown, or move mode reset into qs-shot-action.sh after the ipc call returns.
close_reason: MOOT — sp012 removed the mechanism: region/shell.qml + qs-shot-action.sh deleted, qs-region.py holds the grab and qs-screenshot.sh resets the mode after it exits. Verified live on :10 (2026-07-16): drag capture → selector exits → binding state back to default.

title: [bug] focus border drawn over screenshot overlay + drag flicker
description: Focus frame stayed visible on top of the qs region-screenshot overlay and restacked on every 100ms mouse-drag poll (screen glitching). Root cause: qs-focus-border.py refreshed on ANY i3 mode event (enter included) and every binding/mouse-poll refresh redrew the keep-above border above the dock layer; shell.qml also exited the i3 mode 1.4s before the overlay quit, ending any suppression during the toast.
close_reason: Fixed: mode-aware suppression in qs-focus-border.py (hide+block refreshes while i3 mode != default, re-checked in apply_geom for in-flight refreshes); shell.qml exits mode only at quit so suppression covers the toast; ptr file cleaned on mouse finish/cancel. Regression test test_qs_focus_border_mode.py (RED before fix, GREEN after).

title: Discovered: preview-d poster cache has no eviction (unbounded growth)
design: render_video.go's posterCache (package-level map[string]*posterEntry) caches every video file's extracted poster frame keyed by absolute path, invalidated only by mtime/size change on re-request — entries are never evicted, so a long-running preview-d daemon that previews many distinct video files over its lifetime will grow this map unboundedly. Not a concern for typical usage (small personal notes/dotfiles trees) but worth a bounded LRU or periodic sweep if preview-d ever runs long-lived against large video collections. Discovered while implementing sp008 Task 8 (video renderer).
close_reason:
Fixed: the poster cache is now bounded with LRU-by-last-access eviction, so a long-lived preview-d no longer grows posterCache without limit.

Root: render_video.go's posterCache (package-level map[string]*posterEntry keyed by abs path) was invalidated only by mtime/size on re-request and never evicted — one entry per distinct video ever previewed, each holding the poster JPEG bytes. Unbounded over the daemon's lifetime.

Fix: added lastAccess to posterEntry (guarded by posterCacheMu) and evictPosterCacheLocked, which drops least-recently-accessed entries until len <= posterCacheMaxEntries (var, default 128 — a few MB of JPEGs, a hard ceiling for a large collection). posterFor sets lastAccess on every access and, when a new path grows the map, runs eviction AFTER setting lastAccess so the just-created (newest) entry is never the one evicted. Evicting an entry whose per-file mu is held mid-generation is safe: that goroutine keeps its reference and completes; only future lookups miss and regenerate.

Chose entry-count LRU over a byte-budget (simpler, adequate — poster frames are bounded single JPEGs) and over a periodic sweep (eviction on insert is deterministic and needs no goroutine).

TDD: 2 tests RED-first — eviction policy (over cap → oldest evicted, recent survive) and wiring (posterFor keeps the map bounded; eviction runs before generation so it holds with or without ffmpeg). Full preview suite green under -race, vet clean. No live demo: unbounded-growth prevention isn't observable in a single run — the tests are the verification.

Refs: preview/render_video.go (posterEntry.lastAccess, posterCacheMaxEntries, evictPosterCacheLocked, posterFor), sp008 Task 8.

title: AKM: archive/ mirror tree for retired zettels (delivered specs + obsolete product)
description:
Add docs/notes/archive/ mirror tree holding retired zettels. Layout mirrors notes/: archive/spec/ for specs, archive/lab/ for poc, archive/*.md for root types.

MOVE POLICY (active stays; retired moves to archive mirror):
- sp  (spec)   -> archive/spec/  when status=done (delivered)
- us  (story)  -> archive/        when status in {dropped, superseded}  (done STAYS - live behavior)
- adr          -> archive/        when status=Superseded
- ft  (feature)-> archive/        when status in {superseded, deprecated}
- im  (impl)   -> archive/        when status=superseded
- pn  (persona)-> archive/        when status=superseded
- cat          -> never (permanent taxonomy)
- poc (lab)    -> archive/lab/    when status=invalidated (validated STAYS)

WORK:
1. akm init: create archive/, archive/spec/, archive/lab/ mirror dirs
2. akm next_id + find_zettel: union active dir + archive mirror per type (globs currently non-recursive on $s.dir -> would collide ids / miss aliases once files move)
3. backfill: git mv per policy; flip spec footers Index: [[board]] -> [[archive]]
   - archive/spec/: sp001 sp002 sp004 sp005 sp006
   - archive/: us004
   - archive/lab/: poc001 poc002 poc005
4. wire lifecycle skills: spec-retro (spec done) + supersede paths (adr/ft/im/us/pn) git mv into archive on status flip
5. parser: recursive WalkDir already scans subdirs -> archived nodes still render (verify)

Constraint discovered: akm globs docs/notes/spec/sp*.md NON-recursively for id-alloc + alias lookup; moving files out of that reach risks id collisions.
close_reason: archive/ mirror tree shipped: akm CLI id-namespace spans active+archive, work-merge relocates delivered specs, backfill done (sp001/2/4/5/6, us004), graph verified. Product-obsolete supersede wiring deferred to follow-up.

title: akm-graph viewer: persistent node labels (name pills like country-borders demo)
design: User wants persistent on-node labels like the cosmos country-borders demo (name pills + flags), not just hover tooltips. cosmos core has no text labels — build an HTML overlay: a pointer-events-none container of pill divs, one per node, positioned each frame via graph.spaceToScreenPosition(graph.getNodePositionsMap()) in a requestAnimationFrame loop; onZoom keeps them synced. Pill = id or alias text, colored by node type (reuse TYPE_COLORS). No flag images (AKM has none) — the colored pill + type is the equivalent. Optionally hide labels below a zoom threshold to reduce clutter (40 nodes = show all). Keep straight links + small dots.

Verified 38 pills w/ text, 5490 bright text px screenshot; smoke 3 stages green (color 84 buckets); go test green.

title: akm-graph viewer: nodes too big/fuzzy/uniform — crisper smaller sizing

Fix: reduce sizes (MIN 4, BASE 4, MAX ~20, SIZE_K ~1.0) for crisp dots; scaleNodesOnZoom:false so nodes are a fixed screen size (crisper, no ballooning on zoom); drop renderHoveredNodeRing (extra border). Keep degree-based variation so hubs stand out, and keep the spread force layout + curvedLinks from v7l.

title: akm-graph viewer: bigger visible nodes + country-borders-style 2D spread
design: User wants the cosmos country-borders-demo look: clean 2D force-directed network with clearly visible sized nodes and spread-out clusters. Current issues: (1) nodes barely visible — BASE_SIZE=3 too small, worsened after ov8 dropped degrees; (2) layout reads as a tight/blobby cluster ('seems 3D') rather than a spread 2D network. cosmos v1 is 2D-only and has no text labels in core (hover tooltip already present). Fixes: bump node BASE_SIZE/MAX_SIZE + minimum visible size; tune simulation (more repulsion, less gravity, sensible linkDistance) for a demo-like spread; ensure fitViewOnInit; consider curvedLinks + brighter links. Verify via screenshot (node pixels visible + graph fills viewport).

- Nodes: MIN_SIZE 6px floor, BASE 6, MAX 34, SIZE_K 1.6; ghosts 0.6x floored 4px. Every node legible, hubs larger.

title: akm.md doc-placeholder wikilinks create spurious ghost nodes
design:
The 2ry fix added docs/notes/akm.md (AKM model-overview hub) as a node. But akm.md is SCHEMA DOCUMENTATION: its body has illustrative placeholder wikilinks — [[cat###]], [[us###]], [[adr####]], [[id|alias]], [[link]], [[index]], [[tag]], [[sp###|<title>]], etc. The wikilink extractor treats these as real edges, minting 14 spurious ghost nodes (cat, us, adr, ft, im, sp, pn, poc, id, link, index, tag, archive, US001). Ghost count 21 -> 27; akm renders degree-20 surrounded by garbage neighbors.

Fix options:
(a) Include akm.md as a hub NODE but skip extracting its body wikilinks (hub-notes-are-docs rule) — cleanest; akm stays a node, no fake edges.
(b) Filter placeholder targets: drop wikilink targets containing '#' (template stubs like cat###/adr####) and bare-schema tokens.
(c) Only render ghost nodes whose id matches a real zettel id shape (us/sp/im/ft/adr/cat/pn/poc + digits) — drops id/link/index/tag/archive noise but keeps cat/us/adr bare-prefix ghosts.
Recommend (a): treat the akm hub as label-only (node without outbound edges), since its links are documentation, not relations.

Surfaced when opening the graph after dotfiles-2ry landed.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED: fix on branch bd-dotfiles-ov8.0 (commit 788d648).

Root cause: the wikilink extractor turned documentation/nav/placeholder links into ghost nodes. Junk sourced from akm.md (schema placeholders [[cat###]], examples [[link]]/[[id|alias]]/[[tag]]), spec footers ([[archive]]/[[board]]), and template examples in sp004/im005. 14 junk ghosts: cat,us,adr,ft,im,sp,pn,poc,id,link,index,tag,archive,US001.

Fix (graph.go): a ghost must represent a MISSING NOTE, and notes are zettel-shaped (type prefix + digit). isZettelID() reuses nodeTypeFromID (bare 'cat' -> 'note' -> not zettel; 'us999' -> 'us' -> zettel). Links whose target is neither a real note nor zettel-shaped are dropped (no edge, no ghost). Real missing-zettel diagnostics ([[us099]]) still ghost -> AC3 preserved.

Evidence:
- e2e real repo: 67 nodes/27 ghosts -> 40 nodes/0 ghosts; zero junk ghosts remain; akm hub node present, degree 20->6 (real relations only). All 27 former ghosts were doc noise (no genuinely-missing zettels currently).
- TestBuildGraph_DropsNonZettelDanglingLinks: cat/link/tag/archive/id/us dropped (no node), us999 -> ghost, sp001 -> real, akm keeps only zettel/real edges.
- TestBuildGraph_GhostNode retargeted nonexistent-target -> us999 (ghost still tested for the realistic case).
- Golden fixture: poc001 dangling [[nonexistent-target]] -> [[us999]] (ghost) + [[link]] (dropped); regenerated golden ghost list = [us999].
- go test -race -count=1 green; gofmt -l clean; go vet clean.
Left in_progress for review.
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED

Criteria verified:
- Filter logic sound (graph.go:81, isZettelID:43-45 reusing nodeTypeFromID:172): bare 'cat'/'sp'/'us' + words 'link'/'archive'/'tag'/'id' + 'US001' (case-sensitive miss) all -> nodeTypeFromID 'note' -> dropped; 'us999'/'adr0001' (digit follows prefix) -> kept. Filter runs BEFORE edge append and ghosts derive only from links, so non-zettel targets drop BOTH edge and ghost (no dangling edge to a nonexistent node). Extractor confirms [[cat###]] -> 'cat' via '#' section-strip (parser.go:58).
- AC3 (us006) preserved: TestBuildGraph_GhostNode (us999 -> ghost) + TestBuildGraph_DropsNonZettelDanglingLinks (us999 ghost, sp001 real, cat/link/tag/archive/id/us dropped, akm edges limited to zettel/real) both PASS.
- Non-tautology PROVEN: with the graph.go:81 guard removed, TestBuildGraph_DropsNonZettelDanglingLinks FAILS (cat/link/tag/archive/id/us become nodes; akm keeps junk edges) AND TestBuildGraphFromRoot_Fixture FAILS ([[link]] ghost reappears, poc001 degree 5 vs 4). Restored byte-identical (empty git diff HEAD); suite green.
- Golden legitimate: fixture poc001 [[nonexistent-target]] -> [[us999]] (ghost) + [[link]] (dropped); golden ghost list = [us999], no 'link', poc001 degree 4. TestBuildGraphFromRoot_Fixture passes without -update.
- E2E real repo (/home/jan/.dotfiles, fixed binary port 8199): 40 nodes / 189 links / 0 ghosts / 0 junk nodes; akm present as hub (degree 6, status stable). Old still-running binary shows pre-fix 67 nodes/233 links.
- Automated checks: go test -race -count=1 green; gofmt -l clean; go vet clean; no TODO/FIXME/stub in changed files.

Tests: full suite passed (race), 1 new test (DropsNonZettelDanglingLinks, proven non-tautological), 1 retargeted (GhostNode nonexistent-target -> us999), golden regenerated.
Design judgment: 'ghost only for zettel-shaped missing targets' is the right call. AC3's 'links to notes that do not exist' means genuine broken note references (us099), NOT schema placeholders [[cat###]] / syntax examples [[link]] / nav [[archive]] which were never note references. The fix keeps real broken-ref diagnostics visible while dropping doc noise that would otherwise bury them. Within design's contemplated option (c); more general than recommended (a) since it also cleans spec footers + sp004/im005 template examples.
Deviations: none unlogged. Implementer chose drop-non-zettel (design option c, stronger) over recommended option (a) skip-akm-body; documented in notes, superior + general -> acceptable.

title: nvim clipboard yank lost over xrdp i3 (wayland-0 fallback steals X11 session)
description: In the xrdp Xorg :10 i3 session on WSL, nvim yank to + register never reaches the Windows clipboard. Root cause: nvim/config/options.lua picks the wayland-0-socket fallback branch (WaylandSSH) because WSLg always keeps /run/user/UID/wayland-0 alive, even though the active session is X11 (DISPLAY=:10, WAYLAND_DISPLAY unset). Yanks land in the idle WSLg Wayland clipboard, invisible to xrdp-chansrv which bridges the X11 CLIPBOARD to the RDP client. The xclip-x11 branch is both ordered after the wayland fallback AND dead (xclip not installed). Fix: prefer xclip when an X11 DISPLAY exists, demote the wayland-0-socket branch below it; install xclip in X11-WM sessions.
close_reason: Reordered nvim clipboard provider: X11 (xclip) now beats wayland-0-socket fallback; added xclip to i3 X11 installs. Verified provider resolves to xclip-x11 with DISPLAY set + xclip present.

title: WSL_DISTRO_NAME lost under systemd-spawned tmux — rotz WSL detection silently picks native branch
description: rotz install meta-wsl-i3 ran from tmux (systemd → tmux server → nu) where WSL init env is absent, so WSL_DISTRO_NAME was unset. All {{#if env.WSL_DISTRO_NAME}} guards evaluated false: xrdp install skipped (link only), i3 linked native.conf overlay instead of wsl.conf, wsl-wm-switch i3 failed (no xrdp units). Need robust WSL detection for rotz env (e.g. export WSL_DISTRO_NAME fallback in profile from /proc/version or wslinfo --name, or systemd user environment.d) — relates to CLAUDE.md known issue: 4 different WSL detection methods.
close_reason: WSL detection migrated off env.WSL_DISTRO_NAME to /mnt/c eval/runtime checks (see commit dda40e8).

title: Implement ft003 i3-config-layering: config.d split
description: Split monolithic i3/config into shared base + platform overlays (native.conf, wsl.conf) per ft003 contract: base owns keybinds/workspaces/modes/colors, overlays own all exec autostarts. Wire rotz conditional links in i3/dot.yaml, point xrdp/xinitrc at shared config. Validate on WSL xrdp session.
notes: Implemented in d703aaa. Base: keybinds/workspaces/modes/colors + quickshell exec (all platforms). Overlays: native.conf (st, Mod4, picom/nitrogen/tray/xautolock/setxkbmap, 4 nop grabs), wsl.conf (st, Mod1, 2 nop grabs). Include at top of base so overlay $term/$mod resolve before binds. xrdp consumes shared config (exec i3), depends i3+st. i3 -C validates both variants. PENDING live test: fresh xrdp login after rotz install st. Risk accepted by user: qs-start.sh killall quickshell cross-session kill is fine — sway and i3-xrdp sessions never run in parallel by usage. Remaining unknown: quickshell X11-on-llvmpipe rendering over xrdp (fallback QSG_RHI_BACKEND=software).

title: bashrc: skip tmux auto-start inside zellij (web client blank panes)
description: distro/bashrc auto-starts tmux on every interactive bash. Inside a zellij pane this nests tmux in zellij; over the zellij web client it renders blank. Guard the auto-tmux block with [[ -z $ZELLIJ ]] so zellij panes skip tmux. Mirror the tmux->nushell stack by exec'ing nu when in zellij. Other contexts (local terminal -> tmux) unchanged.
close_reason: Added $ZELLIJ guard to distro/bashrc: inside zellij, skip tmux auto-start (was nesting tmux in zellij + double-attaching shared 'local' tmux session → blank web-client panes). Exec nu instead, mirroring tmux→nushell stack. Local terminals unaffected (no $ZELLIJ).

title: Enable zellij web server over LAN + bring config into rotz
description: Existing ~/.config/zellij/config.kdl already has web_server true, port 50050, mkcert TLS cert/key (SANs include LAN IP 10.42.80.11 + 0.0.0.0). Missing web_server_ip so it binds localhost only. Set web_server_ip 0.0.0.0 for LAN access. Then bring zellij config into dotfiles repo with a dot.yaml (links config.kdl, installs zellij + mkcert cert gen). Do NOT track private key in git.
close_reason: config.kdl: set web_server_ip 0.0.0.0 (was localhost-only), switched to stable cert filename zellij-web.pem (regenerated via mkcert with LAN IP SAN). Added zellij/ to repo + dot.yaml (installs zellij+mkcert, per-machine cert gen, symlinks config). Verified listening 0.0.0.0:50050 over HTTPS.

title: Task 4: Create nushell/actions/wm-launch-terminal dispatcher
description: New nu action script. Signature: wm-launch-terminal <term_cmd>. Resolves focused workspace via wm-current-workspace, looks up entry via project lookup, branches: no entry/no ssh → TMUX_TARGET=$(tmux-start start 0 <ws>) <term_cmd>; ssh set → TMUX_TARGET=$(tmux-to-workstation connect --no-attach -g <ws> --cwd <path> -- <ssh>) <term_cmd>. Required by sway/i3 bindsym updates (Tasks 6, 7).
design:
#### type
feature

#### effort
2h

#### depends
- Task 1 (dotfiles-8ai.1) — project lookup
- Task 2 (dotfiles-8ai.2) — tmux-to-workstation --no-attach

#### files_touched
- nushell/actions/wm-launch-terminal

#### success_criteria
- New nu script. Signature: wm-launch-terminal <term_cmd: string>.
- Resolves workspace via wm-current-workspace; looks up via project lookup.
- Local branch (lookup null or no ssh): TMUX_TARGET=$(tmux-start start 0 <ws>) <term_cmd>.
- Remote branch (ssh set): TMUX_TARGET=$(tmux-to-workstation connect --no-attach -g <ws> --cwd <path> -- <ssh>) <term_cmd>.
- Both branches set TMUX_TARGET; bashrc pattern-match (Task 3) handles socket selection.
- Exits with same status as launched <term_cmd>; ssh/tmux-start errors surface visibly, no silent fallback to local.

#### edge_cases
- Workspace name unregistered → lookup null → local branch with that name as session group (today's behavior).
- Workspace numeric-only (1, 2) → wm-current-workspace returns it; lookup null → local branch.
- term_cmd multi-word (e.g. 'wezterm-gui start') → passed unsplit; use ^$term_cmd or equivalent respecting word boundaries.
- Remote path with spaces → quoted through to tmux-to-workstation (existing escape handles).
- tmux-to-workstation --no-attach fails (ssh error) → propagate non-zero, do NOT spawn terminal.
- wm-current-workspace returns empty → fall back to 'local' session name (today's behavior).

#### test_plan
- Manual: register local1→/tmp; rename workspace to local1; wm-launch-terminal foot → terminal with local tmux session, PWD /tmp.
- Manual: register ws→/tmp/myapp --ssh ws; rename workspace to ws; wm-launch-terminal foot → terminal, bashrc matches ^thin_, attaches thin, ssh lands at /tmp/myapp on remote.
- Manual: rename workspace to random-unregistered; dispatch → local branch with that name.
- Manual: multi-word term — wm-launch-terminal 'wezterm-gui start --always-new-process' → terminal launches with all args intact.
- Manual: bogus ssh profile on remote entry → ssh error visible, no orphaned terminal, non-zero exit.
close_reason: AUDITED: APPROVED — merged into main via bd-dotfiles-8ai.<n>.0 (state-persistence bug re-revert; bulk re-close)

title: Task 3: Patch distro/bashrc to honor ^thin_ pattern in TMUX_TARGET
description: Add pattern guard to the existing TMUX_TARGET attach block in distro/bashrc: when value matches ^thin_, attach via tmux -L thin; otherwise default server. Required by Task 4 so wm-launch-terminal's thin id flows through TMUX_TARGET protocol.
design:
#### type
task

#### effort
30m

#### depends
none

#### files_touched
- distro/bashrc

#### success_criteria
- Existing 'if [[ -n $TMUX_TARGET ]]; then tmux attach -t $TMUX_TARGET; fi' block adds pattern check: ^thin_ → tmux -L thin attach; else default.
- Non-thin TMUX_TARGET values keep today's default-server attach behavior (no regression).
- Non-interactive guard at top of bashrc still short-circuits before attach block.
- Unset/empty TMUX_TARGET still falls through to today's workspace-detection path.

#### edge_cases
- TMUX_TARGET=thin (no underscore) → does NOT match ^thin_, default attach (fails loud — acceptable, prefix thin_ is the contract).
- TMUX_TARGET=thin_5:0 (session:window) → matches ^thin_, -L thin attach works.
- TMUX_TARGET=myproject_thin_0:0 (substring not prefix) → does NOT match, default attach. Prefix anchor load-bearing.
- TMUX env (nested tmux) coexists with TMUX_TARGET — attach block stays inside today's nesting guard.

#### test_plan
- Manual: TMUX_TARGET=thin_5 (real thin_5 session) → fresh bash → attaches to thin_5 via -L thin.
- Manual: TMUX_TARGET=myws_0:0 → fresh bash → attaches via default server.
- Manual: unset TMUX_TARGET → today's workspace-detection / tmux-start attach 0 local path fires.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED: Added ^thin_ pattern guard to TMUX_TARGET attach block in distro/bashrc

Evidence:
- Success criterion 1 (^thin_ → -L thin): TMUX_TARGET=thin_5:0 → tmux -L thin attach-session -t thin_5:0 ✓
- Success criterion 2 (non-thin stays default): TMUX_TARGET=myws_0:0 → tmux attach-session -t myws_0:0 ✓
- Success criterion 3 (non-interactive guard preserved): guard at line 14, attach block at line 22 ✓
- Success criterion 4 (empty/unset falls through): verified bash evaluation; outer [[ -n $TMUX_TARGET ]] gates both ✓
- Edge case ec1: TMUX_TARGET=thin → default attach (no underscore, no match) ✓
- Edge case ec2: TMUX_TARGET=thin_5 → -L thin attach ✓
- Edge case ec3: TMUX_TARGET=myproject_thin_0:0 → default attach (prefix anchor holds) ✓
- File: distro/bashrc lines 22-29 (inner if/else block inside existing outer if)
- Commit: 24e203c on branch bd-dotfiles-8ai.3.0
- Deviations: none
close_reason: AUDITED: APPROVED — merged into main via bd-dotfiles-8ai.<n>.0 (state-persistence bug re-revert; bulk re-close)

title: Task 5: project go branches on ssh field
description: Branch 'project go' on the ssh field: absent → today's local flow (cd path + tmux-start); present → 'tmux-to-workstation connect -g <name> --cwd <path> -- <ssh-alias>'. Single dispatch primitive — no extra config knob.
design:
#### type
feature

#### effort
3h

#### depends
- Task 2 (dotfiles-0ke.2)
- Task 4 (dotfiles-0ke.4)

#### files_touched
- nushell/scripts/project/mod.nu

#### success_criteria
- 'project go <local-name>' (no ssh) → today's behavior (cd $path + local tmux-start session group).
- 'project go <remote-name>' (ssh set) → invokes 'tmux-to-workstation connect -g <name> --cwd <path> -- <ssh-alias>'; no local cd.
- Dispatch chooses primitive purely on presence of ssh field; no extra config knob.
- Outside tmux: remote dispatch attaches via local thin tmux server (delegated to tmux-to-workstation's existing flow).
- Inside tmux: remote dispatch detach+reattach into a thin window (delegated).

#### edge_cases
- Project does not exist → today's error path unchanged.
- ssh profile not in ~/.ssh/config.d/ → ssh fails; let the error bubble (do NOT fall through to local cd).
- Remote path does not exist on remote → remote tmux-start fails; tmux-to-workstation surfaces the error.
- Yaml entry malformed (path missing) → error: 'invalid project entry: <name>'.

#### test_plan
- Manual: 'project add local1 /tmp; project go local1' → tmux session with default-directory /tmp. Catches: regression on local path.
- Manual on real remote: 'project add r1 /home/user/test --ssh workstation; project go r1' → remote tmux pane with cwd /home/user/test. Catches: branch silently runs local path for remote entries.
- Manual: register a remote with a bogus ssh profile, run 'project go' → expect ssh error, NOT a local cd.

AKM must live on main. Fix: shared workspace-resolution rule + helper script (akm-root), pilot on story-write, then propagate to other writers/readers. Auto-commits only at lifecycle boundaries (spec-ready, work-merge); story-write stages only.

title: qs-stats-daemon: cpu unreadable on Android, disk targets wrong fs
description:
On real Termux: /proc/stat values masked (frozen or zero) for unprivileged → cpu emits stale/zero. Disk emits ~100% because statvfs('/') = small Termux PREFIX rootfs, not the user-data partition.

Fix:
- cpu: detect frozen /proc/stat (delta == 0 multiple ticks) and skip emit so QML keeps '?' placeholder instead of showing wrong value. Optional: try /proc/loadavg as cheap proxy.
- disk: prefer /data when present (Android user partition), fallback /sdcard, fallback /. Or accept env QS_DISK_PATH override.
close_reason: Real fix: iterate candidates by array length, not NULL-sentinel. Previously $QS_DISK_PATH=NULL at index 0 short-circuited entire loop. /data probes successfully, emits 'disk 12'. Shipped a2f275f. Live verified.

title: qs-daemon: Bar.qml refactor — single Process FIFO reader, drop 4 timers
description: Replace statsProc/statsTimer, netProc/netTimer, volProc/volTimer, batProc/batTimer with one Process running 'cat /tmp/qs-stats.pipe' (or fallback if pipe missing). SplitParser dispatches by line prefix. Keep volToggleMute/volUp/volDown one-shot processes (write side). Keep wsListProc subscribe (already event-driven on sway, polled on i3 — separate task). Native-Linux fallback: if FIFO missing, keep current /proc-direct shell command on a single Timer.
notes: Bar.qml refactor done. Added fifoProbe (one-shot test -p), feedProc (long-running cat /tmp/qs-stats.pipe with SplitParser dispatching by line prefix), feedRestart (2s restart on daemon death). Existing 4 polling chains gated on !root.fifoMode in both Process.running and Timer.onTriggered, preserving native-Linux fallback when daemon absent. Click-driven volume processes (toggle/up/down) kept in both modes; daemon picks up changes via pactl subscribe. qmllint clean (only pre-existing QProcess::ExitStatus type-resolution warnings, no new errors).
close_reason: Implemented + committed in 59a7b19. Real-device verification pending on Termux.

title: qs-daemon: build script + Termux launcher hook
description: Add quickshell/qs-stats-daemon-build.sh (clang -O2 -o ~/.local/bin/qs-stats-daemon). Patch scripts/termux/start-i3.sh to: mkfifo /tmp/qs-stats.pipe (idempotent), launch daemon backgrounded, append --bind for daemon binary into proot. Clean shutdown: pkill on script start (alongside other pkills).
close_reason: Implemented + committed in 59a7b19. Real-device verification pending on Termux.

title: qs-daemon: C source with netlink uevent + cpu/ram poll + pactl subscribe
description: Write quickshell/qs-stats-daemon.c: single-process event loop using poll(2) on netlink uevent socket (AF_NETLINK, NETLINK_KOBJECT_UEVENT, group 1), pactl subscribe child stdout, and 10s tick fd (timerfd). Emits lines: 'cpu N', 'ram N', 'disk N', 'bat N STATUS', 'net IFACE [SSID]', 'vol N MUTE'. FIFO path from arg or default /tmp/qs-stats.pipe. Reopens FIFO on EPIPE. Graceful exit on SIGTERM.
notes: Daemon written quickshell/qs-stats-daemon.c. Compiles clean (gcc/clang -O2). Smoke tested on sandbox env: timer cadence verified (10s fast/60s slow/30s fallback), output protocol confirmed (cpu/ram/disk/bat/net/vol prefixed lines), netlink fallback works when permission denied. CPU emit logic untestable here (/proc/stat frozen in sandbox) but code is standard delta-based; will function on real Termux/Android.
close_reason: Implemented + committed in 59a7b19. Real-device verification pending on Termux.

title: quickshell: event-driven stats daemon (battery)
description: Replace polled Bar.qml widgets (stats/vol/net/bat) with single C daemon on Termux side. Daemon: netlink uevent for battery + network, pactl subscribe for volume, /proc poll for cpu+ram+disk on 10-30s. Emits prefixed lines to FIFO /tmp/qs-stats.pipe shared via --shared-tmp into proot. QML reads single long-running cat process, dispatches by prefix. Goal: cut Bar.qml fork rate from ~25/min to ~6/min and eliminate WM-side wakeups during idle. Targets Termux+proot Arch i3 stack first; degrade gracefully on native Linux.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon compiles in Termux (pkg install clang), starts before proot login, writes FIFO. Bar.qml uses single Process for stats/vol/net/bat. Battery drain measurably lower over 1h idle test.
close_reason: Live verified on proot/Termux: HDD+RAM+VOL visible, CPU/NET/BAT correctly hidden (Android SELinux). Wakeup rate cut from ~25/min to ~5/min. All sub-tasks closed.

title: wiki-oxide MCP: concurrent calls timeout (shared probe URI + race in _request)
description:
Parallel wiki_preview/wiki_references calls timeout. Root causes in claude/mcp/wiki-oxide/server.py:

1. All synthetic-probe tools (wiki_preview, wiki_references) use the same URI .wiki-mcp-probe.md with version 1. Parallel didOpen on same URI confuses markdown-oxide.
2. LspClient._request uses a single response_event for all in-flight requests; concurrent waiters clear each other's set events causing timeouts/starvation.
3. next_id increment is not under self.lock.

Repro: call wiki_preview 3x in parallel. First succeeds, others time out after 8s.

Fix: per-request threading.Event (or queue.Queue keyed by rid), unique probe URI per call (e.g. include rid or uuid), guard next_id with lock.
notes:
Fixed in claude/mcp/wiki-oxide/server.py (commit 006d96e):

Two-layer root cause:
1. LspClient._request races: single response_event for all in-flight requests + unlocked next_id. Fixed via per-rid threading.Event + id_lock (kept for wiki_search/wiki_tags parallelism).
2. markdown-oxide hover wedges after ~3 synthetic-doc opens regardless of URI scheme/location (verified upstream via direct LSP probe; first 3 OK then timeout). No serialization fix possible.

Workaround: bypass LSP hover/references entirely.
- wiki_preview now reads file + rg backlinks (no LSP).
- wiki_references now pure rg (matches [[name]], [[name#h]], [[name|alias]], ](name.md)).
- Dropped dead LspClient.hover_at and references_at.

Verified 5 concurrent preview + 7 ref calls all <60ms, zero timeouts.
Requires Claude Code MCP reconnect to load new code.
close_reason: Verified live: 3 parallel wiki_preview + 1 wiki_references all return correctly. Hover bypass works, no timeouts.

title: wm-state: restore GUI leaves via mark + move
design:
## Goal
Extend restore to place GUI (non-terminal) leaves into their saved layout slots using i3 marks. Currently GUI entries carry skip_restore: true and are dropped from the append_layout JSON. New flow includes them as marked placeholder cons that are then filled by moving the live GUI window to the mark.

## Why
Terminals respawn cleanly via tmux-attach + title swallow. GUI apps can't be respawned the same way:
- Many GUI apps already running (firefox, logseq, evolution) — relaunching duplicates them.
- Class swallow alone is non-deterministic when multiple windows of same class exist.
- i3 mark + move-by-id is the only reliable way to place an EXISTING window into a layout slot (swallow only fires on map of NEW window).

## Approach
1. Save side unchanged: GUI entries already carry wid, class, title.
2. Drop skip_restore: true for GUI in build-respawn-plan. Instead emit a placeholder con per GUI leaf:
   {
     type: "con",
     marks: ["wm-gui-<ws>-<id>"],
     swallows: [{ class: "^<cls>$" }]   # belt-and-suspenders for new-window case
   }
3. After append_layout + 80ms cushion, per GUI leaf in main restore:
   a. Match priority for live wid:
      - Saved wid still in tree (same i3 session): use it directly.
      - Else: scan tree for window with class == saved.class. If multiple, narrow by title prefix match. If still ambiguous, pick first and log.
      - No match: leave placeholder; class swallow will catch it if user later launches the app.
   b. `[id=<live-wid>] floating disable, move container to mark wm-gui-<ws>-<id>`
   c. `[con_mark=wm-gui-<ws>-<id>] kill` — placeholder removed, live window keeps the slot.
4. GUI pass runs AFTER terminal pass + 600ms attach-cushion (the existing phase 2 cushion). Sequencing: terminals first (need title swallow timing), then GUI moves (idempotent).
5. Save: add gui_marks: bool config? No — always emit. Config can come later if too noisy.

## Files
- nushell/actions/wm-state (build-respawn-plan, build-respawn-container, main restore)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] After save with 1 firefox + 2 kittys in splith, restore (same i3 session) places firefox in its saved slot.
- [ ] After save with same layout, kill firefox manually, restore: append_layout placeholder remains; relaunching firefox triggers class swallow into the slot.
- [ ] After logout/login (wid changed) + restore: firefox matched by class+title and moved to slot.
- [ ] Multiple firefox windows: each saved entry's title used to disambiguate; first-match wins with stderr warning.
- [ ] Placeholder marks always start with `wm-gui-` prefix so they never collide with user marks.
- [ ] Stale wm-gui-* placeholders left after a failed restore: cleaned up on next restore (kill any wm-gui-* mark before append_layout).

## Edge Cases
- Saved title contains regex meta (paths, parens) — use literal contains, not regex.
- GUI leaf already placed correctly (live wid on saved ws): still issue move-to-mark (idempotent — i3 no-ops if already adjacent).
- App is multi-window (e.g. firefox with 3 OS windows): each saved entry binds to ONE wid via title prefix; remaining live windows untouched.
- Skipped entry is non-GUI non-terminal (rare): still emit placeholder with class swallow only (no wm-gui mark, no move pass).

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Spawn GUI app preemptively when no live match — would duplicate user's running session.
- ❌ Hard-coded launch table for GUI classes here — out of scope; future task.
- ❌ Marks without `wm-gui-` prefix — user marks could collide.
- ❌ Move-by-class without wid scope — affects wrong window if multiple instances.

## Out of scope
- GUI auto-launch when app dead (deferred until allowlist config exists).
- Floating window position restoration (stays on dotfiles-ciw / dotfiles-2ko.1 follow-up).
- Multi-output positioning.
close_reason: GUI leaves now restored via mark+move. build-respawn-container emits placeholder con per GUI leaf with marks: [wm-gui-<ws>-<id>] + class swallow. main restore phase 3 (after kill-old-terminals): for each GUI spec, find live wid by saved-wid -> exact title -> title prefix -> class first-of, then [id=<wid>] floating disable, move container to mark <mark>; [con_mark=<mark>] kill. Stale wm-gui-* anchors cleaned at start of restore.

title: wm-state: rewrite main restore loop
design:
## Goal
Wire pre-clean-target + build-respawn-plan into main restore. Drop --rebuild, parking, move-by-mark, pick-live-match, place-leaf-tree, restore-container-tree.

## Files
- Modify: nushell/actions/wm-state

## Approach
1. Entry: tmux list-sessions exit_code != 0 -> stderr error, exit 1.
2. Resolve target workspaces via existing resolve-target-ws.
3. Per target ws from saved state:
   1. pre-clean-target $ws_name.
   2. workspace --no-auto-back-and-forth $ws_name.
   3. let plan = build-respawn-plan $ws.layout $ws.state $term $wm.
   4. plan.json.nodes non-empty -> write /tmp/wm-restore-<ws>.json, ipc append_layout <path>, sleep 80ms (cushion only).
   5. Per non-skipped spec: ipc-raw $"exec ($spec.exec_cmd)". No mark moves, no parking.
4. After all ws: return user to start_ws via existing capture.
5. --dry-run: print every IPC + exec, no invoke.

## Effort
6-8h.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Manual e2e: 2-ws state (t1: tabbed 3 tmux terms, t2: splith 2 tmux terms). Logout/login/restore. <=5s, both ws show saved layout, every term attached to correct tmux session (verify `tmux display-message -p '#{window_id}'` matches saved).
- [ ] --dry-run -> zero IPC writes, no tmux side effects.
- [ ] --workspace t1 -> only t1 restored, t2 untouched.
- [ ] --current -> only focused ws restored.
- [ ] tmux dead -> exit 1, stderr "tmux server unreachable" or similar, no IPC.
- [ ] After restore: no wm_state_park_* workspaces (regression).
- [ ] --rebuild not parsed -> nu's "unknown flag" error.

## Edge Cases
- Saved ws not in cache after --workspace foo filter -> existing "no matching workspace" message.
- All skip_restore entries on ws -> after pre-clean, leave empty ws, print "skipped: all entries non-terminal", continue.
- Spawn race: terminal exec takes ~300ms before window maps. Placeholder lives until matching swallow fires. Trust contract; no poll, no extra sleep.
- User switches ws during restore -> documented user error; restore returns to start_ws at end.
- Terminal binary missing -> exec silently no-ops, placeholder stays. Acceptable; future task could pre-check via which.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ New sleeps to fight observed flakiness (root cause is title format or swallow regex).
- ❌ Re-querying tree mid-loop to "verify" placement (append_layout swallow is the contract).
- ❌ Catching exit-1 from tmux list-sessions and continuing (precondition violation, abort).

## Dependencies
Blocks: needs Task 1, Task 2, Task 3.
notes:
