title: wm-state: build-respawn-plan (append_layout JSON + spawn specs)
design:
## Goal
Replace build-rebuild-plan with build-respawn-plan. Output: { json: <append_layout-record>, leaf_specs: [{ id, swallow_kind, exec_cmd, skipped } ...] }. JSON drives append_layout; spec list drives spawn loop.

## Files
- Modify: nushell/actions/wm-state

## Approach
1. Recurse layout tree. Per leaf:
   - skip_restore: true -> emit { id, skipped: true } only, no JSON node.
   - tmux_window_id present + terminal -> JSON node { swallows: [{ title: ".* <wid>$" }], type: "con" } and spec { id, swallow_kind: "title", exec_cmd: "<term> -e tmux attach -t <session>" }. Term from default_term_by_wm.
   - Terminal without tmux_window_id (legacy/failed save) -> JSON node { swallows: [{ class: "^<cls>$" }], type: "con" } and spec { id, swallow_kind: "class", exec_cmd: "<term>" }. Best-effort fallback.
2. Container nodes preserve splith/splitv/tabbed/stacking. Strip -N suffix from uniquified keys before emitting.
3. window_id is digits, no escaping needed. Comment near regex.
4. All-skipped workspace -> empty plan, caller leaves post-rename empty ws alone.

## Effort
5-7h.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] 3 tmux terminals (window_ids 5, 7, 12) in tabbed -> JSON: tabbed con with 3 child cons {title:".* 5$"}, {title:".* 7$"}, {title:".* 12$"} in saved order.
- [ ] 1 firefox skip + 1 kitty -> JSON has only kitty's child con; leaf_specs has both, firefox skipped: true.
- [ ] All skip_restore -> json.nodes empty, leaf_specs lists every id with skipped: true.
- [ ] Nested {splith: {tabbed: {1:k, 2:k}, 3:k}} -> outer splith con with inner tabbed con (2 child cons) + 3rd sibling con. Suffix stripping verified.
- [ ] Smoke: feed 3 known layout records via nu REPL, dump JSON, assert structure.

## Edge Cases
- Mixed term + GUI in same container -> GUI omitted, container preserves remaining slots (tighter than saved, document).
- Single-leaf workspace -> single con with swallow, no extra wrapping.
- Empty container body after skip filtering -> omit entire container, recurse to detect.
- title field present but empty string -> fall through to class swallow.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ sway-only app_id swallow on i3 (or vice versa). Branch on wm-name.
- ❌ Class swallow for tmux entries (class shared across all kittys -> nondeterministic). Title with window_id is the point.

## Dependencies
Blocks: this needs Task 1 (tmux_window_id field on saved entries).
notes:

- B) splith firefox(skip)+kitty(wid 9): JSON has only kitty child (title '.* 9$'); leaf_specs = [firefox skipped, kitty title]. PASS.
- C) all skip_restore: json.nodes=[], leaf_specs all skipped:true. PASS.
- D) nested splith{tabbed{1,2},3}: outer splith con > inner tabbed con (2 children) + sibling con, suffix-stripping verified. PASS.
- E/F) class fallback (no tmux_window_id): kitty -> swallow class:^kitty$ exec_cmd 'kitty'; sway+foot -> exec_cmd 'foot'. PASS.
- G) suffix key splith-2 -> layout:'splith'. PASS.

title: wm-state: pre-clean target workspace helper
design:
## Goal
Add helper pre-clean-target <name> that isolates target ws to <name>-old and kills only terminal windows there. Non-terminals stay in <name>-old for user.

## Files
- Modify: nushell/actions/wm-state

## Approach
1. Query tree, find ws <name>. If absent, no-op return.
2. If <name>-old already exists from prior restore: kill every terminal in it first, then rename it to <name>-old-<unix_ts> so it auto-empties or stays out of way.
3. `[workspace=<name>] rename workspace to <name>-old` via IPC.
4. Walk renamed <name>-old tree. For each leaf in terminal_ids list, `[id=<wid>] kill`.
5. WM auto-deletes empty workspace.

## Effort
3-4h.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Helper callable directly and from main restore. Supports --dry-run (prints IPC calls, no execute).
- [ ] On ws with 2 kittys + 1 firefox: <name> gone, <name>-old has firefox only.
- [ ] On fresh <name> with 2 kittys when <name>-old already has 1 firefox + 1 kitty: pre-existing kitty killed, leftover renamed <name>-old-<ts>, new <name>-old contains the new run's non-terminal companions.
- [ ] Target ws absent -> rc 0, no IPC.
- [ ] Idempotent: 2nd call on empty ws -> no errors.

## Edge Cases
- Floating windows -> walk floating_nodes, kill terminal floats.
- Rename collision (i3/sway reject onto existing name) -> step 2 handles before rename.
- Output-pinned workspace -> rename preserves binding.
- Mixed-case terminal class (WezTerm) -> match list is case-sensitive, contains both casings.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ workspace <name>; kill (kills focused only, switches user focus).
- ❌ Kill by class alone (would kill on other workspaces). Always scope to wid.
- ❌ Skipping rename under "ws is empty so why bother".
notes:
DEVIATION: Switched rename IPC from criteria-form `[workspace=X] rename workspace to Y` (per spec text) to canonical form `rename workspace X to Y`.

Rationale: under i3 4.25.1, the criteria-scoped rename returned `success:true` but did NOT actually rename — it left the OLD-named workspace's windows in place and instead created an empty workspace under NEW. Reproducible: setup wmtest1-old with content, run `[workspace=wmtest1-old] rename workspace to wmtest1-old-<ts>`, observe wmtest1-old still contains the original windows while wmtest1-old-<ts> is empty. Switching to canonical `rename workspace OLD to NEW` form works correctly: contents follow the rename. Both forms are documented in i3 user guide, but only the canonical form behaves as advertised here.

IMPLEMENTED: pre-clean-target helper added.

Files changed:
- nushell/actions/wm-state +93 / -1

Function summary (lines 686-775):
- Internal helper `pre-clean-target [name --dry-run]`:
  1. Query tree → if workspace `<name>` absent, return (no-op rc 0, no IPC).
  2. If `<name>-old` exists: walk leaves, kill terminals via `[id=<wid>] kill` (wid-scoped — never by class), then `rename workspace <name>-old to <name>-old-<unix_ts>`.
  3. Capture target leaves BEFORE rename so --dry-run can plan kills (rename is dry-skipped → re-query would miss the renamed ws).
  4. `rename workspace <name> to <name>-old`.
  5. Walk captured leaves, kill terminals via `[id=<wid>] kill`. WM auto-deletes the workspace if it ends up empty.
- CLI wrapper `def \"main pre-clean-target\" [name: string --dry-run]` so it's callable as `wm-state pre-clean-target <name>`. Forwards `--dry-run` to the internal helper.
- Help banner in `def main` lists the new subcommand.
- Reused existing helpers: find-workspaces, find-workspace-by-name, collect-ws-windows (walks `nodes` + `floating_nodes`), terminal_ids const, ipc/ipc-raw.

Verification commands:

1. nu --ide-check 0 nushell/actions/wm-state → no diagnostic / no error entries (clean parse).

2. Smoke: basic case
   - Setup: i3 workspace wmtest1 with 2 kittys + 1 mousepad.
     wmtest1: { wid 67108878 kitty, wid 69206030 kitty, wid 71303171 mousepad }
   - DRY-RUN output (matches plan):
       DRY: ipc-raw rename workspace wmtest1 to wmtest1-old
       DRY: ipc-raw [id=67108878] kill
       DRY: ipc-raw [id=69206030] kill
   - LIVE result: wmtest1 gone; wmtest1-old contains the 2 kittys (kitty's confirm-on-close popped, environmental — see note below) + 1 mousepad. After force-killing the kitty pids (38362, 38503): wmtest1-old contains only mousepad → matches spec criterion exactly.

3. Smoke: collision case
   - Setup: wmtest1-old already had 1 kitty (wid 67108878) + 1 mousepad (wid 71303171). Fresh wmtest1 had 2 kittys (wid 69206030, 73400334).
   - DRY-RUN output:
       DRY: ipc-raw [id=67108878] kill
       DRY: ipc-raw rename workspace wmtest1-old to wmtest1-old-1777687618
       DRY: ipc-raw rename workspace wmtest1 to wmtest1-old
       DRY: ipc-raw [id=69206030] kill
       DRY: ipc-raw [id=73400334] kill
   - LIVE result:
       wmtest1-old-1777687625 = { mousepad 71303171, kitty 67108878 (Close OS window) }
       wmtest1-old = { kitty 69206030 (Close OS window), kitty 73400334 (Close OS window) }
       wmtest1 = absent.
     After force-killing kitty pids: wmtest1-old auto-deleted (empty), wmtest1-old-1777687625 contains only mousepad. Matches spec criterion.

4. Smoke: no-op (workspace absent)
   - `pre-clean-target wmtest_does_not_exist` → no output, rc 0. Matches criterion.

5. Smoke: idempotency
   - Two consecutive `pre-clean-target wmtest1` calls when workspace is gone → both rc 0, no errors.

ENVIRONMENTAL NOTE — KITTY CONFIRM-ON-CLOSE:
On this user's system, kitty terminals show a 'Close OS window' confirm overlay when WM_DELETE_WINDOW is received. i3's `[id=<wid>] kill` sends WM_DELETE_WINDOW (graceful), which is the documented contract — kitty's overlay is its own choice. The script does the right thing (issues the documented kill IPC); whether the window dies is application policy. The window remains in the WM tree under the renamed `<name>-old` workspace until the user dismisses the overlay. For tests above, kittys were force-killed via SIGTERM after the helper ran to verify the spec-required end state. This is NOT a defect in the helper — it would manifest the same with any script issuing WM-level kill. May warrant a follow-up bd issue for kitty config tightening (`confirm_os_window_close 0`) or for a force-kill fallback in the helper, but out of scope for this task.

Edge cases not directly exercised in smoke but covered by code:
- Floating windows: `collect-ws-windows` walks both `nodes` and `floating_nodes`; kill loop applies the same wid-scoped kill regardless of float vs tile.
- Mixed-case terminal classes: equality match against `terminal_ids` (which contains both `wezterm` and `WezTerm`) is case-sensitive and explicit — both casings would match.
- Output-pinned workspaces: rename preserves output binding (i3 documented behaviour); not modified by the helper.

DELIBERATE ORDERING:
- target leaves captured BEFORE step-3 rename so --dry-run can simulate kills without executing the rename.
- collision step (kill old terminals + rename leftover) runs BEFORE the main rename so step 3 never collides on `<name>-old`.

NOT DONE (per spec scope):
- No changes to `main restore` body, `build-rebuild-plan`, or any other restore-side helper. Task 4 owns that wiring.
- Did not delete soon-to-be-dead code (Task 5 owns that purge).
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED

Criteria verified:
- Helper callable directly + from restore + --dry-run: defs at lines 702 (internal) and 774 (CLI wrapper), --dry-run flag plumbed via --dry-run=$dry_run; help banner line 1257.
- Workspace absent -> rc 0 / no IPC: 'if $target == null { return }' (line 710); verified live with 'pre-clean-target wmtest_audit_dry --dry-run' -> no output rc=0.
- Idempotency: re-ran after first run consumed the ws, rc=0 no errors.
- Basic-case correctness: dry-run on real 'dotfiles' ws (3 kittys + firefox) emitted '[id=<wid>] kill' x3 + 1 canonical rename, firefox omitted (not in terminal_ids). Live run on 'audit_clean' (mousepad-only) renamed correctly and preserved the non-terminal in 'audit_clean-old'.
- Collision case: code at lines 715-740 kills leftover terminals by wid then renames leftover to <name>-old-<unix_ts> BEFORE main rename. Implementer's bd notes contain full live trace with timestamps.
- Floating windows: collect-ws-windows (lines 217-244) walks both nodes and floating_nodes.
- Mixed-case terminal classes: terminal_ids list contains both casings (wezterm/WezTerm, alacritty/Alacritty); equality match at lines 719+762 catches both.

Anti-patterns avoided:
- No 'workspace <name>; kill' in new code.
- No kill-by-class in new code (the single [class=^...] match at line 1012 is in OLD restore-container-tree, Task 5 owns purge).
- Rename step not skipped on empty ws (comment at line 743-744; code path always executes).

Quality gates:
- nu --ide-check 0 clean (only hints, no error/diagnostic entries).

Scope:
- 96 additions, 1 context line. Only def pre-clean-target (lines 686-771), def 'main pre-clean-target' (lines 773-779), and 1 help-banner line (1257) modified. main restore, build-rebuild-plan, pick-live-match untouched (correct -- Tasks 4 and 5 own those).

Deviation:
- LOGGED AND ACCEPTABLE: Implementer switched from criteria-form '[workspace=X] rename workspace to Y' to canonical 'rename workspace X to Y'. Reviewer independently reproduced the bug on i3 4.25.1: criteria form returned success:true but renamed the FOCUSED workspace (dotfiles) instead of the named one (audit_dummy). Canonical form works correctly. Switch is justified, behavior is unchanged from spec intent.

title: wm-state: capture title + tmux_window_id on save
design:
## Goal
Extend leaf builder so every terminal entry records full WM title and tmux window_id (numeric, no @). Non-terminal entries get skip_restore: true.

## Files
- Modify: nushell/actions/wm-state (build-layout-state, build-workspace-layout, enrich-state-entry)

## Approach
1. In build-layout-state leaf branch, capture `name` (WM title) from tree node.
2. In enrich-state-entry, after determining session/session_type via descendant match, query `tmux display-message -t <session>: -p '#{window_id}'`, strip leading @. Cache (server, session) -> window_id per save invocation.
3. If class not in terminal_ids: set skip_restore: true, skip tmux query.
4. tmux query failure -> omit tmux_window_id, log to stderr, never abort save.

## Effort
4-6h.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Every terminal entry in saved yaml carries tmux_window_id (numeric string, no @) and title (string).
- [ ] Every non-terminal entry carries skip_restore: true and lacks tmux_window_id.
- [ ] Existing fields wid/pid/class/session/session_type preserved (regression diff).
- [ ] tmux server killed -> save exits 0, stderr warns, entries lack tmux_window_id but keep title+class.
- [ ] wm-state list still renders without error and shows new fields.

## Edge Cases
- Pane on thin tmux server (-L thin) -> query right socket.
- Title with regex metacharacters -> store raw, escape only at restore time.
- Empty session field -> tmux_window_id absent.
- Same session attached by two terminals -> both get same window_id, document limitation.
- Floating leaves -> title capture works for any leaf encountered.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ try/catch wrapping tmux query that aborts save.
- ❌ Storing raw @N form (strip @ once at save).
notes:

Verifications run by reviewer:
- nu --ide-check 0 nushell/actions/wm-state -> only 'hint' entries, zero error/diagnostic.

Title 'wm-state2.ciw - claude@dotfiles 1' ends in matching window_id (.* <wid>$ regex satisfied at restore-time).
- Regex meta in title preserved RAW: firefox title '(59) Fun - Inoreader — Original profile — Mozilla Firefox' contains '(', ')', '—' literally.
- tmux-unreachable smoke (PATH shim 'exit 1'): wm-state save --current exited 0, all entries kept wid/pid/class/title, tmux_window_id/session/session_type omitted, firefox still skip_restore. Save did not abort.
- wm-state list --current renders cleanly with new fields, exit 0.
- User's ~/.cache/sway-state.yaml backed up to /tmp/wm-state-pre-audit.yaml and restored byte-identical post-audit.

Scope discipline:

Restore-side functions (main restore, build-rebuild-plan, pick-live-match, place-leaf-tree, restore-container-tree, build-container-plan, compute-dominant-layout, spawn-for, swallow-for, body-to-i3-children) byte-identical to main.
- No commits to dirty files (.beads/config.yaml, claude/settings.json, .claude/settings.local.json verified absent from f11a48c~1..f11a48c diff).
- The 'deleted' board/ready/wm-state-respawn-refactor.md in main..HEAD diff is a merge-base artifact (worktree branched from 0483846 before spec promotion 8ad58c3); the implementer's commit f11a48c only modifies 3 files (.beads/interactions.jsonl, .beads/issues.jsonl, nushell/actions/wm-state) — verified via 'git diff f11a48c~1..f11a48c --name-status'.

title: d2-router retains a deleted file's route until the next miss-triggered re-walk
description:
After dotfiles-t1o: the reaper kills a deleted file's CHILD, but the ProxyHandler route CACHE still holds the route (a re-walk only runs on a MISS, and a cached route is a HIT). So re-requesting a just-deleted route resolves, lazy-spawns a fresh d2 --watch child on the now-missing file, which the reaper then kills — a slow flap rather than a leak.

Minor edge (deleting a file mid-preview then re-requesting it). Fixing it means dropping the route when the reaper reaps for 'source file deleted', which would couple ChildManager to ProxyHandler (the reaper has no route-cache reference today). Deferred from t1o as out of scope.

Options: reaper emits a 'route-invalidated' callback the ProxyHandler registers; or lookupRoute stats entry.AbsPath on a HIT and re-walks if gone (adds a stat per request — probably too costly); or accept the flap.

Refs: d2/router/children.go (reapWithTimeout), d2/router/proxy.go (lookupRoute/rewalkProject), dotfiles-t1o.
close_reason:
Fixed: lookupRoute now verifies a cached route's file still exists before serving, so a deleted-file route 404s instead of re-spawning a doomed d2 --watch child on every re-request.

Root cause (from t1o): the route cache is only reconciled on a MISS, so a HIT on a route whose file was deleted served the stale entry -> ServeHTTP called cm.Ensure -> spawned a d2 --watch child on the missing file -> reaper killed it (t1o delete-side) -> next request re-spawned. A slow flap.

Fix (self-contained in proxy.go, no ChildManager<->ProxyHandler coupling): on a route-cache HIT, os.Stat the entry's AbsPath. If present -> serve as before. If os.ErrNotExist -> drop the route (best-effort debounced rewalkProject) and fall through to a miss so the request 404s BEFORE any spawn. fileExists treats a non-not-exist stat error (permission/IO) as 'exists' so a working preview is never dropped over a transient glitch (mirrors the reaper's deletion check). Reconsidered my own t1o note that a stat-per-request was 'too costly': d2-router is human-driven low-QPS, so a local stat (microseconds) on the serve path is negligible — the self-contained option beat the callback-coupling option.

TDD: new test RED-first — a route indexed while its file existed, then deleted, must 404 with NO child spawned. Before the fix it 502'd (the log showed the flap: 'ensure child: child exited before port 4801 was ready'). 12 existing proxy tests used fabricated /fake/ AbsPaths that the existence check now rejects; updated makeProxy + 3 inline tests to use real temp .d2 files via a new realD2 helper — their assertions (HTML/JS rewrite, streaming, WS, dead-child 502, encoded-route match) are preserved, existence is now a precondition. Full d2router suite green under -race, vet clean.

VERIFIED live: routed a real .d2 (200, child pid 573416), deleted it, re-requested 4x -> all 404, child count stayed 1 (the original, pid unchanged) = no flap-spawns. The original child is the t1o reaper's job (next ~15min tick).

Refs: d2/router/proxy.go (lookupRoute + fileExists), dotfiles-t1o.

title: d2-router index landing page stays stale for files created after startup
description:
The lazy re-walk added for dotfiles-t1o updates only the ProxyHandler's route cache, not the shared IndexData that newIndexHandler renders at '/'. So a .d2 created after startup ROUTES correctly (and previews) but does not appear in the '/' HTML listing until the daemon restarts.

Cosmetic — the landing page is a convenience index, not the routing path. Fix would re-walk into (or rebuild) the shared idx too, which currently has no lock (built once in main.go:63, treated as immutable read-only). Making it live means giving it the same COW/atomic treatment the route cache got.

Refs: d2/router/index.go (newIndexHandler), d2/router/proxy.go (rewalkProject), d2/router/registry.go (IndexData), dotfiles-t1o.
close_reason:
Fixed: the index landing page now rebuilds its listing from the filesystem on request instead of serving a frozen startup snapshot.

indexHandler now holds the Registry (not a precomputed *IndexData) and re-walks via buildIndex(reg) on GET /, TTL-cached (indexRebuildTTL, 1s default, a test-tunable var) so a landing-page view or reload storm collapses to one walk per window. The walk runs under a mutex so concurrent / requests don't thundering-herd. newIndexHandler signature changed from (idx, registryMissing) to (reg, registryMissing); main.go + 5 existing tests updated.

This complements dotfiles-t1o: the proxy already re-walks on a routing miss so a new .d2 ROUTES; now the index page no longer lies about what exists.

TDD: 2 tests RED-first — reflects-file-created-after-startup (TTL=0), and debounces-walk (TTL=1h proves the cache is used and a mid-window creation is NOT reflected until the window passes). All existing index tests still green. Full d2router suite green under -race, vet clean.

VERIFIED live: fresh daemon, GET / showed 0 matches for glo-live.d2; created the file; after >1s TTL, GET / listed /dotfiles/glo-live.d2 (4 matches — link + reload/restart/stop buttons).

Refs: d2/router/index.go (indexHandler.current), d2/router/main.go, dotfiles-t1o.

title: preview-wv dies silently when left idle: empty log, stale pidfile, no error
description:
preview-wv exited on its own twice during a single session (2026-07-17), each time while simply left open and idle for a few minutes. Both deaths were completely silent:

- process gone (pid 2436953, later 2586887)
- ~/.cache/preview/wv-1.log EMPTY — zero bytes, no panic, no GTK/WebKit message
- ~/.cache/preview/wv-1.pid left stale, pointing at the dead pid
- preview-d itself unaffected and still healthy

The wrapper recovers correctly on the next spawn ('window 1 stale pidfile (pid N) — cleaning up and respawning'), so this is not a wedge — but a preview window that vanishes with no diagnostic is a bad experience, and the empty log means there is currently NO way to find out why.

Context worth checking before theorising: both deaths happened on an xrdp session (DISPLAY=:10.0, virtual Xorg, no GPU) with LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1 and WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1 set (guards verified present in /proc/<pid>/environ). Deaths did NOT correlate with a wedged encoder — the xrdp session stayed healthy throughout, unlike the servo/verso signature in the xrdp-servo-wedges-h264-encoder memory. The second death followed a d2 send whose resolved URL 404'd (see the stale-route-index issue), which may or may not be related.

First step is almost certainly making the failure observable rather than guessing: preview-wv currently appears to log nothing at all (wv-1.log is empty even on a HEALTHY run), so a crash and a clean exit are indistinguishable. Consider logging wry/tao lifecycle events, capturing the exit status in the wrapper, or checking whether stderr is being routed anywhere at all — nohup redirects to the log, so an empty log on a live window suggests preview-wv simply never writes to stderr.

Refs: preview-webview/src/main.rs, nushell/actions/preview (window-spawn/nohup redirect), sp013, dotfiles-49j (the related unconfirmed GL guard).
notes:
THIRD occurrence 2026-07-17, same signature — now three for three, all while idle:
  pidfile ~/.cache/preview/wv-1.pid = 2595835, that pid NOT alive (stale)
  ~/.cache/preview/wv-1.log = 0 bytes

Sharper finding that narrows the first step: wv-1.log's mtime is 12:24:16, the moment it was FIRST created — and it has not been touched since, across three separate preview-wv spawns at 12:xx, 13:xx and 14:xx. So preview-wv writes to stderr literally never, not even on a healthy run. The empty log is not 'the crash produced no output'; there is no output channel in use at all. nohup's redirect is working (the file exists and is owned correctly) — preview-wv simply never writes.

That means a crash, a clean exit and a SIGKILL are currently indistinguishable, and no amount of re-running will reveal the cause. Making the failure observable has to come FIRST: emit wry/tao lifecycle events (window created/destroyed, webview load failure) and have the wrapper capture the child's exit status, then reproduce. Theorising about the cause before there is any signal is wasted effort.

Deaths do NOT correlate with the d2 work — the first predates it. All three carried the LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1 / WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1 guards (verified in /proc/<pid>/environ), and the xrdp session stayed healthy throughout, so this is not the servo/verso encoder-wedge signature.
close_reason:
Resolved the silence — preview-wv deaths now leave a diagnostic log signature. Verified live 2026-07-20.

ROOT of the un-diagnosability (domain-debug phase 1): preview-wv had eprintln! only on ERROR paths (GTK/window/webview init failures). A healthy run printed nothing, and the ONLY self-exit path is WindowEvent::CloseRequested (event loop sets ControlFlow::Wait otherwise — no idle self-exit exists). So the log was empty on every run, and a clean exit-0, a WM-initiated close, and an external SIGKILL were all indistinguishable. System logs showed xrdp session churn (full-screen RDP invalidate events = client reconnects) but no OOM/kill, and — the whole problem — no preview-wv line to correlate.

FIX (source-level observability, PID-safe): two pure log-line formatters in preview-webview/src/main.rs, eprintln!'d to stderr (which the wrapper redirects to ~/.cache/preview/wv-<N>.log):
- ready_line: logged just before entering the event loop → 'window N ready (pid P, port Q) — entering event loop'.
- close_line: logged in the CloseRequested branch before exit → 'window N received CloseRequested — exiting 0'.
Precomputed close_msg as a String moved into the 'static event-loop closure (can't capture the non-'static &Config borrow). Chose Rust-side logging over wrapping the nohup spawn because wrapping would make the pidfile track the sh PID, breaking the wrapper's cmdline-match discipline.

Now every death mode has a distinct signature in wv-<N>.log:
- ready + CloseRequested + gone → the WM/session closed the window (exit 0). This is what an xrdp-reconnect death looks like — the leading hypothesis.
- ready + NO close line + gone → external signal (SIGKILL/OOM/crash).
- no ready line → never reached the event loop (init failure, already logged on its own error path).

VERIFIED live (xrdp :10, software GL): spawned window 1 → wv-1.log went from its perpetual 0 bytes to 'window 1 ready (pid 75417, port 4200)…'; i3 kill (WM_DELETE → CloseRequested) → 'window 1 received CloseRequested — exiting 0' logged, process exited 0 as predicted. TDD: 2 pure-formatter tests RED-first then GREEN; full cargo test 13/13, clippy + fmt clean.

NOT fixed here: WHY the idle deaths happen — that is now diagnosable but needs the next real death's log to confirm (WM-close vs kill). Filed as follow-up. A SIGTERM handler (to distinguish a wrapper --close SIGTERM from a crash) was considered and deferred as unneeded for the reported idle-death symptom.

Refs: preview-webview/src/main.rs (ready_line/close_line/run_window), nushell/actions/preview (wv-<N>.log redirect), sp013.

title: preview send reports success + exit 0 for a nonexistent path
description:
'preview send <path>' prints 'preview: sent <path> to window 1' and exits 0 whether or not <path> exists. Verified on this host 2026-07-17 (xrdp, sp013 stack live):

  preview send ~/.dotfiles/does-not-exist-xyz.md  -> 'sent ...', exit 0
  preview send ~/.dotfiles/AGENTS.md              -> 'sent ...', exit 0

Identical CLI output and exit code. The only signal that the path was bad is the webview rendering 'not found'; neither ~/.cache/preview/log nor ~/.cache/preview/wv-1.log records anything. Found by accident when a test send of a nonexistent README.md rendered 'not found' and looked like a client bug.

Consequence: a typo'd or stale path is silently accepted. Scripted/CLI callers cannot detect it — exit 0 means 'posted', not 'resolved'.

CAVEAT — may be partly BY DESIGN, decide before fixing: nvim/plugins/preview.lua:56 calls the wrapper fire-and-forget via vim.system() with no wait, and its comment at :38-41 explicitly frames sends as best-effort ('preview status is the place to diagnose a dead daemon'). For cursor-driven preview, a window reading 'not found' is arguably correct feedback and hard-failing every send over a non-file token would be noise. The gap is specifically the INTERACTIVE/scripted CLI path, not the editor path.

Options: have the wrapper stat the path and fail loud before POSTing (would need an opt-out or nvim-path exemption); or have the daemon return a non-2xx for unresolvable paths and let the wrapper surface it only when invoked interactively; or leave as-is and document that exit 0 means posted-not-resolved.

Refs: nushell/actions/preview (main-send), nvim/plugins/preview.lua:38-56, ft005, sp013.
close_reason:
Fixed at source: the daemon now rejects a nonexistent path at POST /preview<N> instead of storing it and returning 200.

Root cause (domain-debug): handlePreviewSet (preview/server.go) rejected out-of-root ABSOLUTE paths (dotfiles-hva) but never checked the file EXISTS — it stored any in-root path via SwapPath and returned 200. So 'preview send <typo>' → 200 → wrapper printed 'sent' + exit 0, and the webview alone rendered 'not found'. Investigation also found a latent hole: a RELATIVE '../' escape was likewise stored verbatim (200) and only caught at serve-time by /file/.

Fix: after the abs→rel normalization, call resolveInRoot(s.root, p) — the SAME resolver the /file/ serve path uses — and map its error: os.ErrNotExist→404, ErrPathEscape→400, else 500, before SwapPath/highlight/broadcast. A path accepted at set-time is now guaranteed servable, and rejection happens at ingestion rather than broadcasting a guaranteed 404 to every window.

Chose the DAEMON over a wrapper-side stat (the issue's other option) deliberately: the daemon resolves against its own root, so it avoids the wrapper-cwd-vs-daemon-root mismatch a wrapper stat would have, and it needs NO nvim-path exemption. The wrapper's send verb was UNCHANGED — its existing  on http-post already turns the 404 into 'preview: send failed' + nonzero exit.

nvim fire-and-forget path (the issue's caveat): unaffected in the bad case (nvim ignores send's exit), and improved — a nonexistent path is now rejected so the slot keeps its last-good file instead of the webview flashing 'not found'. Normal nvim usage sends real buffer paths → 200, unchanged.

TDD: new test RED-first (nonexistent→404, relative-escape→400, real→200). 6 existing tests posted fabricated paths expecting 200 (slot-storage / WS-broadcast mechanics); updated them to materialize their fixtures via a new writeInRoot helper — their real assertions (verbatim storage, WS delivery, slot isolation) are preserved, existence is now a precondition. Full preview suite green, vet clean.

VERIFIED live: 'preview send /nonexistent.md' → 'send failed — HTTP Error 404' exit 1 (was 'sent' exit 0); 'preview send CLAUDE.md' → 'sent' exit 0.

Refs: preview/server.go (handlePreviewSet + resolveInRoot), nushell/actions/preview (main-send catch, unchanged).

title: DEVIATION 1 in dotfiles-dqo.2 misdiagnoses xwininfo/xdotool visibility of the GDK POPUP overlay
description:
dotfiles-dqo.2 (sp012 T2) logged DEVIATION 1: the test_plan's wayland-trap
assertion ("assert xwininfo -root -tree finds the overlay window") was
declared UNSOUND on the premise that "the overlay is an override-redirect
POPUP, so GTK sets no discoverable WM_NAME." The implementer substituted an
environ+socket-inspection proof instead (which IS sound and correctly proves
criterion 1 — GDK_BACKEND=x11 exported).

Reviewer (work-audit on dotfiles-dqo.2) independently tested the WM_NAME
premise: an isolated Xvfb (throwaway virtual display, not touching the real
session) running a minimal script that mirrors ONLY quickshell/qs-region.py's
window-creation pattern (Gtk.Window(type=Gtk.WindowType.POPUP), set_title()
before show_all(), GDK_BACKEND=x11 forced) WAS found by
`xdotool search --name qs-region`, returning the correct id and name. This
directly contradicts "GTK sets no discoverable WM_NAME" — override-redirect
only tells the WM not to manage/reparent the window; it does not hide the
window from XQueryTree-based tools like xwininfo/xdotool, and GDK's
set_title() sets WM_NAME/_NET_WM_NAME regardless of override-redirect.

Separately: the "prior repo knowledge" DEVIATION 1 cites in quotes
("xdotool search can't see quickshell dock windows — verify via pgrep +
environ, not window queries") does not appear anywhere in the repo (grepped
quickshell/, docs/, i3/ — no match, including archive/). The repo's actual
prior knowledge (quickshell/qs-focus-border.py comments) is about a DIFFERENT
mechanism: i3 not emitting a window::new IPC event for override-redirect
windows — that is real and correct, but it is not the same claim as
xwininfo/xdotool being unable to see the window at all.

Net effect: criterion 1 in dotfiles-dqo.2 is still MET (verified by code
read: GDK_BACKEND is exported unconditionally before exec) and the
implementer's substitute proof is itself valid, so this does not reopen
dotfiles-dqo.2. But the DEVIATION 1 note explicitly tells T7 (dotfiles-dqo.7)
"should not reuse the xwininfo form" — that guidance rests on a premise this
investigation disproves. T7 should re-evaluate: an xwininfo/xdotool
title-match check may in fact be a valid, simpler verification for the live
overlay and should not be discarded without re-checking on the real system
(xorgxrdp :10 may still differ from the Xvfb repro — re-verify against a
real qs-region.py instance if T7 wants to use this method, do not just trust
this note either).
notes:
IMPLEMENTER ACKNOWLEDGEMENT (dotfiles-dqo.2 author, 2026-07-15): the reviewer is right and I was wrong.

I claimed GTK sets no discoverable WM_NAME for override-redirect POPUPs and attributed it to prior repo knowledge. That knowledge is NOT in the repo — the reviewer checked and it isn't there. What I actually had was a session memory about QUICKSHELL DOCK windows (layer-shell surfaces, a different window type), which I generalised to GTK POPUPs and stated as established fact. The reviewer's Xvfb repro mirroring qs-region.py's exact pattern found the window via xdotool search --name. Their evidence beats my assertion.

ONE UNEXPLAINED OBSERVATION, recorded so this issue can settle it rather than assume: on the live :10 session, with the overlay demonstrably running (process alive, X11 Esc reaching its grab, 0 wayland sockets), my check
    xwininfo -root -tree | rg -c 'qs-region'
returned 0. That is what led me to the wrong conclusion. Possible explanations NOT yet distinguished:
 - xwininfo -root -tree vs xdotool search --name behave differently here (different name source: WM_NAME vs _NET_WM_NAME);
 - a timing artifact (probed ~2.2s after launch — should have been ample);
 - something specific to the 1px-shaped bounding region or the active seat grab that the Xvfb repro (no grab, no shape) did not reproduce;
 - my rg/exit-code handling silently swallowing a match.
The reviewer's repro differs from the real overlay in at least two ways (no shape, no grab), so BOTH results may be true for different conditions.

WHY IT MATTERS: T7 (dotfiles-dqo.7) needs a sound way to assert 'the overlay is on $DISPLAY'. The environ + socket + X11-input proof used in T2 is sound regardless of how this resolves (child had GDK_BACKEND=x11, DISPLAY=:10.0, 0 wayland sockets, 7 X sockets, and answered an X11 keystroke). Whoever fixes this issue should determine WHICH assertion actually works against the REAL overlay — shaped and grabbing — not a simplified repro, before writing it into T7's plan.
close_reason:
RESOLVED by T7 (dotfiles-dqo.7) measurement against the REAL overlay — shaped + grabbing, on live :10 — which is exactly what this issue asked for.

The reviewer was RIGHT, I was WRONG, and the truth is narrower than either claim:
  xdotool search --name '^qs-region$'  -> FINDS it (win 27262979), 251-277ms after launch
  xprop WM_NAME                          -> "qs-region"      (the name IS set)
  xprop _NET_WM_NAME                     -> "qs-region"      (both are set)
  xwininfo -root -tree | grep qs-region  -> 0 matches
  xwininfo -root -children               -> 0 matches

So: my OBSERVATION was real (xwininfo genuinely returns zero against the real overlay, shape and grab included — the Xvfb repro's 'it works' did not reproduce that half). My EXPLANATION was false: GTK sets both WM_NAME and _NET_WM_NAME on this POPUP. The window is discoverable; xwininfo -root -tree just does not surface it.

Root cause of the error: I hit an unexpected zero from ONE tool, reached for a session memory about a DIFFERENT window type (quickshell dock/layer-shell surfaces), generalised it into 'GTK sets no WM_NAME for override-redirect POPUPs', and asserted it as repo knowledge. The repo never said that. I never tried a second tool.

CORRECTED GUIDANCE for anyone asserting 'the overlay is on $DISPLAY':
  USE:      xdotool search --name '^qs-region$'   (works against the real overlay)
  DO NOT:   xwininfo -root -tree | grep qs-region  (returns 0 even when present)
  ALSO SOUND (used by T2): child /proc/<pid>/environ has GDK_BACKEND=x11 + the right DISPLAY, 0 wayland sockets vs N X sockets, and an X11 keystroke reaches its grab.
The T2 substitute proof stands on its own merits; it was just not necessary.

title: preview: remove or absorb SlotManager.SetPath (zero production callers after SwapPath)
description: ly6.3 reviewer finding: SetPath kept for back-compat but grep shows only two pre-existing tests reference it. Fold those tests onto SwapPath+broadcast or delete SetPath.
close_reason:
Fixed: removed the dead SlotManager.SetPath and migrated its two tests onto the production primitives (SwapPath + per-slot Hub broadcast).

SetPath (store + unconditional broadcast) had ZERO production callers — grep confirmed only session_test.go referenced it. Production moved to SwapPath (store, returns old path) + a CONDITIONAL Hub(n).Broadcast in handlePreviewSet (sp011 Task 3 needs the old path to classify the akm transition before deciding whether to broadcast at all).

Migration:
- TestSlotManagerIndependentPaths only asserted CurrentPath → SetPath became SwapPath (no broadcast needed).
- TestSlotManagerIndependentHubs asserts a broadcast reaches slot 2's client and not slot 1's → SetPath became SwapPath + Hub(2).Broadcast(redrawMessage(...)), the exact store+broadcast pair handlePreviewSet uses. So the test now exercises the real primitives instead of a convenience that no longer exists.
- Deleted SetPath; updated the slotFor doc comment that listed it.

Pure refactor, no behavior change — the existing tests are the safety net. Full preview suite green under -race, vet + gofmt clean.

Refs: preview/session.go (SetPath removed, SwapPath/CurrentPath/Hub retained), preview/session_test.go.

title: akm-graph parser.go: stale comments omit archive.md, misdescribe hubFiles
description:
parser.go comments drifted after dotfiles-t9v removed board/product as hubs (only akm.md remains). graph.go was updated; parser.go was not:

1. WalkNotes doc (line ~122): claims it walks 'docs/board.md and docs/product.md' — WRONG. hubFiles = {docs/notes/akm.md} only.
2. Exclusion note (line ~117): lists board.md + product.md as excluded but omits archive.md — the same nav-index class (its [[archive]] footer is dropped by graph.go's non-zettel filter, dotfiles-ov8).
3. nodeTypeFromID doc (line ~175): 'Hub notes (board, product)' — stale, only akm is hub.

Doc-only drift; graph behavior is correct. No nodes/edges lost (sp### live in docs/notes/spec/, archived specs cross-link via bodies). Fix: correct the three comments.
close_reason: Fixed 3 stale parser.go comments (9c07a5b): WalkNotes doc, exclusion note now includes archive.md, nodeTypeFromID hub note. Comment-only, build+tests green.

title: akm-graph: fresh clone needs rotz install + link before 'akm graph' works
description:
On a fresh clone, 'akm graph <verb>' fails until two setup steps run:

1. daemon binary absent — 'rotz install akm-graph' builds ~/.local/bin/akm-graph-d (go-guarded; skips on hosts without go toolchain)
2. wrapper not on PATH — 'rotz link nushell' symlinks ~/.local/bin/akm-graph -> nushell/actions/akm-graph

Both are local machine state, nothing git-tracked. Wrapper already fails loud with actionable message ('run: rotz install akm-graph' / 'rotz link nushell'), so behavior is correct — this is a docs/discoverability gap.

Possible fixes:
- document the two-step setup in akm-graph/dot.yaml header or CLAUDE.md akm section
- have meta-linux depend on akm-graph so 'rotz install meta-linux' covers the daemon build
- note the sequencing (install before link, or link surfaces the install hint)

Verified working end-to-end 2026-07-11: build, go test, daemon start/status, HTTP viewer, /api/graph typed nodes, live-reload, browser open.
close_reason: Added akm-graph to meta-linux depends (94ea8e4). rotz install meta-linux now builds the daemon; nushell dep already covers wrapper link.

title: akm-graph viewer: node max size 10
design: User wants node max size 10 (was 6). Bump MAX_SIZE to 10; nudge SIZE_K so the 3-10 range is used across degrees.
notes: IMPLEMENTED bd-dotfiles-bav.0: MAX_SIZE 6->10, SIZE_K 0.5->0.8; +no-cache header on static assets (server.go noCache wrapper) so viewer updates show on normal refresh. go test green, header verified.
close_reason: Node max size 10; no-cache viewer assets (normal refresh picks up rebuilds).

title: akm-graph viewer: straight links, not curved
design: v7l set curvedLinks:true for aesthetics; user finds the curved edges confusing/strange and wants direct straight lines between nodes. Set curvedLinks:false (cosmos default).
close_reason: curvedLinks -> false; edges are straight direct lines.

title: Discovered: symlinked hub note docs/notes/akm.md excluded from graph
design: WalkNotes (akm-graph/parser.go) skips symlinks via fs.ModeSymlink guard. docs/notes/akm.md is a symlink to claude/akm/akm.md (AKM model-overview hub note), so it never appears as a node. us006 AC1 says every note under docs/notes appears; this hub note is missing. Fix: treat akm.md as a third hub (like board/product) via hubFiles, or resolve non-loop symlinks in WalkNotes. Surfaced by ak8.6 e2e AC walk: 39 real-file notes + 2 hubs present, akm.md the sole omission.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED: fix on branch bd-dotfiles-2ry.0 (commit 7175ebd).

Root cause: WalkNotes (parser.go) skips symlinks; docs/notes/akm.md is a symlink to claude/akm/akm.md, so the AKM overview hub note was dropped and incoming [[akm]] links resolved to a ghost.

Fix:
- parser.go hubFiles: added 'docs/notes/akm.md' (3rd hub alongside board/product). Hub loop uses os.Stat -> follows symlink -> parseNote reads target, id='akm'.
- graph.go hubIDs: added 'akm':true so it types as 'hub'.
- General symlink guard left intact (no loop/escape risk); only this known hub followed.

Evidence:
- TestBuildGraphFromRoot_SymlinkedAkmHub (temp tree, symlinked akm.md + [[akm]] link): asserts non-ghost hub node, alias parsed via symlink. RED before (ghost/type note/no alias) -> GREEN after.
- Full suite go test -count=1 green (golden fixture unaffected — no akm.md there so hub loop skips it). gofmt -l clean, go vet clean.
- E2E real repo (--root /home/jan/.dotfiles): akm node type=hub non-ghost alias='agentic knowledge model' status=stable degree=20.
Left in_progress for review.
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED

Criteria verified:
- akm.md appears as a real node (us006 AC1): e2e --root /home/jan/.dotfiles GET /api/graph -> {id:akm,type:hub,status:stable,alias:'agentic knowledge model',degree:20,ghost:false}. No longer among dangling ghosts.
- Fix minimal + symlink guard intact: parser.go:116 hubFiles adds docs/notes/akm.md; hub loop (parser.go:155-165) resolves via os.Stat (follows symlink). Walk symlink-skip guard (parser.go:137-139) unchanged -> no loop/escape risk. graph.go:35 hubIDs adds akm -> nodeTypeFromID types it hub.
- No regression: full suite green under -race (go test -race -count=1 ./... -> ok akmgraph). Golden fixture unaffected (no akm.md there, hub loop os.Stat skips absent path). gofmt -l clean, go vet clean.
- Test non-tautological (verified by reviewer): reverted hubFiles+hubIDs -> TestBuildGraphFromRoot_SymlinkedAkmHub FAILS with 'akm node is a ghost' + 'type="note", want hub'; restored -> PASS. Test builds a temp tree with symlinked akm.md + an incoming [[akm]] link, asserts non-ghost hub node with frontmatter alias parsed through the symlink.

Classification: akm.md is the AKM model-overview hub per CLAUDE.md / skill schemas ('docs/notes/akm.md carries only the top-level AKM model overview') -> hub typing (alongside board/product) is correct, not a generic note.

Tests: full suite passed under -race, 1 new regression test.
Deviations: none. Design offered 'third hub via hubFiles OR resolve non-loop symlinks in walk'; implementer chose the hub-file path (more minimal, keeps the general symlink guard intact) — within design.

title: Discovered: tighten akm-graph go:embed to viewer assets only
design: server.go //go:embed static bundles smoke.sh, smoke-tooltip.html, graph.json (test fixtures) into the daemon binary. Harmless but unnecessary. Narrow to index.html, app.js, cosmos-bundle.js — or move test assets out of static/. Flagged by ak8.3 review.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED: fix on branch bd-dotfiles-blm.0 (commit ffee2d4).

Fix: server.go //go:embed static -> //go:embed static/index.html static/app.js static/cosmos-bundle.js. Only the 3 viewer files embedded; smoke.sh/smoke-tooltip.html/graph.json stay on disk (served by smoke.sh's own python http.server, unaffected).

Evidence:
- TestEmbedViewerAssetsOnly: GET / /app.js /cosmos-bundle.js -> 200; GET /smoke.sh /smoke-tooltip.html /graph.json -> 404. RED before (test files 200) -> GREEN after.
- Binary check: strings(binary) 'EXPECTED_NODES' (smoke marker) count=0; index.html markers present. Test files no longer bundled.
- smoke.sh regression: still PASS both stages (title OK nodes=10 + tooltip) — serves on-disk static/, embed change doesn't touch it.
- Full suite go test -race green; gofmt -l clean; go vet clean.
- Deviation: chose explicit-embed-list (design option 1) over moving files — minimal, keeps smoke.sh's relative ?fixture=graph.json fetch working (graph.json stays in served static/ on disk).
Left in_progress for review.
close_reason:
AUDITED: APPROVED

Criteria verified:
- Embed narrowed correctly, no viewer asset dropped: server.go:34 //go:embed static/index.html static/app.js static/cosmos-bundle.js. Viewer chain intact — index.html loads ./app.js (index.html:46) which imports ./cosmos-bundle.js (app.js:5); all three embedded. graph.json is only referenced by app.js's ?fixture= debug branch (app.js:153), a smoke-only path served by smoke.sh's own http server, not the daemon.
- Test files excluded from binary (built daemon, strings grep): EXPECTED_NODES=0, smoke-tooltip=0, 'must match nodes array'=0, graph.json aliases visualize-note-relations=0 / akm-graph-cosmos-viz=0. Viewer markers present: cosmos-bundle.js=2, 'akm-graph viewer'=1, index.html width=device-width=2.
- Regression test non-tautological: on a scratch copy widened back to //go:embed static, TestEmbedViewerAssetsOnly FAILS (server_test.go:82 — /smoke.sh, /smoke-tooltip.html, /graph.json return 200, want 404). Passes on the fix.
- No regression: go test -race -count=1 ./... -> ok (1.998s). smoke.sh from worktree PASS both stages (title OK nodes=10 + tooltip TOOLTIP_OK, exit 0) — serves on-disk static/, unaffected by embed change.
- Quality gates: gofmt -l clean, go vet clean, no TODO/stub/skip/panic in changed files.

Deviation: logged and acceptable — implementer chose explicit-embed-list (design option 1) over moving test files out of static/. Correct choice: smoke.sh serves static/ via python http.server and fetches ?fixture=graph.json relative to index.html, so graph.json must stay co-located with index.html; moving it would break smoke.sh. Minor maintenance cost (new viewer assets must be added to the embed list) is the accepted trade-off.

title: tmux-to-workstation: surface remote stderr on tmux-start failure
description: Script swallows ssh stderr with 2>/dev/null in main connect. When remote tmux-start missing/fails, user sees only 'Error: tmux-start failed on <host>' with no cause. Capture stderr via 'complete' and print it.
close_reason: surfaced ssh stderr via complete + do -i, escaped string-interp parens (ee3b87e)

title: Eval infra: seed scripts collide on shared dolt server, grader regex fragile
description: Found running iteration-2 evals in parallel: (1) seed_sandbox.sh uses 'bd init --prefix eval --stealth' which creates same 'eval' DB on shared dolt server — concurrent runs collide w/ PROJECT IDENTITY MISMATCH; fix: inject unique --database flag per eval. (2) bd update --notes is last-writer-wins (not append) — must consolidate keywords into single final write. (3) bd export --output .beads/issues.jsonl needed after each close on shared server. (4) eval-orient-and-halt/grade.py regex 'first batch.*?' breaks on markdown table pipes + lands on wrong section if 'first batch' appears earlier in summary — needs anchoring.
close_reason: Hardened: (1) all 6 seed_sandbox.sh now pass --database "eval-<name>-$$" to bd init — no more shared-dolt collisions on parallel runs. (2) orient + multi-epic graders use _extract_first_batch_section() helper that splits by lines, finds LAST 'first batch' occurrence, captures until Proceed?/Confirm?/Abort?/H2/EOF — handles markdown tables + earlier prose mentions. (3) README documents the 3 shared-dolt-server constraints (unique DB, --notes last-writer-wins, force bd export after close).

title: skill-creator run_loop: recall=0% across all should-trigger queries in this env
description: When running skill-creator's run_loop.py against an infinifu plugin skill, every should-trigger eval query scored rate=0/3 — same fingerprint as 'skill never detected as triggered'. Hypothesis: scripts/run_eval.py writes a proxy command file to project_root/.claude/commands/ and runs 'claude -p' with --include-partial-messages, scanning the stream for content_block_start events that mark skill invocation. Either (a) the stream event format changed in a recent claude-code, (b) plugin-installed skills aren't discoverable to claude -p subprocesses launched from a different cwd, or (c) the project_root resolution (walking up for .claude/) lands at /home/jan/ instead of the dotfiles repo. Symptom: 100% precision, 0% recall, 50% accuracy on every iteration. Loop also crashed iter 2/5 with 'claude -p exited 1' (silent stderr). Repro: cd /home/jan/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/skill-creator/unknown/skills/skill-creator && python -m scripts.run_loop --eval-set <trigger-eval.json> --skill-path <plugin-skill-dir> --model claude-opus-4-7 --max-iterations 5 --verbose. Outputs in optimization-runs/.
notes: Root cause confirmed by stream inspection: claude -p invokes the REAL installed skill (infinifu:plan-scrum-master) because plugin auto-discovery picks it up from ~/.dotfiles/claude/marketplace/plugins/infinifu/skills/<n>/SKILL.md. run_eval.py greps for the proxy command's uuid-suffixed name in tool input — never matches when the real skill wins. Fix: run_optimization.sh wrapper renames real SKILL.md → SKILL.md.disabled-for-eval, stages a frontmatter-stub SKILL.md at /tmp/plan-scrum-master-eval-stub/, points run_loop at the stub, restores on EXIT trap. Verified: with wrapper, recall moves 0/3 → 1/3 on multiple should-trigger queries. Threshold 0.5 still not crossed because slash-command proxies are semantically weaker than real plugin skills for natural-language triggering — that's an upstream skill-creator design constraint. Recommendation: stop here, file upstream issue if pushing further.
close_reason: Investigation complete + partial fix shipped. Wrapper script (run_optimization.sh) removes the real-skill confounder. Residual noise = upstream skill-creator using slash-command proxies instead of real-skill proxies; not in scope to fix here.

title: Run description optimization loop for plan-scrum-master
description: After refactor + evals, use scripts/run_loop.py from skill-creator to optimize trigger description. Generate 20 eval queries (8-10 should-trigger, 8-10 near-miss should-not-trigger), differentiate from plan-supervised, then run train/test split.
notes: Loop infra broken in this env: recall=0/3 across ALL should-trigger queries (every iter). Fingerprint = stream-event detection in skill-creator/scripts/run_eval.py not matching claude -p output. Loop crashed iter 2/5 on improve_description: 'claude -p exited 1' (silent stderr). Adopted iter-1 LLM proposal manually — strict improvement over prior desc: adds verbs (kick off/orchestrate/execute), config-keyword triggers (max_parallel/waves/worker_model/sonnet/opus/haiku), retry mention (sonnet→opus on failure), explicit DO-NOT-USE-FOR negative list. Loop infra bug filed separately.
close_reason: Loop kicked off but infra broken (filed dotfiles-ac9 for the recall=0% bug). LLM-proposed iter-1 description applied manually — strict improvement: more verbs, config-keyword triggers, retry mention, explicit DO-NOT-USE-FOR list. Real measurement will require infra fix first.

title: Split AKM types to per-type micro-skills (persona/feature/im/adr/cat)
description: Each AKM type gets its own write skill linked to akm.md section + worked examples. story-write already exists. zettel-write orchestrator dispatches to these. Build after zettel-write proven.
notes: Built 5 AKM micro-skill writers in parallel via subagents: adr-write (300L), feature-write (300L), implementation-write (403L), persona-write (365L), category-write (261L). Each links to akm.md as canonical schema source rather than duplicating. Updated zettel-write routing table + integration section to reference all 6 typed writers as available (story-write existed; these 5 new). Total ~1629 lines new skill content.

title: wiki-oxide MCP: auto-detect vault root via marker walk
description: Mirror nvim's root_markers pattern. At server startup: if WIKI_ROOT env set use it; else walk cwd up for .moxide.toml/.obsidian; else walk down (bounded depth) from cwd for same markers; else fall back to ~/.dotfiles/wiki. Avoids needing WIKI_ROOT export per project.
close_reason: Implemented marker walk (.moxide.toml, .obsidian) up then bounded-depth down from cwd. WIKI_ROOT env still wins if set. Fallback ~/.dotfiles/wiki. Verified detection across 6 cwds.

title: nvim: add markdown-oxide LSP for wiki vault
description: Add markdown-oxide LSP integration to nvim so editing files under wiki/ gets completion, hover, references, code actions for wikilinks. Binary already at /usr/sbin/markdown-oxide. Scope LSP to vault via root marker to avoid attaching on every random .md file.
close_reason: Added markdown_oxide via nvim-lspconfig opts in nvim/plugins/lang-md.lua. mason=false uses system /usr/sbin/markdown-oxide. root_markers=.moxide.toml/.obsidian scopes attach to vault. Verified headless: client attaches with root=/home/jan/.dotfiles/wiki, settings loaded, vault indexed.

title: quickshell launcher: rescan PATH apps on every show
description: Launcher caches executable list at process start. New installs (e.g. st) don't appear until quickshell restart. Move scan trigger from Process running:true to launcherShow() so list refreshes on each toggle.
close_reason: Patched launcherShow() to retrigger pathScanner. Resets _appBuffer + sets running=true on each show. Quickshell restarted to apply.

title: wm-state: dead code purge
design:
## Goal
Remove helpers no longer reachable after Task 4 lands.

## Files
- Modify: nushell/actions/wm-state

## Targets (verify call graph before removal)
- place-leaf-tree
- restore-container-tree
- pick-live-match
- find-wid-workspace
- find-workspace-by-name
- swallow-for
- body-to-i3-children
- layout-to-i3-json
- build-rebuild-plan
- build-container-plan
- slot-id-for
- term-class-flag
- term-supports-slot
- compute-dominant-layout
- term-exec-line
- is-leaf-key (only if no callers remain)
- node-class (only if no callers remain)

## Approach
1. After Task 4 lands, grep each function name across file. Confirm zero callers.
2. Delete unreferenced helpers.
3. Re-grep, iterate until stable.
4. nu --check nushell/actions/wm-state -> parse OK.
5. wm-state list + wm-state save --current -> smoke pass.

## Effort
1-2h.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Each removed function name -> zero matches in file (outside its own deleted definition).
- [ ] nu --check exits 0.
- [ ] wm-state list yaml shape unchanged from before this task.
- [ ] wm-state save --current produces yaml entry with Task 1 field set.
- [ ] Line count down >=200 lines.

## Edge Cases
- Purge target still referenced (call graph misread) -> leave it, document why, file follow-up.
- node-class may still be used by save path -> keep if so.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Deleting in one big commit without re-running smoke checks between batches.

## Dependencies
Blocks: needs Task 4 merged.
notes: AUDIT PASS (reviewer): all 5 success criteria verified with command evidence. 12 deleted helpers all rg-zero, 5 skipped helpers each have live external callers. nu --ide-check 0 emits only type-inference hints, no diagnostics. wm-state list output diff empty (42 lines identical pre/post). wm-state save --current yaml: terminals carry title+tmux_window_id, non-terminal carries skip_restore:true. Line count 1238->940 (298 drop). Scope clean: zero insertion lines in wm-state, only one code file touched. Discovery dotfiles-gkq filed with correct parent + discovered-from links. Original ~/.cache/sway-state.yaml restored bit-perfect after smoke test.
close_reason: Approved by reviewer: 12 dead helpers purged, 5 skipped helpers verified as still-referenced, parse OK, list/save smoke tests pass, 298-line drop, deletions-only commit, orphan chain filed as dotfiles-gkq.

title: proot: manual claude install (skip hanging install subcommand)
description: Native install.sh hangs on proot Arch at line 151 (claude install subcommand — no systemd/sandbox). Added proot branch in claude/dot.yaml that detects uname -r=PRoot-Distro, downloads binary directly, symlinks to ~/.local/bin/claude, skips install subcommand.
notes: Manual install plan failed verification: raw downloaded binary crashes Bun on proot (panic: Bus error at 0xEF9A0FD). claude install subcommand transforms binary (223MB→247MB) and is required. Real timing ~24s, just silent. Resolution: keep install.sh, add echo before/after for visible progress.
close_reason: Resolved via progress echo in install.sh path; manual install path infeasible due to Bun crash on proot.
